Westcott, of the Westcott and Hort Alexandrian text, lost his voice; another reviser of the Greek New Testament, Samuel Tregelles, lost his voice; and J. B. Phillips, who put out [The New Testament in Modern English]{.class_4729-0} (1959), not only lost his voice but struggled with insanity. You can find all the above facts documented in chapter 32 of Gail Riplinger's work [New Age Bible Versions]{.class_4729-0} (1993). All those men didn't believe God's WORDS, so the Lord struck them dumb like He did Zacharias.

Something funny took place in connection with what Riplinger wrote on that subject. A few years after her book came out, John Ankerberg---a typical apostate Conservative---decided to debunk her work on his national television show. Ankerberg invited in scholars from five new version committees (the [NASV, NIV, ]{.class_4729-0}and [NKJV]{.class_4729-0} were three of the ones represented) and also brought in three [KJV]{.class_4729-0} men in hopes of making them look like fools. One of the [KJV]{.class_4729-0} men was Sam Gipp, a graduate of our school and a fine, Bible-believing evangelist, and another was a Pentecostal preacher named Joseph Chambers out of Charlotte, North Carolina who promoted Riplinger's book on his radio program.

During the taping, God pulled a dirty trick on Ankerberg and those new versions scholars. Ankerberg held up Gail's book and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_37}asked if it were true, as Mrs. Riplinger reported, that a number of scholars on the new translation committees had lost their voices. The [NIV]{.class_4729-0} man said no; the [NKJV]{.class_4729-0} guy (Arthur Farstad) said no. But when Ankerberg got to the representative for the [NASV]{.class_4729-0} committee, a Dr. Don Wilkins, the fellow tried to talk but he couldn't; absolutely nothing came out of his mouth. He tried clearing his throat and still couldn't speak. Finally in a frightened, crackling whisper, he said, "I've\...lost\...my voice!"

That shook up Ankerberg and those other new version guys so much that Ankerberg had the cameraman back up the tape and record over it so it would never be seen on the air. The Pentecostal preacher objected and said, "The cameras should record exactly what happened here." Do you think Ankerberg was going to let something like that get out? Not on your life, brother. Ankerberg's series on Riplinger's book flopped so badly that he aired two programs and then pulled the other six so they never saw the light of day. It kind of reminds one of Judas Iscariot being knocked over by Christ's use of the tetragrammaton in the Garden of Gethsemane and then getting up and betraying Christ after such a display of the Lord's power (see John 18:5--8 and comments on the passage in that Commentary).

Now, Zacharias had gone into the Temple to offer incense on the altar and pray, and he took longer than a priest normally would (vs. 21). When he came out, he couldn't talk, and the people knew something had gone on in the Temple besides offering the incense: ["they perceived that he had seen a vision."]{.class_4730-0}

Zacharias came out of the Temple, and he ["beckoned unto them."]{.class_4730-0} In other words, he's used sign language to communicate. Of course, in his case, he wasn't deaf; he just couldn't speak.

Zacharias finishes up his "tour of duty" at the Temple and returns home; shortly thereafter, Elizabeth is with child as the angel promised. You need to notice the relationship between verse 24 and verse 26. Verse 24: ["Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months"]{.class_4730-0}; verse 26: ["And in the sixth month."]{.class_4730-0} Gabriel doesn't appear to Mary in ["the sixth month"]{.class_4730-0} of the year (August/September on a Jewish calendar); ["the sixth month"]{.class_4730-0} of verse 26 comes after the ["five months"]{.class_4730-0} of verse 24 (cf. also vs. []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_38}36). The Annunciation of Christ's birth takes place in the tenth month of the Jewish calendar (our December/January). That's why we call December "December" even though it's the twelfth month on our calendar; "Dec" means [ten,]{.class_4729-0} not twelve.

It is the Annunciation of Christ that takes place around December 25, not His birth (see our comments on vs. 5). The Catholics have it all screwed up and observe the Annunciation in March to get Christ born on the pagan feast of Saturnalia. Of course, no Christian in the New Testament celebrates Christ's annunciation or His birth. Bible-believing Christians observe the [Resurrection]{.class_4729-0} of Christ 52 times a year, every Sunday (John 20:19, 26; 1 Cor. 16:2). (For further discussion, see our remarks under Rom. 14:5--6 and 1 Cor. 16:1--2 in those Commentaries.)

["Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproach among men"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 25). Even today, Jewish women still consider themselves to be second-class wives if they don't give their husbands any children, especially a son. Rachel had that problem in the Old Testament. Her sister Leah gave birth to four sons in a row, and Rachel had no children at all. She got mad and blamed the whole thing on her husband and said, ["Give me children, or else I die"]{.class_4730-0} (Gen. 30:1). When she finally gave birth to Joseph, she said, ["God hath taken away my reproach"]{.class_4730-0} (Gen. 30:23).

26 And in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,

27 To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name was Mary.

28 And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.

29 And when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be.

30 And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.

31 And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name JESUS.

32 He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest: and the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David:

33 And he shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever; and of his kingdom there shall be no end.

34 Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?

35 And the angel answered and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.

36 And, behold, thy cousin Elisabeth, she hath also conceived a son in her old age: and this is the sixth month with her, who was called barren.

37 For with God nothing shall be impossible.

38 And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.

Notice in verse 26 that Gabriel gave a prophetic revelation to a [woman;]{.class_4729-0} Mohammed's "Gabriel" never did. That's because neither Mohammed nor Allah had a very high regard for women. Here are just a few of the things Islam teaches about women; you can find all the documentation, from official Islamic sources, given in chapter 12 of our work [Will the Real "Allah" Please Stand Up?.]{.class_4729-0}

Men are [superior]{.class_4729-0} to women, and a man is [better]{.class_4729-0} than a woman.

Women are [intellectually inferior]{.class_4729-0} to men.

Women are [toys]{.class_4729-0} to be used by men.

The [Devil]{.class_4729-0} welcomes a woman when she goes outside of her house.

Women are [deficient in religion.]{.class_4729-0}

A woman can't go to Paradise unless her [husband is pleased with her.]{.class_4743}

There are [more women]{.class_4729-0} in Hell than men.

A woman may be [beaten]{.class_4729-0} by her husband as he pleases.

[How's ]{.class_4728}[that]{.class_4729-1}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_40}[ for "women's lib." You reckon Hillary Clinton and Nancy Pelosi would approve of all that?]{.class_4728}

In the Holy Bible, God spoke to all kinds of women [directly]{.class_4729-0} (Gen. 16:7--13, 18:15, 25:22--23; Judg. 13:3--5), and there were many [prophetesses]{.class_4729-0} throughout the Scriptures ([Miriam]{.class_4729-0}---Exod. 15:20; [Deborah]{.class_4729-0}---Judg. 4:4; [Huldah]{.class_4729-0}[---]{.class_4750}2 Kings 22:14; [Isaiah's wife]{.class_4729-0}---Isa. 8:3; [Anna]{.class_4729-0}---Luke 2:36; and [the daughters ]{.class_4729-0}of ["Philip the evangelist"]{.class_4730-0}---Acts 21:8--9). Any one of those women had more true revelation from God than Mohammed got in an entire lifetime. Any Christian woman (from nine to ninety) who knows about the Rapture, the Judgment Seat of Christ, the Tribulation, the Second Advent, and the Millennial reign of Jesus Christ is more of a prophet than Mohammed (Rev. 19:10). Evidently, when Allah picked Mohammed as his "prophet" ("There is no god but Allah, and Mohammed is his prophet"---the [Shahada]{.class_4729-0}), Allah got short-changed and short-sheeted.

Verse 27: ["To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name was Mary." ]{.class_4730-0}And of course, this is the material that is covered in Matthew 1, specifically verses 18, 22--23; and you can see our remarks on the passage in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew.]{.class_4729-0} Here in Luke 1:27, you have the observations of a registered physician on the "marital status" of Mary. As Matthew pointed out in his Gospel (Matt. 1:22--23), this is a fulfillment of Isaiah 7:14, and any attempt by Christ-rejecting Jews or Bible-denying Liberals to make the Hebrew word [almah]{.class_4729-0} in that prophecy mean "young woman" is merely the vain efforts of infidels trying to skirt their appointment with Jesus Christ at the judgment bar of God (Rom. 1:3--4 cf. Acts 17:31).

Note that both Joseph and Mary are ["of the house of David."]{.class_4730-0} Joseph's genealogy is given in Matthew 1, and Mary's is given in Luke 3. We will discuss this more when we come to that chapter.

["And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 28).

This is the source of the famous "Hail Mary" used by the Roman Catholics in the ["vain repetitions"]{.class_4730-0} of the rosary (see Matt. 6:7). []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_41}Naturally, the verse is taken out of context, privately interpreted, and misapplied by the Pope. The following things should be noted about the [Biblical]{.class_4729-0} Mary, not the demoniac "Queen of Heaven" (read Jer. 44) worshipped by the Roman Catholic Church.

1\. The Mary of the Bible was not ["highly favoured"]{.class_4730-0} enough for her own Son ever to call her "Mother" in the Scriptures (see John 2:4, 19:26). In other words, when you hear a Catholic pray, "Hail Mary, Mother of God," you need to remember that God the Son never called her that. Jesus said His ["mother"]{.class_4730-0} was ["whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt. 10:47--50).

2\. Mary wasn't ["highly favoured"]{.class_4730-0} enough for any disciple of Jesus Christ in the New Testament to give her the time of day. When the disciples were gathered for prayer in Acts 1:14, Mary was right there with them, and nobody "hailed Mary" long enough for her to hail him back. She wasn't consulted when it came time to replace Judas (Acts 1:16--26), and no one asked her opinion at the council of Jerusalem in Acts 15. She's a complete blank throughout the rest of the New Testament. The only thing for which the Apostle Paul recognizes her is being the physical vessel to bring Jesus Christ into the world, and then he doesn't even mention her by name (Gal. 4:4). When it comes time to pray, you can skip the "secretary" and march straight into the front office (Heb. 4:15--16).

In World War II, there were Catholics on board ships and in combat who were praying the "Hail Mary" and the "Our Father" while they were cussing out the enemy: "Hail Mary full of grace\...(Get that S.O.B. coming in, Joe!) the Lord is with thee, blessed art thou\...(Watch out for that blankety-blank over there!)." When JFK got his brains blown out in Dallas, Jackie didn't pray "Hail Mary"; she said, "Oh God!" When I got hit by the choo-choo several years back, I didn't say, "Our Father, which art in Heaven." All I could think of was a child's prayer I used as a sermon illustration: "Lord, if you wanna help someone, you've got a good chance now!"

3\. Mary is not ["blessed"]{.class_4730-0} because she was [sinless]{.class_4729-0} or because she is a "co-redemptrix" with Jesus Christ. She is ["blessed"]{.class_4730-0} because she needed a ["Saviour"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 47) and she ["believed"]{.class_4730-0} on Him (vs. 45).

4\. There are people in the Bible blessed higher than Mary. Mary was only ["blessed\...AMONG women,"]{.class_4730-0}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_42 .class-1} but in the Old Testament, [Jael,]{.class_4729-0} the woman who killed Sisera with the tent nail, was ["blessed ABOVE women"]{.class_4730-0} (Judg. 5:24). Why was that? Because Jael was a picture of the Bride of Christ returning with her husband (Eph. 5:22--32 cf. Rev. 19:7--1; 2 Thess. 1:7--8; Judg. 5:20; Song of Sol. 6:10, 13) to bruise the head of the Serpent in the person of the Antichrist (Gen. 3:15; Rom. 16:20). Every saved person has a greater blessing than Mary had in Luke 1 (Eph. 1:3). (See also our comments on vs. 48.)

Verse 29: ["And when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying."]{.class_4730-0} I guess she would be if she knew her Bible. Way back there in Genesis 3, an angel of light showed up in the form of a serpent, and by the time he got through talking to the woman, he had finished off the human race. When an angel showed up in Judges 13 and talked to a woman, she did what Eve didn't do and went and told her husband so he could deal with him. That's a picture of what went on in Luke 1 and Matthew 1. The angel told Mary about the birth of Jesus; then he had to go and tell Joseph too.

Now when it comes to visions of angels, not everyone is as gullible as the Charismatics and the New Agers. One time an angel of light came into the room where Martin Luther was and claimed to be Jesus Christ. The old Kraut yelled, "You're a liar; Jesus Christ is at the right hand of God." Then he threw an ink well at that apparition, and ink splattered all over the wall. You can go over to Germany and see the stain to this day. Of course, if it had been a Charismatic, he would have written a song about it called "I Saw a Man." A New Ager would have written a book about "Angels Among Us" and made a mint off a bunch of suckers.

["And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 30). Why? Because she's [sinless?]{.class_4729-0} No! Because she was [immaculately conceived?]{.class_4729-0} No! Because she's the [mediatrix]{.class_4729-0} to calm the anger of her Son so we can get our prayers answered? No! Because she's the ["Mother of God"]{.class_4729-0}? No! Because she's a [co-redemptrix]{.class_4729-0} with Jesus Christ and the mother of all the saints? No! God favored her by letting her bring the Messiah into the world---AND THAT'S IT. That's the extent to which she was ["highly favoured"]{.class_4730-0} of God---that and no other reason.

Was she a good woman in the moral sense? Yes. Was she a []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_43}concerned, loving mother? Yes. Did she trust her Son for salvation? Yes. But she was not the "Mary" of Roman Catholic mythology. The Mary of the Bible refers you to her Son, not herself: ["His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever HE]{.class_4730-0} \[i.e., Jesus\][ saith unto you, do it"]{.class_4730-0} (John 2:5).

["And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name JESUS"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 31). This is the message the angel brought to Joseph in Matthew 1:18--25, and you can see our comments on that passage in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew.]{.class_4729-0} The emphasis there was on Jesus Christ coming to ["save his people from their sins"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt. 1:21). The emphasis here, though, is Jesus Christ coming to reign on a literal, visible, physical throne on Mt. Zion in Jerusalem. Any man, scholar, or preacher who doesn't believe that (any Amillennialist) is a Bible-rejecting heretic.

Look at it: ["thou shalt conceive in thy womb."]{.class_4730-0} Did she? Yes. ["And bring forth a son."]{.class_4730-0} Did she? Yes. ["And shalt call his name JESUS."]{.class_4730-0} Was that His name? Yes. ["He shall be great"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 32). Well, was He? Yes. ["And shall be called the Son of the Highest" ]{.class_4730-0}(vs. 32). Was He? Yes. ["And the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 32).

And that is where the Catholics and the Amillennialists "lose it." It is obvious to anyone who has read the Gospels that Jesus Christ did [not]{.class_4729-0} get ["the throne of his father David,"]{.class_4730-0} and He hasn't reigned ["over the house of Jacob"]{.class_4730-0}[ ]{.class_4750}one time in the last 2,012 years. So what do all the Amillennialists among the Catholics, Episcopalians, Lutherans, and Presbyterians do? They spiritualize the throne; they spiritualize Mt. Zion (Psa. 2:6); and they spiritualize Jerusalem. It's not really David's throne; it's God's throne. It's not really Mt. Zion down here on earth; it's the heavenly Zion (Psa. 48:1--2). It's not the Jerusalem over in Israel; it's the New Jerusalem upstairs (Heb. 12:22).

"Bless these tithes and offerings to the ongoing of Thy kingdom." "Mine eyes have seen the glory of the coming of the Lord." No you didn't, sister (Julia Ward Howe), you big liar.

Do you know what the slaves sang during the Civil War? "Oh, the masters say, Ha, Ha; and the darkies say, Ho, Ho. For it must be that []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_44}the kingdom am a comin' in the year of Jubilo." That's Abraham Lincoln sending Northern troops down to enforce the Emancipation Proclamation (the Emancipation Proclamation freed no slaves up North---and they were there; it was a political, military move to get the black slaves in the South to turn against the Confederacy); that was how he was "bringing in the kingdom." "The kingdom" that "am a comin'" is the Kingdom of Death and Hell (Isa. 28:15; Rev. 6:8), and it comes straight out of the Pit (Rev. 9:11).

The idea that we're "bringing in the kingdom" is Postmillennialism, and it goes hand in glove with Amillennialism. Most denominations that are Amillennialists theologically are Postmillennialists practically. They believe that the kingdom is coming gradually with the Mass, missionaries, revolutions, food stamps, rat control bills, immunizations, elections, etc. The standard teaching of the Parable of the Leaven in Matthew 13:33 is that the woman is the Church, the leaven is the Gospel, and the meal is the world. The Church puts the Gospel in the world until the whole world is converted to Christ; that's what I was taught by Father Sullivan when I took Catholic convert courses at St. Michael's in downtown Pensacola. That's why no Pope talks about the main theme of the Bible: the Second Coming of Christ. They're trying to bring in the kingdom without the King coming back; they want to be in charge.

The Bible is absolutely clear on the matter. Jesus Christ sits on a literal throne in Jerusalem as the literal King over the entire earth (see Zech. 14). Jesus Christ is not sitting on that throne now, and He won't until He returns in power.

["When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, THEN shall he sit upon the THRONE OF HIS GLORY"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt. 25:31).

That ["throne"]{.class_4730-0} is the throne of a political kingdom, not a spiritual one.

["And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the THRONE OF HIS GLORY, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt, 19:28).

["The throne of his glory"]{.class_4730-0} is [not]{.class_4729-0} His throne [in]{.class_4729-0} Glory (Heaven). []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_45}It is the throne on which the Kings of Israel and Judah sat and reigned.

["Do not abhor us, for thy name's sake, do not disgrace the THRONE OF THY GLORY: remember, break not thy covenant with us"]{.class_4730-0} (Jer. 14:21).

That's not all. Do you know what Solomon's throne in Jerusalem---the one "downstairs," not the one "upstairs"---is called?

["Then Solomon sat on the THRONE OF THE LORD as king instead of David his father"]{.class_4730-0} (1 Chron. 29:23).

The throne of David was ["the throne of the LORD,"]{.class_4730-0} and [that's]{.class_4729-0} the throne Jesus Christ gets when He reigns in the Millennium.

["At that time they shall call Jerusalem the THRONE OF THE LORD; and all the nations shall be gathered unto it, to the name of the LORD, to JERUSALEM]{.class_4730-0} \[see Isa. 2:1--4\][: neither shall they walk any more after the imagination of their evil heart"]{.class_4730-0} (Jer. 3:17).

Back to Luke 1:[ "And he shall reign over the house of Jacob FOR EVER: and of his kingdom there shall be NO END"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 33). That's the prophecy from Isaiah 9:6--7.

"For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder\...Of the increase of his government and peace THERE SHALL BE NO END, upon the THRONE OF DAVID, and upon his kingdom, to order it, and to establish it with judgment and with justice from henceforth even for ever."

The reign of Jesus Christ goes beyond the Millennium out into Eternity. The Jews don't just get all of Jerusalem, all of Palestine, the "East Bank" of the Jordan, and everything from the Nile River to the Euphrates River (the original Abrahamic land grant in Gen. 15:18); they get the [entire earth]{.class_4729-0} (Rom. 4:13 and comments in that Commentary).

That's the "handwriting on the wall" for Obama, the United States, the UN, the "Palestinians," and the "Muslim Brotherhood." The international move right now is to divide Jerusalem and Israel into two capitals and two States. Of course, the Palestinians won't be satisfied until they've driven every Jew into the Mediterranean (see [Israel: A Deadly Piece of Dirt]{.class_4729-0}). Well, the Lord God Almighty has a surprise for them: that land is [His land]{.class_4729-0}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_46 .class-1} (2 Chron. 7:20; Isa. 14:25; Jer. 2:7, 16:18; Ezek. 36:5, 38:16; Joel 1:6, 3:2), and that city is [His city]{.class_4729-0} (Matt. 5:35; 1 Kings 9:3, 11:36; Neh. 1:9). He's given it to [His people,]{.class_4729-0} the Jews (see Zeph. 3:16--20 and our comments on the passage in that Commentary); and He's going to run every "Palestinian," Arab, Moslem, Catholic, and Greek Orthodox out of it (Hab. 3:12--13; Zeph. 2:1--7).

Verse 34: ["Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?"]{.class_4730-0} That's not intellectual knowledge or familial knowledge there; that's what's known as "carnal knowledge." It's knowing someone on the most intimate of levels. The Bible says in Genesis 4:1, ["And Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived."]{.class_4730-0} It's obviously talking about marital relations.

Through Mary, God made a man with no man involved. Darwin teaches humans were made with no God involved; only blind, random chance. The Bible has God making mankind in four different ways: He can make them out of dust with no man and no woman involved (Adam---Gen. 2:7); He can make them out of a man with no woman involved (Eve---Gen. 2:22); He can make them the normal way with both a man and woman involved (Cain and Abel---Gen. 4:1--2); and He can make them out of a woman with no man involved as He did with the Lord Jesus Christ. ["For with God nothing shall be impossible"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 37).

Verse 35: ["And the angel answered and said unto her, the Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God."]{.class_4730-0} Mary is about to give birth to the God-man: 100% God, 100% man; God manifest in the flesh (1 Tim. 3:16; John 1:14). This dual nature of Christ causes no end of trouble for the Catholics. The Catholics (and the Lutherans and Episcopalians who retained the doctrine) call Mary "the Mother of God." After all, wasn't Jesus Christ [God]{.class_4729-0} in the flesh? Didn't Mary give birth to Him? Then Mary's the mother of God, right?

WRONG! The existence of Jesus Christ as the Second Person of the Godhead goes back all the way before Genesis 1:1 (John 1:1--3; []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_47}Mic. 5:2). If Mary gave birth to the One who created the universe, where was she when He made everything? [She wasn't there.]{.class_4729-0} Obviously Mary didn't give birth to His divine nature.

It was Nestorius, Patriarch of Constantinople from A.D. 428--431, who saw the Scriptural problems involved with calling Mary "God's Mother" (after all, if God had a Mother, then who was His [Grandmother]{.class_4729-0}? Wouldn't the doctrine of the Immaculate Conception demand that Mary have an unbroken line of sinless births in her ancestry all the way back to Genesis 1? How did she[ skip ]{.class_4729-0}the Adamic nature with a human father \[see 3:23\]?). Nestorius called her the Mother of [Christ,]{.class_4729-0} the [man]{.class_4729-0} (1 Tim. 2:5). The Catholics condemned him, and anyone else who would not call Mary "the Mother of God," as a heretic at the Council of Ephesus in A.D. 431.

Just by "coincidence" (don't you know), Nestorius was trained at [Antioch of Syria,]{.class_4729-0} where the disciples were first called ["Christians"]{.class_4730-0} (Acts 11:26), and where we get the Biblical text that brought about the Protestant Reformation and the worldwide missionary movement. It was Cyril of [Alexandria]{.class_4729-0} who condemned Nestorius as a heretic at the Council of Ephesus without Nestorius even being there to defend himself. That's [Alexandria, Egypt]{.class_4729-0}---the city where Origen produced the corrupt text of the Bible that brought on the Roman Catholic Dark Ages (Jerome's Latin [Vulgate]{.class_4729-0}); the same Greek text used by the [American Standard Version,]{.class_4729-0} the [Revised Standard Version,]{.class_4729-0} the [New International Version,]{.class_4729-0} the [English Standard Version,]{.class_4729-0} and the Jehovah's Witness [New World Translation. That's]{.class_4729-0} the fruit of calling Mary "the Mother of God." There are "heretics" (Acts 24:14), and then there are [heretics]{.class_4729-0} (Tit. 3:10).

Jesus Christ is the Son of [God]{.class_4729-0} and the Son of [man.]{.class_4729-0} As a man, He got [hungry;]{.class_4729-0} He got thirsty; He got [tired.]{.class_4729-0} Well, God doesn't get hungry or thirsty. He told those Jews: ["If I were hungry, I would not tell thee\...Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?"]{.class_4730-0} (Psa. 50:12--13). No, He won't. Psalm 121:4 says the Lord doesn't ["slumber nor sleep."]{.class_4730-0} Well He did in the back end of that boat on the Sea of Galilee (Mark 4:38).

[When Jesus died, do you remember what He said? ]{.class_4728}He said, ["My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"]{.class_4747} (Matt. 27:46). Did the Father forsake the Son? No, there was no split in the Trinity. But as a [man,]{.class_4746}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_48 .class-1} Jesus suffered the penalty for your sin. That's why He said, ["I thirst"]{.class_4747} (John 19:28), because that's what a man says in Hell (Luke 16:24). Jesus Christ was wholly [man]{.class_4746} as well as wholly God. Mary gave birth to the [human nature,]{.class_4746} not God.

["The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee."]{.class_4730-0} There are two religions that teach that for God to have a Son, He had to have [physical relations]{.class_4729-0} with a woman, and they are two sides of the same coin. They are [Islam]{.class_4729-0} and [Mormonism]{.class_4729-0}. Both were started by men who claimed to have had [revelation from an angel]{.class_4729-0} (Mohammed---Gabriel; Joseph Smith---Moroni); both of the founders were [fornicating polygamists;]{.class_4729-0} both produced a book that claimed to surpass the Bible in authority (Mohammed---[Koran;]{.class_4729-0} Joseph Smith---[Book of Mormon]{.class_4729-0}); and both religions have multiple authorities outside those books (Islam---[Hadith;]{.class_4729-0} Mormonism---[Doctrines and Covenants; Pearl of Great Price]{.class_4729-0}). The [difference]{.class_4729-0} between the two is that Mohammed said God never had a Son (Sura 3:58, 9:30, 17:39, 2:116, 72:3, 6:102, 37:152), and Joseph Smith said that ["Elohim"]{.class_4729-0} came down and fornicated with Mary (cf. John 8:41) and got her "knocked up" (the Mormon "Heavenly Father" was kind of like the pagan Greek god [Zeus]{.class_4729-0}).

The God of the Bible [did]{.class_4729-0} have a Son (John 3:16), but He did not have to have any physical relations with a woman in order to have Him. In the [Holy Scriptures]{.class_4729-0} (not some pulp fiction given by a demon masquerading as an ["angel of light"]{.class_4730-0}), the [Holy Ghost]{.class_4729-0} miraculously prepares a body in the womb of Mary (Heb. 10:5). God no more "knew" Mary than Joseph did at that time (vs. 34).

["Therefore also that HOLY THING which SHALL be born of thee SHALL be called the Son of God."]{.class_4730-0} That's Jesus Christ being referred to as a [neuter]{.class_4729-0} (see our remarks about the Holy Spirit under Rom. 8:16 in that Commentary). At this point, He's an embryo beginning to turn into a fetus. In the womb, He was [not]{.class_4729-0} referred to as a [person.]{.class_4729-0} He is merely the ["seed"]{.class_4730-0} of God.

Genesis 3:15, ["And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed, and HER SEED]{.class_4730-0} \[a reference to the virgin birth of Christ\][; IT]{.class_4730-0} \[the seed of the woman is a [thing]{.class_4743}\] [shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel."]{.class_4730-0}

As long as Christ's body was being formed in the womb, He was not referred to as a person. It was not until He was born that He was ["called the Son of God"]{.class_4730-0}; hence, the future tense: ["shall be."]{.class_4730-0}

I can already hear the cries of: "Ruckman believes in abortion." Shut your mouth; you don't know about what you're talking. You're taking something that's obviously WRONG (an act can be a [sin]{.class_4729-0} without being [murder]{.class_4729-0}) and twisting the rest of the Bible to make it fit what you believe to be so. You are commanded to ["speak as the oracles of God"]{.class_4730-0} (1 Pet. 4:11), and in ["the oracles of God"]{.class_4730-0} (see Rom. 3:2--4), a body does not have a soul until it [breaths]{.class_4729-0} (Gen. 2:7; Ezek. 37:1--10). Under the Old Testament Law, if a man caused a miscarriage with no harm to the mother, he was [not]{.class_4729-0} counted as a murderer of that unborn child (Exod. 21:22). Does that mean it's all right for a teenage girl to get an abortion to cover up the consequences of her fornication? Of course not!

If these "pro-lifers" were going to be consistent with the position they take on abortion being murder, they would have to execute the mother who gets the abortion as well as the doctor who performs the operation. Why? Because ["whoso sheddeth man's blood, by man shall his blood be shed"]{.class_4730-0} (Gen. 9:6). That's true [before]{.class_4729-0} the Law, [under]{.class_4729-0} the Law (Num. 35:31), and [after]{.class_4729-0} the Law (Acts 25:11; Rom. 1:29--32, 13:4). These people who kill abortion doctors are just being consistent with their beliefs.

Listen, people, if the pro-lifers are right, there's no use singing "God Bless America" or praying for revival for America as a nation. You know: ["If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land"]{.class_4730-0} (2 Chron. 7:14---which has nothing to do with any Gentile nation or "Christian nation").

The same God who told you that, told you: ["So ye shall not pollute the land wherein ye are: for blood it defileth the land: and the land CANNOT BE CLEANSED of the blood that is shed therein, but by the BLOOD OF HIM THAT SHED IT"]{.class_4730-0} (Num. 35:33).

There have been nearly [forty million]{.class_4729-0} babies aborted since Roe []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_50}vs. Wade was "passed" by the Supreme Court in 1972 (that's the judicial branch usurping the job of the legislature). You're not going to get the Lord to heal this land without the slaughter of something like over a tenth of the population, no matter how much repenting you do or how many laws against abortion are passed.

Verse 36: ["And, behold, thy COUSIN Elisabeth."]{.class_4730-0} You will want to mark that word ["cousin."]{.class_4730-0} The Catholics will tell you that the brothers and sisters of Jesus Christ, as they are given in Mark 6:3, are not [brothers]{.class_4729-0} and [sisters]{.class_4729-0} at all; they are [cousins.]{.class_4729-0} They do that because they believe in the "perpetual virginity" of Mary.

Well, if you believe in the perpetual virginity of Mary, then you have to believe in the [perpetual virginity of Joseph.]{.class_4729-0} That's something to think about, wouldn't you say? If Mary was a perpetual virgin all her life, that means she [defrauded her husband]{.class_4729-0} and deprived him of the power due him in a marriage (1 Cor. 7:4--5). That is, she [sinned]{.class_4729-0} as a wife.

Now the Scriptures are very clear on these matters. When the Holy Spirit wanted to say ["cousin,"]{.class_4730-0} He said ["cousin"]{.class_4730-0}; and when He wanted to say ["brothers"]{.class_4729-0} and ["sisters,"]{.class_4730-0} He said what He meant, and He meant what He said. A brother is not a cousin, and a cousin is not a brother. The words are not the same in English, and they are not the same in Greek (cousin---[suggenas]{.class_4729-0}; brother---[adelphos]{.class_4729-0}).

Moreover, the Holy Spirit left no doubt whatsoever as to what He meant by ["brothers"]{.class_4729-0}[ ]{.class_4750}and ["sisters"]{.class_4730-0} in Mark 6:3. Look at Psalm 69:8 and note the context in verse 9 is the First Advent as given in John 2:17.

"I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my MOTHER'S CHILDREN."

The Holy Spirit is not talking about the Jews being Christ's ["brethren"]{.class_4730-0} nationally in that verse; He's talking about the [other children of Mary]{.class_4729-0}---Mark 6:3. So what you have in the Kennedy family, four Supreme Court justices, the former heads of the FBI, HEW, State Department, and CIA are a bunch of [Bible-rejecting fools.]{.class_4729-0} That goes for all the Popes too---John XXIII, Paul VI, John Paul II, and the one in there right now, Benedict XVI (John Ratzinger); they're all a bunch of bald-faced, [Bible-rejecting liars.]{.class_4729-0}

["For with God nothing shall be impossible"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 37). That's []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_51}one of the most misused, misapplied verses in the Bible. The Catholics use it to prove a Priest can take a starchy cookie and a jug of hootch and turn them into the literal body and blood of Jesus Christ. The Charismatics use it to prove all their shenanigans. The atheists, agnostics, and skeptics turn the verse on its ear and use it to prove God isn't omnipotent and therefore doesn't exist (you know the old chestnut: "Can God make a rock too big for God to lift?" Answer: yes, He can; He simply crushes it to powder and blows it away---Matt. 21:44; Dan. 2:34--35, 44--45).

There [are]{.class_4729-0} some things impossible with God. Why? Because it is not part of His [nature]{.class_4729-0}: ["God is not a man, that he should lie"]{.class_4730-0} (Num. 23:19). That is, it is impossible for God to go against His nature. It is impossible for God to do wrong to save you; it's contrary to His nature. He's not going to turn wine and bread into His Son's literal body and blood because it's impossible for Him to violate His word. He said, ["The flesh profiteth nothing ]{.class_4730-0}\[the context is eating Christ's flesh and drinking His blood\][: the WORDS that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life"]{.class_4730-0} (John 6:63). God is not going to violate the rules He laid down for the sign gifts to the Jews (1 Cor. 14). That's the business.

I'll tell you something else. It is impossible for God to send a sinner to Hell once that sinner has [trusted the blood atonement of the Lord Jesus Christ]{.class_4729-0} (Rom. 8:38--39; Eph. 4:30; Phil. 1:6).

When the Holy Spirit says ["with God nothing shall be impossible,"]{.class_4730-0} He tells you what He means right in the same book.

["The things which are impossible WITH MEN are possible with God"]{.class_4730-0} (Luke 18:27).

In other words, God can do what man can't as long as it is in accordance with His nature, will, and purpose. Scripture with Scripture, people (1 Cor. 2:13); you can't just go charging through that Book making it say what you want it to say (2 Pet. 1:20).

Notice in verse 38 that Mary does not suffer the same fate as Zacharias because she accepted what the angel told her. Zacharias had basically asked for a sign of the proof of Gabriel's words back in verse 18; Mary didn't ask for any sign that what the angel said was so because [she was the sign]{.class_4743} (Isa. 7:14).

39 And Mary arose in those days, and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Juda;

40 And entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.

41 And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost:

42 And she spake out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.

43 And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

44 For, lo, as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.

45 And blessed is she that believed: for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.

Again, the pro-lifers use verse 41 to prove a baby is a living soul in the womb. But remember, John was the [exception to the rule]{.class_4729-0} because he was the only man in the entire Bible ["filled with the Holy Ghost\...from his mother's womb"]{.class_4730-0} (see vs. 15 and our remarks). The reaction here is the reaction of the Holy Spirit in Elizabeth's womb; the baby isn't hearing anything.

Verse 42 is another verse used in the rosary. You know, "Hail Mary, full of grapes; blessed be the fruit of the loom." If you consider that to be sacrilegious, it's because you've been spending too much time with the wrong company. You would get rid of the second commandment (["Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image,"]{.class_4730-0} Exod. 20:4), pray to a demon (Jer. 44), run through a pagan mantra (Matt. 6:7), and [degrade your Saviour]{.class_4729-0} just so you wouldn't offend momma and daddy, or granddaddy and grandma (Luke 14:26). Martin Luther used to say, ["Cursed]{.class_4729-0} be that love for whose sake the Bible must be put to the stake." Some of you would get rid of that Bible before you would give up the religion of momma and daddy, and some of you will do that all the way to the Lake of Fire.

Before I got saved, I was taking Catholic convert courses from Father Sullivan at St. Michael's on Palafox Street in downtown Pensacola. In fact, I was only about a week from being confirmed when Hugh Pyle led me to Christ. My mother-in-law back on the farm in Hale County, Alabama had sent me a [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4729-0} which I took to my last class on Saturday night; I was to be confirmed the next morning.

For some reason, I took out that [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4729-0} and said to Father Sullivan, "There's only one thing that really bothers me. I can't find anything in this Bible about worshipping Mary." I had been reading that Book about four or five days after I had been saved, and I couldn't have told you where Matthew was without looking it up in the Table of Contents. "What is all of this stuff about worshipping Mary?" I asked.

The "Father" said, "Do you remember when Christ was on the cross in John 19?"

"Yes," I replied, but I wouldn't have been able just to open up to it.

"Do you remember when He said to John, ['Behold thy mother']{.class_4730-0} and to Mary ["behold thy son']{.class_4730-0}?" the Priest asked.

"Yes," I said. I vaguely remembered something like that from my childhood in the Episcopalian Church.

Continuing on, he said, "Well, doesn't it stand to reason that if the Son of God is dying, His last few words are going to have more significance to them than just 'take that woman home'?"

"Yeah, I guess that makes sense," I said.

He said, "What He's actually saying there is 'Mary, anyone who follows Me is your son'; if you are a Christian, you have to follow Mary because you're one of her sons."

"Oh," I said, "but what is this bit about worshipping Mary?"

"Oh, we don't worship her," he replied, "we just honor her [like you do your own mother."]{.class_4729-0}

That didn't strike a chord with me at all. My mother died a chronic alcoholic. I learned to play five card draw and seven card stud watching my mother; I learned to sing dirty songs listening to my mother. "Honor Mary like I honored my own mother"? What was that?

About that time the Holy Spirit said to me, "Open that Book and look down." It just "happened" to fall open to the very passage about which that Priest was talking---John 19. I looked down and read verse 30 out loud. "Why, here He says, ['It is finished.']{.class_4730-0} If He's dying and His last few words have more significance than just 'My life is over,' then He must mean that SALVATION IS COMPLETED!"

That Priest snuffed out his cigar, shifted in his chair, and said, "PUT THAT BOOK AWAY, Ruckman; you've been READING THAT TOO MUCH. It's BAD for you to read the Bible if you don't understand it."

But the Holy Spirit wasn't through yet. He had me go back and forth with that Priest through 2 Timothy 3:16--17; John 21:25; and Revelation 22:18--19. As far as I could tell, I was just randomly thumbing through my Bible; I couldn't have taken you to those passages consciously if I had tried. Old Father Sullivan got so mad at the Book that he shouted, "PUT THAT BOOK AWAY! What you need to read is some GOOD BOOKS."

That's your Catholic Church. What he meant was put down "THE GOOD BOOK" and substitute for it some junk by some man that contradicted what God said in HIS BOOK. He brought out five volumes on [How to Study the Scriptures]{.class_4729-0} by Thomas Aquinas [which I had already read.]{.class_4729-0}

That was the beginning of the end of my contact with the Catholic Church. I said to myself, "Something's wrong here, man." For the first time in my life I had a Book that spoke to me personally. It started to clean me up; the more I read it the more stuff I was giving up. It took away my beer and whiskey, and I was just about to dump the cigars. For the first time in my life I had a Book that was doing me some good, and this fellow was telling me to put it down and read some good books. [I had read a book a day since I was ten years old.]{.class_4729-0}

To make a long story short (you can read the full account in chapter 11 of [The Full Cup]{.class_4739}), that was as far with Catholicism as I got. I left there that night and went to a Youth for Christ meeting at the old Brent Baptist Church where I confessed Christ as my Saviour. It has been the Book and I ever since. But I had to make a choice: it was either going to be what the Catholic Church said in "good books" or what God said in [His Book.]{.class_4739} I chose [God's Book.]{.class_4739}

["And whence is this to me, that the Mother of God should come to me?"]{.class_4729-0} Oops! I read that verse like a Roman Catholic Priest. ["And whence is this to me, that the mother of MY LORD should come to me?"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 43). Elizabeth uses a personal appellation that can apply to either Christ's humanity or Deity. What you have to do is interpret the verse with what the rest of the Scriptures say about the matter (see our comments on vs. 35). One of the basic rules of Bible "hermeneutics" (Biblical interpretation) is never interpret a clear passage (like, say, John 2:4 and Matt. 12:49--50) by an unclear passage (vs. 43); [always interpret an unclear passage by a clear passage.]{.class_4729-0}

We have already commented on verse 45 under verse 28 (which see).

46 And Mary said, My soul doth magnify the Lord,

47 And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.

48 For he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for, behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.

49 For he that is mighty hath done to me great things; and holy is his name.

50 And his mercy is on them that fear him from generation to generation.

51 He hath shewed strength with his arm; he hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.

52 He hath put down the mighty from their seats, and exalted them of low degree.

53 He hath filled the hungry with good things; and the rich he hath sent empty away.

54 He hath holpen his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy;

55 As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever.

This "song" of Mary's is known as the "Magnificat," taken from the word ["magnify"]{.class_4730-0} in verse 46. Its counterpart in the Old Testament is found in 1 Samuel 2:1--10, where Hannah is rejoicing []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_56}over the birth of Samuel (see our remarks on Hannah as a type of Mary under vs. 7). Both Hannah and Mary are speaking of the [Second Advent]{.class_4729-0} of Jesus Christ in their "songs" (see our remarks about double application under vs. 17).

Notice in verse 47 that Mary is a [sinner]{.class_4729-0} in need of a ["Saviour."]{.class_4730-0} The Roman Catholic doctrine of the Immaculate Conception is NOT the Bible doctrine of the Virgin Birth of Jesus Christ. The "Immaculate Conception" teaches that for Mary to bear a sinless Saviour, [she had to be sinless herself.]{.class_4729-0} This perverted logic flies in the face of what the Holy Spirit revealed in the Scriptures.

In Luke 2:22--24, Mary offers a sacrifice for ["her purification."]{.class_4730-0} Why was that? Because [the woman herself is unclean in sin]{.class_4729-0} (Job 25:4; Psa. 51:5). Although Jesus Christ Himself was ["holy]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 35), the sacrifice was made ["under the law"]{.class_4730-0} by someone who needed to be [redeemed]{.class_4729-0} ["under the law"]{.class_4730-0} (Gal. 4:4--5).

The "Mary" of Roman Catholic fiction is a pagan demon going all the way back to Genesis 10--11 (for the documented evidence, get the work by Alexander Hislop, [The Two Babylons]{.class_4729-0}). The Mary of the Bible is a young Jewish woman whom God chose to bring His Son into the world even though she was a sinner in need of a Saviour. She fulfilled that task, and after that, she came together with her husband and bore four half-brothers and at least two half-sisters to Jesus (Mark 6:3). Her Son never called her "mother," not even as a child, according to the written record given to us by the Holy Ghost (see Luke 2:49). As a man, her Son distanced Himself from her on at least two occasions (John 2:4; Matt. 12:46--50), and at His death, He severed any earthly relationship He had with her (John 19:26--27).

After her Son returned to His Father, the other disciples gave her no special place in the Church, and none of them prayed to her (Acts 1:14). If she lived past Pentecost (Acts 2:1), the same Holy Ghost who overshadowed her in Luke 1:35 and prepared the body of Jesus Christ in her womb (Heb. 10:5), baptized her into the spiritual body of her Son (Acts 1:5 cf. 1 Cor. 12:13) where [He]{.class_4729-0} is ["the head"]{.class_4730-0} (Col. 1:18) and provides access in prayer to God (Heb. 3:14--16). As a member of the Body of Christ, the only "mediating" she can do for you as a sinner is to point you to the ["one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus"]{.class_4730-0} (1 Tim. 2:5 cf. John 2:5).

If you are saved, your ["mother"]{.class_4730-0}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_57 .class-1} is not Mary or the Roman Catholic faith; it is the New Jerusalem in Heaven that will some day come down from God (Gal. 4:26; Heb. 12:22--24; Rev. 21:2). Mary has [absolutely nothing]{.class_4729-0} to do with your salvation (John 14:6; Acts 4:12). You no more need to "venerate" her than you do Peter, Joseph, or "blessed John the Baptist" because every born-again believer in the Body of Christ is a priest with God (1 Pet. 2:5, 9). [That's]{.class_4729-0} the Mary of the Bible.

Verse 48: ["from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed."]{.class_4730-0} Mary's saying that because of what Gabriel told her in verse 28 and what Elizabeth told her in verse 42. You can understand why she said what she said, but when someone actually [tried it,]{.class_4729-0} look at what Mary's Son said to that person.

["And it came to pass, as he ]{.class_4730-0}\[i.e., Jesus\][ spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked"]{.class_4730-0} (Luke 11:27).

There's somebody calling Mary ["blessed"]{.class_4730-0} just like Mary said people would. What does Jesus have to say about that? Why, He [corrects]{.class_4729-0} that woman and, in so doing, His own mother.

["But he said, Yea rather ]{.class_4730-0}\[He's saying, "That's true, BUT\...\][, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it"]{.class_4730-0} (Luke 11:28). He told that woman exactly what He told those folks in Matthew 12:46--50 who told Him His mother wanted to see Him. If you have trusted Jesus Christ as your personal Saviour (John 6:29), [you are more blessed than Mary.]{.class_4729-0}

Now, what you have in verses 51--54 is prophecy given in the [past tense]{.class_4729-0} (["hath shewed," "hath scattered," "hath filled," "hath sent," "hath holpen"]{.class_4730-0}), exactly as you find it in Isaiah 53. Isaiah 53 is a prophecy about the death, burial, and resurrection of Jesus Christ for sinners that would take place over 700 years after Isaiah wrote; but it is all given in the [past tense]{.class_4729-0} to mess up that Christ-rejecting Jew (e.g., ["Surely he HATH borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows,"]{.class_4730-0} Isa. 53:4. No, He didn't; He hadn't even been born yet).

Look at verse 52 here in Luke 1: ["He hath put down the mighty from their seats."]{.class_4730-0} No, He didn't. Not only had he not []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_58}been born yet, Herod, Pilate, and Caesar were right there through His whole ministry.

Verse 53: ["The rich he hath sent empty away."]{.class_4730-0} Are you kidding me, man? Are you talking about Ted Turner or Bill Gates? How about Steve Forbes or Warren Buffet?

You've got a perfect contradiction unless the Holy Spirit is talking through Mary about the future as if it's already happened. God does that for two reasons. One is His very nature. God lives outside of time (Isa. 57:15); He's sitting out in Eternity beyond the year 3000 and writing things backwards into His creation. That's why a lot of that future stuff in the Bible is in the past tense, because to God, it's history.

The other reason is to mess up a bunch of puffed-up, conceited, proud, Bible-rejecting scholars in the World Council of Churches, the National Council of Churches, the Southern Baptist Convention, the Presbyterians, the Methodists, etc., and the Christian Colleges, Universities, and Seminaries, who don't believe the Book, won't study the Book, and won't rightly divide the Book. They'll spiritualize passages like this one to make you believe everything's in the past. God says in Hosea 6:5, ["I have slain them by the words of my mouth."]{.class_4730-0} Why was that? Because they won't believe what God says, where He says it, as He says it. They've got to change it, correct it, cut it up, or spiritualize it away.

Now, verses 46--49 are personal to Mary: she's rejoicing over what the Lord's doing through her. Verse 50 is good for anybody, anytime, anywhere, in either Testament. Back in the very first book of the Bible ever written, Job says, ["the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom"]{.class_4730-0} (Job 28:28). Solomon says, ["Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man"]{.class_4730-0} (Eccl. 12:13). Paul says the believer in Christ is to be ["perfecting holiness in the fear of God"]{.class_4730-0} (2 Cor. 7:1). And the last "gospel" preached on this earth before Jesus Christ returns in power is: ["Fear God, and give glory to him"]{.class_4730-0} (Rev. 14:7).

But when you get down to verses 51--55, the whole thing is Second Advent. ["He hath shewed strength with his arm"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 51): a reference to Jesus Christ as God's ["right hand"]{.class_4730-0} coming back to fight the United Nations in Palestine (Psa. 118:15, 98:1). []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_59 .class-1}["He hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts."]{.class_4730-0} Second Advent: Psalm 18:14, 68:1, 14, 89:10, 92:9; Isaiah 33:3.

["He hath put down the mighty from their seats"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 52). Isaiah wrote, ["And it shall come to pass in that day]{.class_4730-0} \[Second Advent\][, that the LORD shall punish the host of the high ones that are on high, and the kings of the earth upon the earth"]{.class_4730-0} (Isa. 24:21). The famous Messianic prophecy in Psalm 110 says Christ makes His enemies His footstool (Psa. 110:1); they have to get down on their hands and knees and ["lick the dust"]{.class_4730-0} off His feet (Psa. 72:9).

["And exalted them of low degree."]{.class_4730-0} In the Beatitudes, Christ said, ["Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt. 5:5). Well, not now, no; but at the Second Advent, yes (Zeph. 2:3).

["He hath filled the hungry with good things, and the rich he hath sent away empty"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 53). That's because in the Tribulation, the only people who have the money to buy the food are those who take the Mark of the Beast (Rev. 13:17). James 5:1--6 is a blanket condemnation of the rich defrauding the poor. Well, not all the rich are bad. Job was rich, and he was more righteous than any man you know (Job 1:1, 31:1--40). Abraham was rich. R. G. LeTourneau was rich, and he ended up giving 90% of his income to the Lord and keeping only 10%. On the other hand, not all poor people are good. There are plenty of poor drunkards, wife-beaters, and dead-beat dads on welfare and food stamps. But notice the context of James 5: verse 7---["the coming of the Lord"]{.class_4730-0}; verse 8---["the coming of the Lord."]{.class_4730-0} When Jesus Christ comes back, He takes all the rich people who have worshipped the Antichrist and puts them down, and exalts all the people who refused to take the mark and starved to death.

["He hath holpen]{.class_4730-0} \[an archaic way of saying "helped"\][ his servant Israel\...As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever"]{.class_4730-0} (vss. 54--55). At Christ's return, that Jew is completely restored to the land (see Zeph. 3:18--20 and our comments on the passage in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets,]{.class_4729-0} Vol. II) and gets the full Abrahamic land grant []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_60}promised in Genesis 15:18 (see Psa. 105:8--11).

56 And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her own house.

57 Now Elisabeth's full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.

58 And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy upon her; and they rejoiced with her.

59 And it came to pass, that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child; and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

60 And his mother answered and said, Not so; but he shall be called John.

61 And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.

62 And they made signs to his father, how he would have him called.

63 And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is John. And they marvelled all.

64 And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue loosed, and he spake, and praised God.

65 And fear came on all that dwelt round about them: and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of Judaea.

66 And all they that heard them laid them up in their hearts, saying, What manner of child shall this be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.

Mary came to visit Elizabeth in Elizabeth's sixth month of pregnancy (vs. 36), and she stays ["about three months"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 56) and goes back to Nazareth just before Elizabeth gives birth. By the time Mary sees her "betrothed" again, she is "showing." So the events of Matthew 1:18--24 probably took place after Luke 1:56.

Notice again the word ["cousins"]{.class_4730-0} in verse 58 (see our comments on vs. 36). The Holy Spirit has no problem using the word ["cousins"]{.class_4730-0} when He [means cousins.]{.class_4729-0}

Now for nine months, Zacharias has been unable to speak. When it comes time to name the child, all the relations and relatives want to call him Zacharias "Junior." Elizabeth insists on the name the angel told her husband back in the Temple. That was when Gabriel struck Zacharias dumb for not believing what God said. The moment that Zacharias confirms the child's name, he's able to talk again. That's what Gabriel promised back in verse 20.

["And fear came on all that dwelt round about them"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 65). When the Lord came down on Mt. Sinai and spoke to the children of Israel, those Jews got so scared they told Moses, "You speak to God for us, but don't let God speak to us directly, or we'll die." Then Moses made a peculiar statement.

["FEAR NOT: ]{.class_4750}[for God is come to prove you, and that HIS FEAR MAY BE BEFORE YOUR FACES, that ye sin not"]{.class_4730-0} (Exod. 20:20).

Moses is saying, "Don't be afraid; God is just doing that so you'll be afraid." But those are two different fears there. There's the paralyzing fear those Jews had where they didn't want God speaking to them; that's the kind of fear God didn't want them to have (see 2 Tim. 1:7). Then there is the fear that keeps you in line and doing right (["that ye sin not"]{.class_4730-0}). That's the kind of the fear of God the Lord wanted them to have. ["The fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding"]{.class_4730-0} (Job 28:28).

The news spreads fast (vs. 65), and everybody's wondering: ["What manner of child shall this be!"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 66). He's going to be God's ["messenger"]{.class_4730-0}; that's what he's going to be.

There are three titles Mohammed tries to usurp for himself from the Bible. You will see Moslems call Mohammed Allah's "Prophet." That's a reference to Jesus Christ in Deuteronomy 18:18 and Acts 3:22--26. Then they call him the "Apostle" of Allah; that's Mohammed stealing another title from the Lord Jesus Christ (Heb. 3:1). But the most prevalent title used by the Moslems today for Mohammed is Allah's "Messenger." That was stolen from Malachi 3:1.

["Behold, I will send MY MESSENGER, and he shall prepare the way before me."]{.class_4730-0} That's the Lord talking about John the Baptist (Mark 1:2). Mohammed doesn't win, place, or show. As a []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_62}"Prophet," he's a perfect blank. God never sent Mohammed to do "nuthin'" (the word [apostle]{.class_4729-0} means "sent one"---Matt. 10:5). And the only "message" Mohammed ever delivered was from a 600-winged angel; Allah never said anything to him directly.

In the Holy Bible, John the Baptist was ["more than a prophet"]{.class_4730-0} (Matt. 11:9). He was the herald of Jehovah God in the flesh (Matt. 11:10). That old, epileptic, demon-possessed, fornicating, murderous camel jockey couldn't touch the hem of John's garment (Matt. 11:11).

67 And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,

68 Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,

69 And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;

70 As he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets, which have been since the world began:

71 That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us;

72 To perform the mercy promised to our fathers, and to remember his holy covenant;

73 The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,

74 That he would grant unto us, that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemies might serve him without fear,

75 In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.

76 And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;

77 To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,

78 Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,

79 To give light to them that sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.

80 And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.

Verses 67--79 are what Zacharias says when the Lord looses his tongue (vs. 64). What he's going to give you is [prophecy.]{.class_4729-0} That should warn you right away that what he's about to say goes far beyond anything in Matthew, Mark, Luke, or John. In fact, the only verses in the entire prophecy that took place at the First Advent are verses 76--77. Everything else is Second Advent.

You see, the problem is that when you read words like ["redeemed"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 68), ["salvation"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 69), and ["saved"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 71), you are reading them as a New Testament Christian in the Body of Christ with the Pauline Epistles in front of you. There are no "Christians" in Luke 1; "Christians" don't show up until Acts 11:26. There is no spiritual Body of Christ in Luke 1 into which Jews and Gentiles are put upon salvation; that Body doesn't exist until the cross (Eph. 2:16). In fact, there is no "New Testament" until Christ institutes it at the Last Supper (Matt. 26:28), and it's not in force until the death of Christ (Heb. 9:16--17). The Gospels are a part of the New Testament because they give the historical material associated with the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ, which is the message of the New Testament (1 Cor. 15:1--4). But the only "Bible" Elizabeth, Mary, and Zacharias had was the Old Testament: Genesis--Malachi; they had no New Testament books.

In the Old Testament, the redemption and salvation of the Jews is a [physical]{.class_4729-0} one from their political enemies. Look at Exodus 15.

["The LORD is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation"]{.class_4730-0} (Exod. 15:2). ["Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people which thou hast redeemed"]{.class_4730-0} (Exod. 15:13). What's that? Spiritual salvation and redemption from sin? No! It's God delivering those Jews from Pharaoh by leading them through the Red Sea and drowning the Egyptians---read the chapter. True, it's a [picture]{.class_4729-0} of your salvation (1 Cor. 10:1--4), but that wasn't revealed till 1,500 years later.

Want to see it again in the prophetic setting of Luke 1:67--79? Look at Psalm 25:22.

["Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his TROUBLES."]{.class_4730-0} What ["troubles"]{.class_4730-0}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_64 .class-1} are those? Those are the troubles those Jews are going through during ["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4730-0} (Jer. 30:7): it's Daniel's Seventieth Week. At Christ's return, He redeems those Jews not only from their national sins (Psa. 25:7 cf. Rom. 11:26--27), but from their ["enemies"]{.class_4730-0} (Psa. 25:2).

The distinction between physical salvation and spiritual salvation is clearly brought out in Zacharias' prophecy.

Verse 71: ["That we should be saved from OUR ENEMIES."]{.class_4730-0} Second Advent. Those enemies are defined in Zechariah 14:2; they are all 191 Gentile nations in the UN. They are not sin nor the world, the flesh, and the Devil. No Jew in verse 71 is saved from Hell.

Verse 77: ["To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the REMISSIONS OF SINS."]{.class_4730-0} There's spiritual salvation from sin, but that's John's ministry at the First Advent (Luke 3:3). That has nothing to do with verses 68, 69, 70, 71, 72, 73, 74, or 75. There is definite [double application]{.class_4729-0} in verses 78--79, but the cross-reference in Isaiah 9:1--2 is doctrinally the Second Advent (Isa. 9:4--5).

The ["horn of salvation"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 69) is a [conquering King,]{.class_4729-0} not a [crucified Saviour.]{.class_4729-0} In Daniel 7:7--8, those ["horns"]{.class_4730-0} on that last beast are defined as Kings (Rev. 17:12). That ["horn"]{.class_4730-0} in verse 69 is said ["to bud"]{.class_4730-0} (Psa. 132:17; Ezek. 29:21), exactly as Aaron's rod budded in Numbers 17:8. So He's a Priest, but at the same time He's a King (Psa. 89:17--18) who wears a crown and conquers His enemies (Psa. 132:18).

["That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all them that hate us"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 71). The ["us"]{.class_4730-0} in that verse is literal, physical [Jews]{.class_4729-0}; it's not "spiritual Jews" or ["the Israel of God"]{.class_4730-0} (Gal. 6:16). They're descendants of the ["fathers"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 72)---Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Jesus Christ comes back to deliver the nation of Israel from Moslems and other anti-Semites (["them that hate us"]{.class_4730-0}).

The ["holy covenant"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 72) which was made by ["the oath"]{.class_4730-0} sworn to Abraham (vs. 73) is not the Mosaic covenant or the new covenant of Jeremiah 31:31--34. The only ["covenant"]{.class_4730-0} that God ever swore to Abraham is the ["covenant"]{.class_4730-0}[ ]{.class_4750}of Genesis 15:18 giving him everything from the Nile to the Euphrates.

"In the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates."

That covenant was not given to the descendants of Abraham in general; Ishmael and the Arab Moslems have no part in it whatsoever (Gen. 17:19--21). Moreover, it is not a covenant that is to be spiritualized through Isaac (cf. Gal. 4:28). It is a covenant confirmed with [Jacob]{.class_4729-0} and to the [nation of Israel]{.class_4729-0} (Psa. 105:10).

It is called ["an everlasting covenant"]{.class_4730-0} because it was a [unilateral]{.class_4729-0} agreement made by God with Abraham with [no conditions attached.]{.class_4729-0} It was made while Abraham was asleep (Gen. 15:12), and it had nothing to do with anything Abraham or any of his descendants did or did not do. It is a [real estate deal]{.class_4729-0} made by God with [one nation]{.class_4729-0} (Ezek. 37:24--28).

One more time with feeling: ["That he would grant unto us, that we BEING DELIVERED OUT OF THE HAND OF OUR ENEMIES might serve him without fear"]{.class_4730-0} (vs. 74). Nobody's trusting Christ; nobody's getting saved in the New Testament sense; nobody's going to Heaven. It's Jesus Christ coming in wrath to ["strike through kings"]{.class_4730-0} till He ["fills the places with the dead bodies"]{.class_4730-0} of the heathen (Psa. 110:5--6).

Even the ["holiness and righteousness"]{.class_4730-0} of verse 75 is not the imputed righteousness of Christ at salvation. It's the holy, righteous reign of the Lord Jesus Christ in the Millennium.

["Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will raise unto David a righteous BRANCH ]{.class_4730-0}\[the Lord Jesus Christ; see our comments on Zech. 3:8 in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets,]{.class_4729-0} Vol. II\][, and a King shall reign and prosper, and shall execute judgment and justice in the earth. In his days Judah shall be saved ]{.class_4730-0}\[in what sense? keep reading\][, and Israel shall dwell safely ]{.class_4730-0}\[i.e., they can live without worrying about invasion, slaughter, gas chambers, suicide bombers, rockets, nuclear missiles, etc.; that's how they are ["saved"]{.class_4730-0}\][: and this is his name whereby he shall be called, THE LORD OUR RIGHTEOUSNESS" ]{.class_4730-0}(Jer. 23:5--6).

Verses 76--77 are obviously the ministry of John the Baptist at []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_66}the First Advent, but verses 78--79 can apply to either Advent. The ["dayspring"]{.class_4730-0} of verse 78 is a reference to the [sun]{.class_4729-0} (Job 28:12), and the Second Advent of Jesus Christ is likened to the ["Sun of righteousness"]{.class_4730-0} treading down His enemies (Mal. 4:2--3; Job 28:13). The return of Jesus Christ is likened to the day dawning and ["the daystar"]{.class_4730-0} (the sun) rising (2 Pet. 1:19). But in verse 78, ["the dayspring"]{.class_4730-0} is visiting the Jews in ["the tender mercy of our God"]{.class_4730-0}---First Advent.

Verse 79 is obviously another reference to the fulfillment of Isaiah 9:1--2 (see Matt. 4:14--16 and our comments on the passage in that Commentary). The problem is that Matthew leaves 25 words out of the passage in Isaiah 9; [he only quotes the part that applies to the First Advent.]{.class_4729-0} There is no disputing the fact that Jesus Christ is ["the light of the world"]{.class_4730-0} and ["the light of life"]{.class_4730-0} spiritually (John 8:12); He is ["the true light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world"]{.class_4730-0} (John 1:9).

But just before the return of the Lord Jesus Christ, God shuts off the lights of this sin-cursed world under the Antichrist (Rev. 16:10, 6:12--13; Matt. 24:29). When Jesus Christ returns in His glory, His coming is likened to lightning shining from the east to the west (Matt. 24:27).

Isaiah 60:1--2 describes the Second Advent this way: ["Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee. For, behold, the darkness shall cover the earth ]{.class_4730-0}\[Rev. 16:10\][, and gross darkness the people: but the LORD shall arise upon thee, and his glory shall be seen upon thee."]{.class_4730-0}

The rest of the chapter deals with the regathering of the Jews to the land of Israel, the second gathering of the Gentiles during the Millennial reign of Christ, and the Gentiles serving those Jews (see Isa. 60:12). So Jesus Christ is a literal light at His Second Coming.

Verse 80: "And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit \[the Holy Spirit---vs. 15, and "the spirit and power" of Elijah---vs. 17\], and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel." The next time we pick up John, he comes marching out of the Judean wilderness (Luke 3:3) wearing a "camel's hair" coat and a leather girdle (Mark 1:6), looking for all the world like the Biblical []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ102.xhtml_page_67}description of Elijah (2 Kings 1:8). He's got grasshopper legs and pieces of wild honeycomb stuck in his beard (Mark 1:6), and he's waving his Bible around yelling, "Repent! Repent! Repent (Matt. 3:1--2)!"

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ501.xhtml}

# Chapter 2 {.heading_78-0}

2:1 And it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Caesar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.

2 (And this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)

3 And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.

4 And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judaea, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)

5 To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.

6 And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.

7 And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.

8 And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.

9 And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.

10 And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.

11 For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.

12 And this shall be a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.

13 And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,

14 Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.

15 And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.

16 And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.

17 And when they had seen it, they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.

18 And all they that heard it wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.

19 But Mary kept all these things, and pondered them in her heart.

20 And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.

This is the so-called "Christmas story." Most people are familiar with it because it is recited and gone through every year in the same fashion until it becomes tiresome; it is overdone that much. The reason for that is because Luke presents Jesus Christ as the Son of man. Humanists and Modernistic, Liberal preachers like Luke because it emphasizes the human part of Christ's two natures.

John isn't used every year because it places the emphasis on Christ's Deity---the Son of God. The Matthew account of the Christmas story isn't covered every year because the emphasis is on Christ as the King of the Jews. But they like that harmless baby in a manger because they don't have to give account to Him as God and King.

The attacks on the [King James ]{.class_4725}text start at the very first verse. All the corrupt, apostate modern versions change the taxation of verses 1--5 to a ["registration"]{.class_4725} or ["census."]{.class_4725} Once more the translators and revisers are covering up for Satan (see the [NIV]{.class_4725} and the [NASV]{.class_4725} convert Lucifer into Jesus Christ \[Rev. 22:16\] in Isa. 14:12) by destroying the cross-reference to Daniel 11:20--21.

"Then shall stand up in his estate a RAISER OF TAXES in the glory of his kingdom: but within few days he shall be []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ501.xhtml_page_70}destroyed, neither in anger, nor in battle. And in his estate shall stand up a vile person, to whom they shall not give the honour of the kingdom: but he shall come in peaceably, and obtain the kingdom by flatteries."

That's a reference to the white horse rider of Revelation 6:2 coming in to conquer with no arrows in his bow. It's the [Antichrist.]{.class_4725} The Holy Spirit is recording history here for you as a [prophetic parable]{.class_4725} (see our comments on Psa. 78:2 in that Commentary). In other words, the First Coming is a picture of the Second Coming.

When Christ came the first time, a Roman dictator was in charge---["Caesar Augustus"]{.class_4723} (vs. 1). When Christ comes the second time, a Roman dictator will be in charge---the Antichrist (Rev. 17:8--12). In the image in Daniel 2, Christ comes the first time in the iron legs---Rome. But that iron isn't replaced like the gold, silver, and brass were; it goes right down into the feet and ten toes and is mingled with clay. That's the kingdom of the Antichrist, and that's the kingdom that's in charge when the Smiting Stone hits the image and grinds the whole thing to powder. The prophetic expositors call that the "revived Roman Empire" because it retains the iron of Rome.

The first time Christ came, there was one universal language, and it wasn't Hebrew or Latin. It was Greek. The second time He comes, there is one universal language---[English.]{.class_4725} God's final authority before His Son's return isn't any Greek or Hebrew "originals," manuscripts, or texts. It's a Bible in the universal language of the last days---English.

At the First Advent, Christ appears secretly to believers only (the shepherds and the wise men); later He shows Himself publicly at John's baptism. At the Second Advent, He comes first secretly to believers only (the Rapture); then seven years later, ["every eye shall see him"]{.class_4723} at His coming in power (Rev. 1:7).

So at Christ's First Coming, there is an international taxation. At His Second Coming, there will be an international taxation. The push by Obama, Clinton, and the Democrats is for the United Nations to levy a [world tax.]{.class_4725} So in the next few years you can expect an [International]{.class_4725} Revenue Service to replace the Internal Revenue Service.

["(And this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ501.xhtml_page_71} (vs. 2). The whole sentence is a parenthetical note in the historical narrative. ["Cyrenius"]{.class_4723} was the Greek name of [Quirinius,]{.class_4725} the Roman Governor of Syria at that time. From the time of Alexander the Great, Israel was a part of the province of Syria.

For many years, this passage was a favorite of Bible-rejecting infidels because the only historical records of "the enrollment of Quirinius" had it taking place between A.D. 10 and A.D. 12. Of course, Luke, being a much more accurate and meticulous scholar than his critics, was careful to include the words ["first made,"]{.class_4723} letting you know that Quirinius was Governor of Syria [more than once.]{.class_4725} Scholarship eventually caught up with the Bible and discovered that Quirinius was Governor of Syria [twice, the first time in 4 B.C. when Christ was born.]{.class_4725}

The Bible was [right]{.class_4725} and scholarship was [wrong.]{.class_4725} Billy Sunday used to say, "When the Bible says one thing and scholarship says another, [scholarship can go plumb to the Devil."]{.class_4725}

There's an old riddle that says: "What was the largest island before Australia was discovered?"
