["But his mother kept all these sayings in her heart."]{.class_4723} This is the second time in the chapter such a thing comes up (see vs. 19). Her Son has put down her attempt to protect the integrity of His birth and has finally claimed God as His Father. Even though the angel told her that Jesus would ["be called the Son of the Highest"]{.class_4723} (1:32), she never really thought about what that meant until now, and even then she didn't fully comprehend it (vs. 50).

["And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man"]{.class_4723} (vs. 52). Verses 51--52 are the only statement on Christ's life from age 12--30 (cf. 3:23). That's an interim period of eighteen years that the Holy Spirit purposefully [omits]{.class_4725} from the inspired account for His own reasons. What you have to keep in mind in that regard is that God's [omissions]{.class_4725} are just as important as His [additions]{.class_4725} and [inclusions.]{.class_4725} There are some things the Lord doesn't tell you because He doesn't want you to know and doesn't think you should have; and, of course, that is His prerogative in the matter.

This lack of information on the early life of Christ has led to the so-called "search for the historic Jesus" about which you read every Christmas and Easter in [Time, Newsweek,]{.class_4725} etc.; meaning [no "Jesus" who ever existed in history.]{.class_4725} The real Jesus of history is described by four biographers whose works have been preserved in the Holy Bible (see our comments on 1:2). The junk you see put out by ABC, NBC, and CBS citing a bunch of Liberal infidels is the hallucinated pipe dreams of deceived fools.

The esoteric literature of the New Agers has him spending seventeen years, after He was twelve years old, in India to learn about "religion" (e.g., [The Aquarian Gospel,]{.class_4725} and [The Lost Years of Jesus]{.class_4725} by Elizabeth Clare Prophet, 1984). The idea is to have Jesus borrow everything He said from Egyptian Gnosticism (Matt. 2), Hindu mythology, and Greek philosophy because that is the junk that has been taught in every liberal College in Europe and America since 1700. They teach that great "wisdom" comes from the East []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ501.xhtml_page_105}(India, China, Japan---Confucianism, Buddhism, Hinduism), so if Christ has great wisdom (vss. 40, 52), He had to get it from there.

Listen, the wisest man in either Testament was no Greek philosopher, Egyptian Gnostic, Hindu mystic, or Buddhist monk; he was a [Judean Jew.]{.class_4725} In the Old Testament, that man was [Solomon.]{.class_4725} God told him, ["I have given thee a wise and an understanding heart; so that there was none like thee before thee, neither after thee shall any arise like unto thee"]{.class_4723} (1 Kings 3:12). The Bible says, ["And Solomon's wisdom EXCELLED the wisdom of all the children of the east country]{.class_4723} \[India, Tibet, China\][, and all the wisdom of Egypt"]{.class_4723} (1 Kings 4:30).

Amateurs like the Dalai Lama and Buddha weren't even "in the running" with Solomon. The only man ever to surpass Solomon in wisdom was the [God-man,]{.class_4725} Jesus Christ---another [Judean Jew.]{.class_4725} The Bible says of Him, ["In whom are hid ALL THE TREASURES OF WISDOM and knowledge"]{.class_4723} (Col. 2:3).

Why would you go for wisdom to some Greek philosopher who committed suicide because he got caught molesting young boys, and who, before he died, sacrificed a rooster to the snake god (Socrates)? Why would you go over to India where they bust open a coconut and pour its milk out to a snake god while twenty feet away from them are people starving to death? Why would you seek wisdom from a bunch of dumb thumps like that?

That Bible says that [Jesus Christ]{.class_4725} ["is made unto us WISDOM, and righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 1:30). Wisdom isn't in Greek philosophy or Eastern religions; it's in [Him]{.class_4725} and in [His Book]{.class_4725} (Psa. 119:100).

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml}

# Chapter 3 {.heading_78-0}

3:1 Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judaea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituraea and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the tetrarch of Abilene,

2 Annas and Caiaphas being the high priests, the word of God came unto John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.

3 And he came into all the country about Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins;

4 As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the prophet, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

5 Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways shall be made smooth;

6 And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.

7 Then said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

8 Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father: for I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.

9 And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.

Notice again in verse 1 how Luke gives you the precise historical time periods and characters so there will be no doubt in your mind that what he is writing is [not]{.class_4725} mythology, fables, or legends (see our comments on 1:5). The Roman Emperor is now Tiberius, the Caesar who ruled after Augustus (2:1). The kingdom of Herod the Great (Matt. 2) has been split into four parts (a ["tetrarch"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_107} is the ruler over the fourth part of a kingdom): Herod's son Antipas ruled over Galilee; Antipas' half-brother Philip ruled over Ituraea; a "dark horse" named ["Lysanias"]{.class_4723} ruled over Abilene; and the region of Judea had come under Roman administration with Pontius Pilate as the Governor. Ituraea and Abilene are north of Galilee, and you can see their locations in any Bible atlas.

As at other times in Israel's history, there are two high priests serving at the same time (vs. 2). The general interpretation for this is that the elder one, Annas, is up in years, so he has turned his day-to-day responsibilities over to his son-in-law Caiaphas (see John 18:13 and our comments on the verse in that Commentary). But it was nothing unusual for there to be two high priests serving together. In David's day, [Zadok]{.class_4725} and [Ahimelech]{.class_4725} served as high priests at the same time (2 Sam. 8:17), and later on ["Zadok and Abiathar"]{.class_4723} (2 Sam. 15:35). When Judah was carried away into captivity, there were ["Seraiah the CHIEF PRIEST, and Zephaniah the SECOND PRIEST"]{.class_4723} (2 Kings 25:18). When the Jews returned from exile, there were [Joshua]{.class_4725} the high priest and a secondary priest named [Josiah]{.class_4725} (see our comments on Zech. 6:14). So the reason for Annas and Caiaphas being in power at the same time is irrelevant. The historical fact is that they were, and there is no contradiction in Luke's or John's account.

It was in this setting that John the Baptist steps out of the wilderness ["preaching the baptism of repentance FOR the remission of sins"]{.class_4723} (vs. 3). Now mark that little three-letter word ["for."]{.class_4723} Every denomination except the Baptists will say it means you are to be baptized IN ORDER TO GET your sins remitted. That will be the Catholics, the Greek Orthodox, the Episcopalians, the Lutherans, the Presbyterians, the Methodists, the Church of Christ, and even a lot of the Charismatic groups. They may differ as to the mode of baptism, the candidates for baptism, and the relationship of baptism to salvation; but every single one of them will tell you in their confessions of faith that you can get your sins forgiven ("remitted") by [water.]{.class_4725} That is just what it does NOT mean.

Now I'm going to give you some third grade English. If a man goes to jail FOR stealing, is he going to jail SO he can steal? Of course []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_108}not; he's going to jail BECAUSE he stole. Did Christ die FOR your sins? Yes (1 Cor. 15:3)! Well, did He die in order for you to sin or because you did sin? It's obviously [because]{.class_4725} of your sins. You would think anyone could figure that out.

Here's a place where ["for"]{.class_4723} seems to mean "in order to get," but if you study the thing out, it doesn't even mean that there either: Matthew 27:28, ["For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed FOR the remission of sins."]{.class_4723}

Well, didn't Christ die so you could get your sins remitted? Yes, but look over at Hebrews 9:22.

"And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission."

The last half of that verse is often used in personal work in reference to the blood of Christ, and there's not anything wrong with doing that, but that is not the doctrinal context of the verse. The ["blood"]{.class_4723} in Hebrews 9:22 is the blood of bulls and goats that was sprinkled on the furniture and vessels of the Tabernacle to sanctify them to God's service (Heb. 9:13, 19--21). Under the Law, God would remit sins based on the blood of bulls and goats, but He wouldn't take them away.

["For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 10:4).

For 4,000 years God had been forgiving sins without redeeming them because there was no basis for doing it. But at Calvary, things change. The blood of Christ could do more than just remit sins. That's why Colossians 1:14 says:[ "In whom we have redemption THROUGH HIS BLOOD, even the forgiveness of sins."]{.class_4723}

The corrupt modern "Bibles" remove ["through his blood,"]{.class_4723} making you think that ["redemption"]{.class_4723} is [synonymous]{.class_4725} with ["forgiveness of sins."]{.class_4723} [They're not the same.]{.class_4725}

All through the Old Testament God [forgave]{.class_4725} sins without [redeeming]{.class_4725} them (Exod. 34:7), because sins couldn't be taken away with the blood of bulls and goats. But the blood of Christ could do [both.]{.class_4725} When Jesus said ["this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for the remission of sins,"]{.class_4723} the ["sins"]{.class_4723} had [already]{.class_4725} been remitted in the Old Testament by the blood of bulls and goats, but they could be [completely removed]{.class_4725} by the blood of Jesus []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_109}Christ. How do you know that is so? Two verses: Romans 3:25 and Hebrews 9:15.

["Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness FOR THE REMISSION OF SINS THAT ARE PAST, through the forbearance of God"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 3:25).

["And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions THAT WERE UNDER THE FIRST TESTAMENT, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 9:15).

So the first application of the blood atonement was to the sins of the Old Testament saints: it was ["FOR"]{.class_4723}---that is, [because of]{.class_4725}---["the remission of sins."]{.class_4723} So mark it down: anyone who drags you over to Acts 2:38 to get your sins forgiven by water baptism is trying to give you a one-way ticket to [Hell.]{.class_4725} I would say a good 900 million people have been damned by the little three-letter word [for.]{.class_4725}

So what does John mean by ["preaching the baptism of repentance FOR the remission of sins"]{.class_4723}? He's telling those Jews, "The time has finally come: the Messiah is here. Repent and your sins will be forgiven. Then come down here and get under the water to show that you believe it." He's not telling anybody that their sins are going to be forgiven when they get baptized. That's why no Baptist ever baptizes anybody in order to get his sins forgiven; we baptize someone whose sins have [already]{.class_4725} been forgiven (see Acts 10:47).

Verses 4--6 are a quote from Isaiah 40:3--5, and we have covered the material on that passage under our remarks on 1:17 (which see).

Now if you want to see how cockeyed American Christianity actually is, just compare John the Baptist's preaching to this purpose-driven, positive-thinking, possibility-thinking, promise-keeping, "love-wins" mess you are getting today from Joel Osteen, Rick Warren, Robert Schuller, Rob Bell, et al.

["O GENERATION OF VIPERS, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 8).

Why, that's what Jesus Christ called the scribes and Pharisees over in Matthew 23.

["Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 23:33).

One time Jesus asked the disciples, [''Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 16:13). Do you know who the first person that came to mind was? ["John the Baptist."]{.class_4723}

You've become so used to all these pictures that show Jesus Christ as soft-skinned and effeminate-looking that you've forgotten that He was a carpenter who could claim the loyalties of commercial fishermen. You reckon commercial fishermen are going to follow some half-queer swishing around in long robes, with honey dripping from his mouth, saying, "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall see Gawd." Have you ever met a carpenter or a commercial fisherman? I have. They're not like this Osteen-Warren-Schuller crowd.

One time the head of the Bible department at Bob Jones University wrote: "There is never a particle of [bitterness]{.class_4733} or [unkindness]{.class_4733} in the words of the Lord Jesus Christ" (Stewart Custer, [The Truth about the King James Version Controversy,]{.class_4733}[ ]{.class_4749}pg. 30). Well, what would a fellow at a "militant" Christian University be doing talking like an [unsaved Liberal?]{.class_4733}

Stewart Custer is talking about the Lord Jesus Christ who said:

["Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do"]{.class_4723} (John 8:44).

["Ye fools and blind"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 23:17, 19).

["Thou blind Pharisee"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 23:26).

["Go ye, and tell that fox"]{.class_4723} (Luke 13:32).

["Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 25:41).

In the Old Testament, that same Jesus (He's [God]{.class_4725} in the flesh, remember?) said:

["I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 1:26).

["Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there be none to deliver"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 50:22).

["I will tread them in mine anger, and trample them in my fury; and their blood shall be sprinkled upon my garments, and I will stain my raiment"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 63:3).

[That's]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_111} the Jesus Christ of the Bible. He's not like the one invented by Stewart Custer, Joel Osteen, or Rick Warren.

Verse 8: ["Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance."]{.class_4723} There's a good example of the [King James]{.class_4725} defining its own terms. If you ever wondered what the word ["meet"]{.class_4723} meant when it said ["fruits meet for repentance"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 3:8), the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible told you what it meant right in its own text.

["Bring forth\...fruits worthy of repentance."]{.class_4723} Show you mean business; the possession should match the profession. That's not just Old Testament under the Law either (which is the case here---16:16). The Apostle Paul preached to the Gentiles under the New Testament ["that they should repent and turn to God, and DO WORKS MEET FOR REPENTANCE" ]{.class_4723}(Acts 26:20). When you are saved, you are ["created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 2:10). When a Hyperdispensationalist tries to divorce the ministry of Paul from the Old Testament and the Gospels, he knows nothing about the "Pauline ministry." Paul emphasized works as much as John the Baptist did (see Eph. 4).

["And begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to our father."]{.class_4723} That's what they said to Jesus in John 8:33; Jesus called them liars (John 8:46). Being a [physical child]{.class_4725} of Abraham means nothing (see Gal. 4:22--31); after all, Ishmael and Esau both "had Abraham to their father," and God rejected both of them. Paul said, ["he is not a Jew, which is one outwardly\...But he is a Jew, which is one inwardly"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 2:28--29).

The tragedy of the Jewish people is that after receiving the promises through Abraham (Rom. 9:4), after having ["the oracles of God"]{.class_4723} committed to them (Rom. 3:2), after having the Lord send His Son to save them from their sins (Matt. 1:21), a lot of them are going to miss out on everything and [wind up in Hell.]{.class_4725} Jesus said, ["many shall come from the east and west]{.class_4723} \[Gentiles\][, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven. But the children of the kingdom]{.class_4723} \[Jews\][ shall be cast out in outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 8:11--12).

["For I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham."]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_112} Well, hello, Darwin! What are you doing here in Luke 3? Darwin has you coming from [rocks.]{.class_4725} Evolution teaches you came from rocks with no God involved. Sure, man! The earth got slung out of the sun, and the igneous rock cooled into the metamorphic rock. Then a little pool of water formed in the rock, and "in that pool two tiny amebas---the start of life" (to borrow a line of baloney from [Bugs Bunny]{.class_4725}).

That's evolution; that's Darwin. How is that any more believable than God making man out of [dust]{.class_4725} (Gen. 2:7)? One relies on [blind, random chance;]{.class_4725} the other relies on an [intelligent Creator.]{.class_4725}

When I was growing up, we used to sing: "Be kind to your web-footed friends, for a duck may be somebody's mother" (sung to the tune of "Stars and Stripes Forever"). Well, if you came from rocks through evolution, you had better watch where you step; you might be walking over someone's future great, great, great (etc.) grandmother.

The fact of the matter is that God [did]{.class_4725} raise up other children to Abraham, and according to the revelation of God in His word, He did it through a Rock.

["And if ye be Christ's, then are ye Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise"]{.class_4723} (Gal. 3:29).

["Ye also as LIVELY STONES, are built up a spiritual house\...Wherefore also it is contained in the scripture, Behold I lay in Sion a chief corner STONE, elect, precious: and he that believeth on him shall not be confounded. Unto you therefore which believe he is precious: but unto them which be disobedient, the STONE which the builders disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner, And a STONE of stumbling, and a ROCK of offence"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 2:5--8).

Verse 9:[ "And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire."]{.class_4723} Another Second Advent reference. That's the fire that burns up the wicked at Christ's return (Matt. 13:49--50; 2 Thess. 1:7--8).

Malachi 4:1, ["For, behold, the day cometh, that shall burn as an oven; and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_113}up, saith the LORD of hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch."]{.class_4723}

There's Jesus Christ coming up as the sun in the morning in Malachi 4:2 (cf. 2 Pet. 1:19; 2 Sam. 23:4); there He is stomping all over the Antichrist's army in Malachi 4:3 (cf. Rev. 14:20); and there's Moses and Elijah of Revelation 11 in Malachi 4:4--6. Like I told you under 1:17, everything's ready for the Second Advent at the First Advent.

10 And the people asked him, saying, What shall we do then?

11 He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.

12 Then came also publicans to be baptized, and said unto him, Master, what shall we do?

13 And he said unto them, Exact no more than that which is appointed you.

14 And the soldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we do? And he said unto them, Do violence to no man, neither accuse any falsely; and be content with your wages.

The passage deals with the ["fruits worthy of repentance"]{.class_4723} back in verse 8. This is the stuff John tells them to do to show they mean business.

Verse 11 is the perfect verse for Obama's Socialistic system: the "redistribution of wealth." But notice that nothing in the passage is addressed to a government, a Congress, a Senate, or any "bureaucracy." Everything John says is addressed to [individuals]{.class_4725} who are [voluntarily]{.class_4725} submitting to it. Nobody is being [forced]{.class_4725} by the BATF, the FBI, the IRS, or a SWAT team to give away their extra clothing or food. Marxist Communism or Socialism isn't Christian charity; it's [organized crime.]{.class_4725}

Someone asked the gangster Lucky Luciano at the end of his life if he would have done anything differently. He replied, "Yeah, I'd get a license." The only difference between the Mafia and the United States government since World War I is that the government is [licensed]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_114} to intimidate and threaten you, and take your money and assets. "Lucky" finally realized that if he had just joined up with the government, he could have kept on doing what he did [with a license.]{.class_4725}

The ["publicans"]{.class_4723} of verse 12 are the tax collectors. They are called ["publicans"]{.class_4723} because they are the [public servants]{.class_4725} of the Roman government. The Jews not only despised them because they were [collaborators]{.class_4725}, but also because they were thieves and extortionists. The Romans didn't care how much money the publicans collected in taxes as long as the government got its cut. So the publicans would often demand more than what was actually owed in taxes, and then they would pocket the rest (see what Zacchaeus said in 19:8).

John didn't tell the publicans to stop working for Rome or collecting taxes. In fact, you will never read anywhere in the New Testament where John, Jesus, or any of the apostles ever taught that a Christian shouldn't pay his taxes; the modern anti-tax movement is [unscriptural.]{.class_4725} What John told the publicans was to collect only what the government required.

John's answer to the soldiers in verse 14 must come as quite a shock to the pacifists, conscientious objectors, and "anti-war" Christians. John doesn't tell them to desert or that they were wrong for joining or to disobey an order or anything like that. The Christian in the military is to obey ["the powers that be"]{.class_4723} (see Rom. 13:1--7 and our remarks on those verses in that Commentary).

When John tells the soldiers ["Do violence to no man,"]{.class_4723} he's not telling them not to fight an enemy in a war. The Romans were an occupying army. John's telling those soldiers not to bully the populace just because they can; they're not to get into fights back in the barracks and off duty. ["Neither accuse any falsely."]{.class_4723} They're not to lie about their buddies or their C.O. or those whose land they're occupying. ["And be content with your wages."]{.class_4723} No gambling to get your buddy's pay; don't steal from the people you're occupying. Those are exactly the types of things a G.I. needs to hear.

15 And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of John, whether he were the Christ, []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_115}or not;

16 John answered, saying unto them all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:

17 Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner; but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.

18 And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.

19 But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias his brother Philip's wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,

20 Added yet this above all, that he shut up John in prison.

John comes on so strong that everyone's trying to figure out who he is (see John 1:19--20). They ask him, "Are you the Christ?" He says, "No." "Well, are you Elijah?" "No (see our comments on 1:17)." "Are you the Prophet God told Moses He would send (Deut. 18:18)?" "No." "Then who are you, John?" "I'm a [voice,"]{.class_4725} said John.

John the Baptist was a [street preacher,]{.class_4725} like John Wesley and George Whitefield; the exception being that he never occupied a pulpit in a church building a day in his life. He was a ["messenger"]{.class_4723} (Mal. 3:1); he was the herald of the King. He had no reputation of his own; before his death he said, ["He must increase]{.class_4723} \[speaking of Christ\][, and I must decrease"]{.class_4723} (John 3:30).

John called himself ["the friend of the bridegroom"]{.class_4723} (John 3:29). In a wedding, the sole purpose of the "best man" is to stand with the groom in the background, and to witness and rejoice in the groom's marriage to the bride. No wedding is about the best man; it's about the bride and the groom.

So John's purpose was to prepare the way for Jesus Christ and to point men to Jesus Christ (John 1:29--37), and then to fade away into the background. So he says, ["I indeed baptize you with water"]{.class_4723} (vs. 16). Why? So they could get their sins forgiven? No!

["And I knew him not: but that he should be made []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_116}manifest to Israel, THEREFORE am I come baptizing with water"]{.class_4723} (John 1:31).

The purpose of John's baptism was to manifest ["the Lord's Christ"]{.class_4723} to [Israel,]{.class_4725} and you can get the details on that in our comments on that verse in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on John.]{.class_4725}

A lot of the brethren don't think I'm too good of a Baptist because I don't think the Baptist Church started with John the Baptist. Why do I not teach that? Because there were no New Testament Christians in the Gospels; Christians don't show up until eleven chapters into the book of Acts (Acts 11:26). John himself wasn't a "Christian." He said he was ["the friend of the bridegroom"]{.class_4723} (John 3:29); he was [not]{.class_4725} a part of the Bride of Christ (Eph. 1:22--23 cf. 5:22--32). John was an [Old Testament prophet]{.class_4725} under the [Old Testament Law]{.class_4725} (Matt. 11:13; Luke 16:16). If he were a "Baptist," he wasn't any kind of a Baptist in the New Testament sense. He didn't baptize anybody to picture the death, burial, and resurrection of Jesus Christ (Rom. 6:3--4 cf. 1 Pet. 3:21); he didn't baptize anyone who had already trusted Jesus Christ as Saviour and had already received the Holy Spirit (Acts 10:47). John baptized for one reason: to manifest Christ to [Israel; not]{.class_4725} the Church, [not]{.class_4725} Christians, and [not]{.class_4725} the Body of Christ.

["But one mightier than I cometh."]{.class_4723} The reason Christ is ["mightier"]{.class_4723} is obviously because He is ["God ]{.class_4723}\
[\...manifest in the flesh."]{.class_4723} John says, ["After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: FOR HE WAS BEFORE ME"]{.class_4723} (John 1:30). He's not talking about age because John was six months older than Jesus (see 1:36). But John the apostle traces Jesus' lineage clear back [before Genesis 1:1]{.class_4727} (see John 1:1). Micah 5:2 says, ["whose goings forth have been of old, FROM EVERLASTING."]{.class_4723}

["The latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose."]{.class_4723} Why is that? Because John knew he was a [sinner]{.class_4725} and Christ would be [sinless.]{.class_4725} When Jesus shows up to be baptized, John says to Him, ["I have need to be baptized of thee, and comest thou to me?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 3:14). John knew that he had plenty to repent of but that Jesus didn't need to be baptized to show He had repented of anything.

["He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire."]{.class_4723} The Pentecostals, Holiness, and Charismatics will immediately drag you over to Acts 2:3--4 to prove to you that the two baptisms are the same, but there is no fire in that passage. The verse says ["cloven tongues LIKE AS OF FIRE"]{.class_4723} (Acts 2:3). There is no mention of fire in Acts 1:5 where Jesus told what was going to take place at Pentecost: ["Ye shall be baptized with the HOLY GHOST not many days hence."]{.class_4723} Where's the fire? If you want to see the ["fire"]{.class_4723} in Acts 2, go down to verse 19.

"And I will shew wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and FIRE, and vapour of smoke."

That's the fire that comes at the Second Advent (see our comments on Acts 2:16--20 in that Commentary). That's what the baptism of fire is here in Luke 3. Look at the next verse.

["Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner, but the chaff he will BURN WITH FIRE UNQUENCHABLE" ]{.class_4723}(vs. 17).

That's what takes place in the Parable of the Wheat and the Tares. The wheat is gathered into the ["barn,"]{.class_4723} and the tares are bound in bundles and burned (Matt. 13:30). The interpretation Jesus put on that was the wicked being cast ["into the furnace of fire"]{.class_4723} at ["the end of the world"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 13:49--50). The unquenchable fire is in [Hell]{.class_4725} (Mark 9:44, 46, 48). [You don't want the baptism of fire.]{.class_4725}

Verse 18:[ "And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people."]{.class_4723} John the Baptist is more than just a prophet; he's a [preacher.]{.class_4725} When John shows up, he shows up [preaching]{.class_4725} (Mark 1:4, 7).

Now, every pastor is to be a [teacher]{.class_4725} (Eph. 4:11; 1 Tim. 3:2), but his primary calling is to ["Preach the word"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 4:2). There is a difference between [preaching]{.class_4725} and [teaching.]{.class_4725} Teaching is simply [imparting knowledge;]{.class_4725}[ ]{.class_4723}the aim of preaching is to get somebody to [do something]{.class_4725} about what they've heard. Preaching involves reproving, rebuking, and exhorting (2 Tim. 4:2).

The Bible says that in the last days people will ["heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 4:3). Americans don't mind Bible teachers, but they can't stand real preaching. It upsets them; it offends them.

The Bible schools haven't been turning out preachers. All they've put out are ministerial students who don't have the guts to tell their congregations where to head in. Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "What this country needs is a dozen John the Baptists to pick it up by the back of the neck and the seat of the britches and shake it over Hell." You're not going to get them. All you're going to get are some nice, smooth, slick fellows like Rick Warren or Joel Osteen who know how to couch what they say in some crafty form to make it easy for baby tummies to digest.

Brethren, the command of the Apostle Paul to the young minister is: ["rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith"]{.class_4723} (Tit. 1:13). It's: ["Preach the word\...reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 4:2). A Christian's speech is to be ["seasoned with salt"]{.class_4723} (Col. 4:6), and salt [burns]{.class_4725} when it is put on an open wound. The prophets weren't "sugar sticks"; they were [salty.]{.class_4725}

Verses 19--20 are a reference to the events that led to John's death (see Matt. 14:1--10; Mark 6:17--28). The ["Herod"]{.class_4723}[ ]{.class_4748}here is Herod Antipas, and according to the historians, the ["Philip" ]{.class_4723}is supposed to be a different son of Herod the Great than Philip the tetrarch (cf. vs. 1).

Be that as it may, Herod had committed adultery with Herodias, Philip's wife, and John the Baptist read Herod the "riot act" for it. Herodias wanted John killed for that, but the problem was her new husband listened to that Baptist preacher.

["For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just man and an holy, and observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly"]{.class_4723} (Mark 6:20).

Herod liked to hear John preach, and he even did some of the things John told him to do. The problem was that he seared his conscience with a belly dancer and got John killed.

21 Now when all the people were baptized, it came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened,

22 And the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_119}said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well pleased.

The passage obviously refers back to a time before John was arrested and thrown in prison. There are times in the Gospels where the Holy Spirit jumps ahead in the narrative and then goes back and covers material that took place beforehand (e.g., Matt. 26:1--13 cf. John 12:1--8).

["It came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened"]{.class_4723} (vs. 21). The first time heaven was opened was when God sent down ["the fountains of the great deep"]{.class_4723} in Noah's Flood (Gen. 7:11). Heaven is opened here for the Holy Spirit to come down; it is opened in Revelation 4:1 for someone to go up (see our comments on the verse in that Commentary); and it is opened again in Revelation 19:11 for Jesus Christ to come back at the Second Advent. I'll tell you something else: if you will give faithfully and cheerfully, God will open it for you ["and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it"]{.class_4723} (Mal. 3:10).

Now when the Holy Spirit comes down in verse 22, He assumes the bodily form of a dove. That is a revelation on the nature of spirits that all the expositors and commentators miss through 2,000 years of church history---that includes the good ones as well as the bad ones. In the Bible, spirits are pictured as [winged flying creatures.]{.class_4725}

The first time that dove shows up in the Scriptures is in Genesis 8:8. Noah sends two birds out of the ark to search for land. One is a [black]{.class_4725} bird (a raven), and one is [white]{.class_4725} bird (a dove). That black raven is an unclean bird (Lev. 11:13--15) that pictures [death]{.class_4725} (see the famous poem by Edgar Allen Poe). That raven is a carrion bird, and when he found a dead body (the Flood had just drowned out a couple of million people), he lit down on that thing and started feasting and didn't return to the ark. That white dove comes back to Noah with ["an olive leaf"]{.class_4723} in her mouth that she had plucked off a tree; that olive tree is a picture of the Tree of Life (see our comments on Gen. 2:9 in that Commentary). So when the politicians want to picture peace, they use the dove. So the dove is a clean bird that typifies the Holy Spirit who brings peace and life to the believer.

The Song of Solomon says the Bridegroom has "dove's eyes" []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_120}(Song of Sol. 1:15). That's a picture of the Holy Spirit in Jesus Christ. The same thing is said of the Bride in Song of Solomon 4:1, which means that a Christian should be like his Lord and look at things the same way He does.

All throughout the Scriptures, birds are types of spirits. Over in Revelation 18:2, it says, ["Babylon the great is fallen, is fallen, and is become the habitation of DEVILS, and the hold of every FOUL SPIRIT\...."]{.class_4723} What are those ["devils"]{.class_4723} and ["foul spirits"]{.class_4723}? Keep on reading. ["\...and a cage of every UNCLEAN AND HATEFUL BIRD."]{.class_4723} Unclean birds typify evil spirits.

Don't believe me? Turn on over to Isaiah 34. The whole chapter is on the lake of fire that will be in Edom at the Second Advent and in the Millennium. But look at what's in there: ["the cormorant"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), ["the bittern"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), ["the raven"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), ["the screech owl"]{.class_4723} (vs. 14), ["the great owl"]{.class_4723} (vs. 15). Those are all unclean birds (Lev. 11). Well, God's not sending birds to Hell; those are demons. Look at Isaiah 34:13 and notice the similarity in wording to Revelation 18:2.

["And it shall be an HABITATION OF DRAGONS]{.class_4723} \[the Devil---Rev. 12:9\][, and a COURT OF OWLS."]{.class_4723}

Let's put the capstone on this, shall we? In the Parable of the Sower, do you know what those ["fowls of the air"]{.class_4723} are that devour the seed that falls on ["the way side"]{.class_4723} (Mark 4:4)? Mark 4:15, Scripture with Scripture, the Scriptures interpreting themselves without the help of any "Holy Mother Church," Pope, Cardinal, or Bishop:

"And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, SATAN cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts."

In the parable, Satan is a bunch of [birds.]{.class_4725} So in a [King James]{.class_4725} Bible, demons are called [devils]{.class_4725} because they are unclean spirits that are part of [THE]{.class_4725} [Devil. ]{.class_4725}We will get into that more in Luke 8 and 11.

Ecclesiastes 10:20, ["Curse not the king, no not in thy thought; and curse not the rich in thy bedchamber; for A BIRD OF THE AIR shall carry the voice, and THAT WHICH HATH WINGS shall tell the matter."]{.class_4723}

That's a [spirit]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_121} carrying your thoughts to someone and telling them; hence the expression: "A [little bird]{.class_4725} told me." If there is an electronic listening device in your room, the room is [bugged.]{.class_4725} The bug has [wings,]{.class_4725} so folks say when they want to eavesdrop on a conversation and can't: "Wouldn't you like to be a [fly]{.class_4725} on the wall in that room?" And we'll get into that more too when we get to Luke 8 and 11.

["And the HOLY GHOST descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and A VOICE CAME FROM HEAVEN, which said, Thou art MY BELOVED SON; in thee I am well pleased"]{.class_4723} (vs. 22). There are the three persons of the Trinity right there in the verse. It's not just different manifestations of one God like we saw in Revelation 4 (see our remarks on ["the angel of the Lord"]{.class_4723} under 2:9); it's three [distinct persons.]{.class_4725} There's God the Holy Spirit descending in the form of a dove, God the Father speaking from Heaven, and God the Son being baptized.

That's [one God]{.class_4725} (Deut. 6:4) manifested in [three persons]{.class_4725} (1 John 5:7); it's not three separate gods as the Mormons, Jehovah's Witnesses, and Moslems suppose the doctrine of the Trinity to teach. There are all sorts of "Triads" in pagan mythology (e.g., Brahma, Vishnu, and Siva in Hinduism), but only one [Triune]{.class_4725} God. The pagan Triads were separate gods (polytheism) created from [chaos]{.class_4725} (evolution); the God of the Bible is three persons united into one being who is the [Creator Himself.]{.class_4725} For further study on the Trinity, I refer you to the chapter on "The Trinity" in [Theological Studies,]{.class_4725} Volume I.

["In thee I am well pleased."]{.class_4723} God has said that to only one person in the entire history of the human race. There are nearly seven billion people on earth at the time of this writing (2012), and you could probably add another seven billion onto that number if you counted the entire population of the earth back to Adam. In that entire time, out of that mass of humanity, when did God ever say to anyone else, "You're my [beloved Son,]{.class_4725} and I'm [well pleased]{.class_4725} in you"? He never said it about Abraham, Moses, or David; and He said some pretty good things about those men in the Bible. He never said it about any of the apostles; in fact, He called them all ["evil"]{.class_4723} (11:13). He never said it about any King, Prime Minister, Emperor, Pope, or President; and he sure []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_122}[never said it about Mohammed.]{.class_4725}

He said it about [Jesus Christ.]{.class_4725} In fact, He said it [twice:]{.class_4725} here at Christ's baptism and again at the Transfiguration (Matt. 17:5). God was ["well pleased"]{.class_4723} with His Son at His First Advent, and He is ["well pleased"]{.class_4723} with His Son at His Second Advent (see Matt. 16:28; 2 Pet. 1:16--17).

Now if you are saved, do you know what the Bible says about you? It says you are ["accepted in the beloved"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 1:6). That means that although you have done nothing to please God in your natural state (see Rom. 3:10--23), when you accept Christ as your Saviour and are placed by the Holy Spirit into His Body (1 Cor. 12:13), God accepts you in [the person of His Son.]{.class_4727} He loves you because you are [in Christ,]{.class_4727} God's Son, and the Father loves the Son. One day, you will ["be conformed to the image of his Son"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 8:29), and God the Father will be just as pleased with you as He is with Jesus Christ. WELL GLORY! I can hardly wait!

23 And Jesus himself began to be about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, which was the son of Heli,

24 Which was the son of Matthat, which was the son of Levi, which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Janna, which was the son of Joseph,

25 Which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Amos, which was the son of Naum, which was the son of Esli, which was the son of Nagge,

26 Which was the son of Maath, which was the son of Mattathias, which was the son of Semei, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Juda,

27 Which was the son of Joanna, which was the son of Rhesa, which was the son of Zorobabel, which was the son of Salathiel, which was the son of Neri,

28 Which was the son of Melchi, which was the son of Addi, which was the son of Cosam, which was the son of Elmodam, which was the son of Er,

29 Which was the son of Jose, which was the son of Eliezer, which was the son of Jorim, which was the son of []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_123}Matthat, which was the son of Levi,

30 Which was the son of Simeon, which was the son of Juda, which was the son of Joseph, which was the son of Jonan, which was the son of Eliakim,

31 Which was the son of Melea, which was the son of Menan, which was the son of Mattatha, which was the son of Nathan, which was the son of David,

32 Which was the son of Jesse, which was the son of Obed, which was the son of Booz, which was the son of Salmon, which was the son of Naasson,

33 Which was the son of Aminadab, which was the son of Aram, which was the son of Esrom, which was the son of Phares, which was the son of Juda,

34 Which was the son of Jacob, which was the son of Isaac, which was the son of Abraham, which was the son of Thara, which was the son of Nachor,

35 Which was the son of Saruch, which was the son of Ragau, which was the son of Phalec, which was the son of Heber, which was the son of Sala,

36 Which was the son of Cainan, which was the son of Arphaxad, which was the son of Sem, which was the son of Noe, which was the son of Lamech,

37 Which was the son of Mathusala, which was the son of Enoch, which was the son of Jared, which was the son of Maleleel, which was the son of Cainan,

38 Which was the son of Enos, which was the son of Seth, which was the son of Adam, which was the son of God.

Verse 23 places the baptism of Jesus sometime at the end of July or the beginning of August, being at least forty days earlier than His birthday (4:2). Christ's ministry begins around His birthday on the Feast of Tabernacles, and it runs six months to the Feast of Passover. It then goes through four Passovers in the Gospel of John (John 2:23, 5:1, 6:4, 13:1), with Jesus dying on the fourth Passover (cf. 1 Cor. 5:7). The length of Christ's earthly ministry matches the exact period of time that the Antichrist reigns---three-and-a-half years (42 months---Rev. 13:5).

The genealogy recorded by Luke does not match that of Matthew because Luke traces Christ's line through [Mary.]{.class_4725} ["Heli"]{.class_4723} (vs. 23) was not the father of Joseph (Matt. 1:16), and Joseph was descended from [Solomon]{.class_4725} (Matt. 1:7), not ["Nathan"]{.class_4723} (vs. 31). Joseph was Heli's ["son"]{.class_4723} in the sense of a [son-in-law]{.class_4725} (1 Sam. 24:16 cf. 22:14). There is no record in history, tradition, or Scripture of Mary having a brother, so by Jewish law, Joseph could be reckoned among Heli's sons because the genealogy was reckoned by the [father,]{.class_4725} not the mother.

Now this switch to the line of Mary is important because it gives Jesus the right to the throne of David (see 1:32). You see, although the Davidic line of Kings comes down through Solomon, God cut off that line at Jeconiah (Matt. 1:11--12).

["As I live, saith the LORD, though Coniah the son of Jehoiakim king of Judah were the signet upon my right hand, yet would I pluck thee thence\....O earth, earth, earth, hear the word of the LORD. Thus saith the LORD, Write ye this man childless, a man that shall not prosper in his days: for NO MAN OF HIS SEED SHALL PROSPER, SITTING UPON THE THRONE OF DAVID, AND RULING ANY MORE IN JUDAH" ]{.class_4723}(Jer. 22:24, 29--30).

You see the "Je" on Jeconiah's name in Matthew 1:11--12? That's an abbreviation for the name [Jehovah.]{.class_4725} God got so mad at Jeconiah that in Jeremiah 22, He took His name off the man and just called him ["Coniah."]{.class_4723} Then the Lord gave a revelation to the entire globe (["O earth, earth, earth, hear the word of the LORD"]{.class_4723}---Jer. 22:29). The revelation was that no man descended from Coniah would ever reign as King over Israel.

Problem: [Joseph]{.class_4725} is descended from Coniah (Matt. 1:11--16).

Problem: God promised David that his seed would rule on his throne over Israel ["for ever"]{.class_4723} (2 Sam. 7:12--16; Psa. 89:20--37).

Problem: How can Jesus Christ claim the throne of David if He is the literal son of Joseph? Answer: [He can't.]{.class_4725}

Solution: Have Jesus Christ born of a virgin (Isa. 7:14) from the house of David, with [God]{.class_4725} as His Father, so that He can bypass the curse placed on the descendants of Jeconiah. As the [adopted]{.class_4725} son of Joseph, he can claim the legal right to the throne through Solomon; as an [actual]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_125} son of David through Mary, He can claim the covenantal right to the throne because He's not under the curse of Jeconiah. Problem solved.

Some blank fool who pastors one of these "seeker-sensitive," "emergent" (i.e., neo-Liberal) churches recently said that it wouldn't shake his faith any if he found out through DNA testing that Joseph was Jesus' real father. [It should.]{.class_4725} Not only would it mean that God [lied]{.class_4725} (and on more than one occasion---Jer. 22; Isa. 7) and couldn't be trusted, it would mean that [you couldn't be saved no matter what you did.]{.class_4725}

Folks, if God Almighty weren't the Father of Jesus Christ, that means He had the same blood in Him that you have---[Adam's]{.class_4725} blood, the blood that brings [death]{.class_4725} (Rom. 5:12; 1 Cor. 15:22). If Christ's blood were Adam's blood, that would make Jesus Christ a sinner just like you, and He wouldn't be able to save a dead horse. If He were a sinner just like you, that means He didn't get out of the grave, and if He didn't get out of the grave, you're lost and on your way to Hell with no hope (1 Cor. 15:13--14). The fact that Jesus came up from the dead [proves]{.class_4725} He was virgin born.

["Concerning his Son Jesus Christ our Lord, which was made of the seed of David according to the flesh; And declared to be the SON OF GOD with power, according to the spirit of holiness, BY THE RESURRECTION FROM THE DEAD"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 1:3--4).

Verses 27 and 36 of Luke's genealogy are supposed to be two of the "errors" in a [King James]{.class_4725} Bible because Luke inserts two names into the genealogy that are missing from the accounts in 1 Chronicles 3 and Genesis 5, 10: ["Rhesa"]{.class_4723} (vs. 27) and ["Cainan"]{.class_4723} (vs. 36). Now, outside of the fact that the inclusion of additional material in a later account does not constitute a [real, provable]{.class_4725} error or contradiction, there are all kinds of arguments against Luke's insertions being "mistakes." We have already seen from Matthew's genealogy how the Holy Spirit will [omit]{.class_4725} material for His own purposes (see our comments on Matt. 1:8 in that Commentary).

The standard dodge by the Fundamentalists and Conservatives is that Luke got his material from a B.C. [Septuagint,]{.class_4725} but the scholars confess that the only ["Septuagint"]{.class_4725} we have comes from the fifth column of Origen's [Hexapla,]{.class_4725} written by [Origen himself]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_126} around A.D. 200---more than a hundred years [after]{.class_4725} Luke finished Acts. It was not Luke copying the [Septuagint;]{.class_4725} it was Origen trying to "help God out" by inserting Luke's additions into his Greek Old Testament. For further study on verse 36, we refer you to pages 36--38 of our work on [The "Errors" in the King James Bible]{.class_4725} (1999 ed.).

Finally, notice in verse 38 how Luke traces Christ's ancestry back to the first [man,]{.class_4725} Adam. This reinforces the theme of Luke's Gospel---Jesus as the [Son of man.]{.class_4725} Matthew will take you back to the Father of the Hebrew race (Abraham) and the first God-honored King of Israel (David) in Matthew 1:1 because Matthew is dealing with Jesus as the [King of the Jews.]{.class_4725} John will take you all the way back before Genesis 1:1 because he is presenting Christ as the [Son of God]{.class_4725} (John 1:1--3). Mark has no genealogy in his Gospel because there Jesus is the [Servant of God,]{.class_4725} and no one cares about a servant's lineage.

Both the [Koran]{.class_4725} and the new "Bibles" crash into a brick wall in verse 38, for the Holy Spirit says ["Adam\...was the son of God."]{.class_4723} The [Koran]{.class_4725},[ ]{.class_4725}of course, declares Allah has no son (Sura 23:89, 17:111, 19:34, 112:1--4). The corrupt, apostate modern versions won't go that far, but they will say that Jesus is God's ["one and only Son"]{.class_4725} (see our comments on John 3:16 in that Commentary).

Baloney! Baloney on both counts! You couldn't get a bigger stack of baloney if you went to the deep freeze. God has all kinds of "sons" in the Scriptures. There are:

1\) [angelic]{.class_4725} ["sons of God"]{.class_4723} (Job 1:6, 2:1, 38:7).

2\) Adam was a [created]{.class_4725} ["son of God"]{.class_4723} (here).

3\) Israel was the [national]{.class_4725} son of God (Exod. 4:22).

4\) Jesus Christ is ["the only BEGOTTEN Son of God"]{.class_4723} (John 3:16; Heb. 1:5--6).

5\) Christians are [spiritual]{.class_4725} ["sons of God"]{.class_4723} (John 1:12).

One of these stupid Charismatics on TV figured that since Christ is ["the firstborn among many brethren"]{.class_4723}[ ]{.class_4737}(Rom. 8:29) and would bring ["many sons unto glory"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 2:10) that He is NOT ["the only begotten son of God"]{.class_4723}---EVEN THOUGH THE BIBLE SAYS HE IS. While it is true that Christians are ["begotten"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 4:15; 1 Pet. 1:3), that "begatting" is not the same as Christ's. A Christian is ["begotten\...through the gospel"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 4:15) ["by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ744.xhtml_page_127} (1 Pet. 1:3). That's a reference to the [New Birth.]{.class_4725} Christ didn't need to be ["born again"]{.class_4723} because He was [born right the first time.]{.class_4725} A Christian is [spiritually]{.class_4725} begotten at salvation.

["Of his own will BEGAT HE US with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures"]{.class_4723} (James 1:18).

["We know that whosoever is BORN OF GOD sinneth not; but he that is BEGOTTEN OF GOD keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not"]{.class_4723} (1 John 5:18).

["Being BORN AGAIN, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 1:23).

So it's a spiritual begatting. Jesus Christ was [physically]{.class_4725} begotten of God. When you are finally "physically begotten" by God, it [won't]{.class_4725} be the same way Christ was. Jesus Christ got His sinless body [when He was born;]{.class_4725} you get yours [at the Rapture.]{.class_4725}

["For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ: who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself"]{.class_4723} (Phil. 3:20--21).

["Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is"]{.class_4723} (1 John 3:2).

You are a son of God by the New Birth. In theology, that's known as "regeneration." The word appears only twice in your Bible: once in Titus 3:5 in reference to the spiritual New Birth at salvation and once in Matthew 19:28 in reference to the physical rebirth of the earth at Christ's return (cf. Isa. 11:4--10). When Jesus Christ returns at the Second Advent, the physical creation is "born again" (Rom. 8:19--22). The physical rebirth of the Christian takes place seven years earlier at the Rapture.

["Even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, to wit, THE REDEMPTION OF OUR BODY"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 8:23).

["Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed. In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump; for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed. For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 15:51--53).

We are adopted [spiritually]{.class_4725} as sons of God at salvation (Eph. 1:5; Gal. 4:5), and our bodies are adopted [physically]{.class_4725} to be like Jesus Christ at the Rapture (Rom. 8:18--23, 29). I've got the down-payment---the "earnest money" (see 2 Cor. 1:22, 5:5)---on it now; I'm just waiting for the bill to be "paid in full" (["the redemption of our body"]{.class_4723}).

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml}

# Chapter 4 {.heading_78-0}

4:1 And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,

2 Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.

3 And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.

4 And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.

5 And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

6 And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.

7 If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.

8 And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

9 And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:

10 For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:

11 And in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.

12 And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

13 And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.

This is a great portion of Scripture on the temptation of Christ. The order of the temptation here is different from that given in []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_130}Matthew 4:1--11 because Matthew is given in a logical order of importance. Luke's account is laid out in the theological order of 1 John 2:16---["the lust of the flesh"]{.class_4723} (vss. 3--4), ["the lust of the eyes"]{.class_4723} (vss. 5--8), and ["the pride of life"]{.class_4723} (vss. 9--12).

Verse 1: ["And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost."]{.class_4723} Now John the Baptist was ["filled with the Holy Ghost\...from his mother's womb"]{.class_4723} (1:15). The deacons of the early church were chosen because they were ["full of the Holy Ghost"]{.class_4723} (Acts 6:3), and Stephen was said to be ["a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost"]{.class_4723} (Acts 6:5). Christians are commanded to ["be filled with the Spirit"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 5:18). But there is a difference between how John, Stephen, and Christians are filled with the Holy Ghost and how Jesus Christ was filled with the Holy Ghost. Because Jesus was the sinless Son of God, He had the Holy Spirit in a manner which no sinner could (see our comments on Heb. 1:9 in that Commentary). John the Baptist said, ["God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him"]{.class_4723} (John 3:34).

God will measure out the Holy Spirit to you based on your faith and obedience, but not to Jesus Christ. That's because Jesus is the Holy Ghost incarnated in the flesh. Jesus Christ is ["the fulness of the Godhead bodily"]{.class_4723} (Col. 2:9). The Holy Spirit is ["the Spirit of Christ"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 8:9; 1 Pet. 1:11). Second Corinthians 3:17 says, ["Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty"]{.class_4723}; and if the [Holy Spirit]{.class_4725} lives in you as a believer, it is [Jesus Christ]{.class_4725} living in you (Rom. 8:9 cf. Col. 1:27).

["And was led by the Spirit into the wilderness."]{.class_4723} Now the text doesn't outright say where He went, but undoubtedly it was Mt. Sinai. Why do I say that? Because the only other men to go forty days fasting in the wilderness were Moses and Elijah, and both of them end up on Mt. Sinai (Exod. 34:28--29; 1 Kings 19:7--9). The reason that is so significant is because Moses and Elijah show up with Christ on the Mount of Transfiguration; it is Moses and Elijah that precede the Second Advent (Mal. 4; Rev. 11). Christ is baptized at Bethabara on the east side of Jordan (John 1:28); that's where Elijah is caught up to Heaven (2 Kings 2) and where Moses is buried and then taken up to Heaven (Deut. 34 cf. Jude 9). So ["the wilderness"]{.class_4723} to which the Holy Spirit leads Jesus is without a doubt Sinai.

Notice in verse 2 that Jesus goes through more temptations than merely the three that are given in the text: ["Being forty days tempted of the devil."]{.class_4723} The whole time Jesus is fasting, the Devil is tempting Him.

The question arises: "Why would the Spirit lead Jesus into the wilderness to be tempted of Satan, especially after Jesus commanded His disciples to pray, ['And lead us not into temptation']{.class_4723} (Matt. 6:13)?" There are two reasons, and they are both found in the book of Hebrews.

First, Jesus had to overcome temptation to fulfill His office as our High Priest. ["For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 4:15). As a man, Jesus Christ went through the same kinds of temptations through which you go and overcame them. That's why He was able to make the perfect sacrifice for your sins.

Second, He was tempted so He could help you overcome your temptations. ["For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 2:18). He can get you through it because He went through it and knows what it's like.

Now as I said in my prefatory remarks, the temptations here in Luke are given in the same order you find them in 1 John 2:16. Someone asked me in an open Bible forum once, "If Christ was tempted like we are, how can He understand the temptations of a married man if He was never married?" The answer is that He faced the same [types]{.class_4725} of temptations that you and I face without experiencing every possible temptation that might arise in any given circumstance. Hebrews 4:15 says, ["all POINTS,"]{.class_4723} meaning the major points of temptation as you find them given by John in his epistle.

Those are the same temptations Eve faced in the Garden of Eden. ["And when the woman saw that the tree was good for FOOD ]{.class_4723}\[["lust of the flesh"]{.class_4723}\][, and that it was pleasant to the EYES]{.class_4723} \[["lust of the eyes"]{.class_4723}\][, and a tree to be desired to make one WISE]{.class_4723} \[["pride of life"]{.class_4723}\][, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat"]{.class_4723} (Gen. 3:6). So what Jesus is facing are the three basic []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_132}temptations every man, woman, and child on this earth face, regardless of their class, status, or situation.

Temptation 1---["If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread"]{.class_4723} (vs. 3). See Satan try to raise a question in Christ's mind about His identity? The first thing the Devil ever did in the Scriptures was to [cast doubt on what God said:]{.class_4725}[ ]{.class_4737}["Yea, hath God said\...?"]{.class_4723} (Gen. 3:1) Over in the book of Job, the Devil opens his mouth and raises a question as to Job's integrity: ["Doth Job fear God for nought?"]{.class_4723} (Job 1:9).

A good rule in theology is never build your doctrine on a verse about which there is some serious question or doubt. For example, Acts 2:38---why would you base your belief on a verse where there are no Christians present and where the only Gentiles are Jewish proselytes? Why would you base New Testament theology for the local church on someone going to a Jewish Temple to pray (Acts 3:1)? Why would you dwell on a verse that is never repeated again in the Acts or Epistles? You see all the problems involved?

Here's another good one: Matthew 16:18. Why would you build your church on someone whom Jesus called ["Satan"]{.class_4723} five verses later (Matt. 16:23)? Why would you brag about ["the gates of hell"]{.class_4723} not prevailing against your church? What's the matter? Are you trying to bust in? You see how unclear that is?

Now if you want a verse about which there is no doubt, here's a beauty: ["He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life"]{.class_4723} (1 John 5:12). You see how simple that verse is? Every single word in it is a one-syllable word. You couldn't miss it if you couldn't hit the broad side of a barn with a bunch of bananas. Do you have the Son? Then you have life. Don't go running to some verse where you can fall from grace and have to endure to the end and might lose it; just park right there in 1 John 5:12. [Do you have the Son?]{.class_4725}

Now practically speaking, the first temptation is: [do it yourself.]{.class_4725} Don't ask God to help you; do it yourself. Frank Sinatra used to sing: "I did it [my]{.class_4725} way." Yeah, you sure did, bub, and you made a mess of things. "Old Blue Eyes" used to end up at parties with a drink in his hand just staring into space. He had the money, he had the booze, he had the women, he had the fame, [and he had nothing.]{.class_4725} The problem []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_133}with a "self-made man" is the material he has to work with and the fellow doing the work: both are "low grade."

Never do anything without including God in it. The Germans say, ["An Gottes segen ist alles gelegen"]{.class_4725}; that means, "Everything depends on [God's blessing.]{.class_4725}" Amen, it sure does. If you don't have God's blessing on what you want to do, you're shot; you're toast.

The first temptation is [Arminianism: works]{.class_4725} salvation. Do it yourself! Keep the Ten Commandments, follow the Golden Rule, live by the Sermon on the Mount, get baptized, join the church Christ founded, observe the sacraments. [You'll go to Hell]{.class_4725} (Eph. 2:8--9; Tit. 3:5)!

Now that's the practical view of the first temptation, but look at that thing from a doctrinal, prophetic viewpoint. There's Christ in the wilderness with nothing to eat, and the Devil tells Him to make food through a miracle. Why, that's what He did when He fed the 5,000; in fact, that's one of the few miracles recorded in all four Gospels (Matt. 14:13--21; Mark 6:31--44; Luke 9:10--17; John 6:1--13). Do you know what that's a picture of? Well, the Jews connected it to Moses giving the children of Israel manna in the wilderness (John 6:31). According to Micah and John, that's what's going to happen in the Tribulation to the Jewish remnant who won't bow to the Antichrist or his image.

["Feed thy people with thy rod, the flock of thine heritage, which dwell solitarily in the wood, in the midst of Carmel: let them feed in Basham and Gilead, as in the days of old. According to the days of thy coming out of the land of Egypt will I shew unto him marvellous things"]{.class_4723} (Mic. 7:14--15; see also our comments on the passage in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets,]{.class_4725} Vol. I).

Revelation 12:6 says, ["And the woman ]{.class_4723}\[Israel---see our comments on Rev. 12:1 in that Commentary\][ fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred and threescore days ]{.class_4723}\[the last 3½ years of the Tribulation---Rev. 11:3, 13:5\][."]{.class_4723}

Revelation 12:14---["And to the woman were given two wings of a great eagle, that she might fly into the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_134}wilderness, into her place, where she is nourished for a time]{.class_4723} \[a year\][, and times]{.class_4723} \[plural---two years\][, and half a time]{.class_4723} \[a half year, making 3½ years\][, from the face of the serpent."]{.class_4723}

So the Devil is not just trying to get Christ to do something wrong; he's trying to get Him [to fulfill Scripture,]{.class_4725} but at the [wrong time.]{.class_4725} You talk about subtle, boy; the Devil is more subtle than anyone you know. He'll get you to read your Bible when you should be witnessing or witness when you should be reading and studying your Bible. If he can't shove you one way, he'll shove you another. He'll get you to read your Bible when you should be doing your English or Greek homework. I mean, [duties never conflict.]{.class_4725} The Devil will tempt you to do something you ought to do at the wrong time.

The Devil is tempting Jesus to bring about the Second Advent without regard to the Father's timing. That means the Devil knows that Book better than any Ph.D. or College professor you know, for Satan is pulling out things on Christ's Second Coming that 99% of the scholars have never gotten in 2,000 years of church history. If you ever wondered why there are so many things from Christianity in pagan religions before Jesus ever showed up, that's why. Satan knows that Book even if you don't, and he counterfeits it so when the real thing shows up, you won't believe it and you damn your soul.

Now look at how Jesus deals with Satan: ["It is written"]{.class_4723} (vs. 4). Jesus quotes Scripture (Deut. 8:3). He pulls the old twoedged sword of the Spirit on him (Heb. 4:12; Eph. 6:17). Christian, you may not be called to preach or be a Bible teacher, but you have got to learn enough of that Book to use it as a weapon when the Devil goes to work on you. David said, ["Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 119:11).

The Devil wants to take away your weapon before you have a chance to use it, so do you know what the new "Bibles" do? [They remove the last six words of verse 4:]{.class_4725} ["but by every word of God."]{.class_4723} The translators and revisers don't want you having ["every word of God,"]{.class_4723} so they get rid of them. The [NIV]{.class_4725} gets rid of 64,000 of them, and the [NASV]{.class_4725} gets rid of 36,000 of them.

["Man shall not live by bread alone, but by EVERY WORD of God."]{.class_4723} Job said, ["I have esteemed the WORDS of his mouth more than my necessary food"]{.class_4723} (Job 23:12). Solomon wrote, ["EVERY WORD of God is pure"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_135} (Prov. 30:5). It's not the thoughts, ideas, principles, message, or fundamentals of "things" (see the [RSV]{.class_4725} reading in Deut. 8:3); it's the W-O-R-D-S. Jesus said, ["the WORDS that I speak unto you, THEY are spirit, and THEY are life"]{.class_4723} (John 6:63). It's not just the word (or Word) of God singular; it's those plural [words.]{.class_4725} Jesus said, ["He that is of God heareth God's W-O-R-D-S]{.class_4723} \[PLURAL\][" ]{.class_4723}(John 8:47).

Temptation 2---["And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time. And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it. If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine"]{.class_4723} (vss. 5-7).

The man responsible for removing ["every word of God"]{.class_4723} from verse 4 (Origen) wrote in one of his commentaries on verse 5: "Of course, this is not to be taken literally because nobody can see all the kingdoms of the world ['in a moment of time.']{.class_4723}" That is rather a stupid thing to say since the ["moment of time"]{.class_4723} is left undefined; it is kind of like a skeptic saying Noah's ark couldn't hold all those animals when he doesn't know how big the ark was or how many animals went aboard.

The fact is that with TV today, you can do exactly what the Bible says Satan did for Jesus. Those commercials change the image every two seconds. You could review every nation in the UN at that rate in a little over an hour. The Lord showed Nebuchadnezzar all the kingdoms of the world that meant anything in Biblical prophecy in a dream in Daniel 2 and again with Daniel in Daniel 7, and it doesn't take more than twenty minutes for even a slow reader to read both chapters completely through.

Some of the brethren object to Satan's claim in verse 6 that all the power and glory of the nations of this world are committed to Satan and are dispensed by him. They base their objections on verses like Daniel 4:17, 25 and Matthew 28:18. Of course, Matthew 28:18 is spoken by Christ after His glorification and exaltation, but even now, it should be obvious to anyone that Jesus Christ is not now exercising the power He will have over the earth during His Millennial reign (see Psa. 2:9; Rev. 2:27, 12:5, 19:15). But even with []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_136}the Lord reigning as King of Heaven over the kingdoms of men as He says in Daniel 4:17, 25, that does not exclude Satan's rule. Notice that Christ never contradicts Satan's claim.

As we saw back in our remarks on 2:1 here in Luke, God and the Devil often work together toward different ends. Moreover, Paul makes it clear that Satan is ["the god of this world"]{.class_4723} (2 Cor. 4:4). John says, ["the whole world lieth in wickedness"]{.class_4723} (1 John 5:19), and the context of ["wickedness"]{.class_4723} is ["that wicked one"]{.class_4723} (1 John 5:18). Because of Adam's sin, Satan has power designated to him by God over death (Heb. 2:14) and over this sinful world system. Satan sets up his own rulers over the nations by the permission of God just as he got permission to persecute Job in Job 1--2.

One of the founders of this country said, "Those who refuse to be ruled by God will be ruled by [tyrants."]{.class_4725} So Germany dumps Luther for rationalism, and God lets Satan give them Hitler. England doesn't want to be ruled by the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible, so the Lord lets the Devil set up Winston Churchill who signs the "White Papers" in 1921, giving the inheritances of Reuben, Gad, and Manasseh to Transjordan. God dumps the British Empire and busts the whole thing up to where England is a third-rate country. America wants queers, so God lets the Devil give us Obama. Now you've got the faggots in your military and schools, and now the churches are forced to unite them in whatever they are trying to be. You've got a Socialist-Marxist-Communist set-up in your hospitals, and the Moslems are taking over cities and enforcing Sharia law. It's like the Jews asking for Caesar to be their King, so God gives it to them (John 19:15).

Satan is offering Christ what He will get in Revelation 11:15 at His return, except He can get it 2,000+ years ahead of time by worshipping the Devil. The temptation here (from a practical standpoint) is if you won't do it yourself, [let Satan do it for you]{.class_4725}. The problem is, the Devil never makes good on his promises. You take Charlemagne, he got a good chunk of this world, but he didn't get it all. Ghenghis Khan got a pretty sizeable portion, but he didn't get it all.

When Napoleon conquered Egypt, he went into the Great Pyramid all by himself and spent some time in there. When he came []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_137}out, he was in a sweat, and that place is like a cave with a constant temperature in the 60s or 70s. Napoleon went back to Europe and invaded Russia in the Winter and lost his shirt. Years later when he was in exile, one of his aids who had been with Napoleon in Egypt asked him what went on in the pyramid. Napoleon got this far-off look in his eye and said, "I can't tell you; you wouldn't understand anyway."

When Hitler invaded France, he went into the room where Napoleon's chair was kept. He spent an hour in that room all by himself sitting in that chair, and when he came out, he ended up invading Russia just like Napoleon, his army got stuck there in the Winter just like Napoleon's, and Hitler "lost his shirt" there just like Napoleon.

There is something about getting up there where "the peaks of worldly ambition are covered with a perpetual snow," where you make contact with ["the rulers of the darkness of this world"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 6:12). I'm not saying that people like Clinton, Bush, and Obama have to make a deal with the Devil to become President; but you won't become a President, Prime Minister, Premier, or a [Pope]{.class_4725} (!) without his consent. I don't want to have anything to do with it, boy; you can [count me out!]{.class_4725}

I have often thought about Satan's offer to Christ. One of these days at the White Throne Judgment, when every knee will bow and every tongue confess that Jesus is Lord, the Lord's going to call the Devil in there (see Phil. 2:10--11 and note those ["things UNDER the earth"]{.class_4723}; that's [Hell]{.class_4725}). In my mind's eye, I see the Lord saying to Satan: "Front and center, buddy. You remember that deal you offered me back there in Luke 4? Well, I got it now, and I got it [without you.]{.class_4725} So down, boy! Sit, roll over, play dead! Now off you go into the Lake of Fire." Of course, that's Ruckman's "living translation," but that's how she's going to go.

Again, Jesus whips out ["the sword of the Spirit"]{.class_4723} and BLAM!---["Get thee behind me, Satan: for IT IS WRITTEN, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve"]{.class_4723} (vs. 8 cf. Deut. 6:13, 10:20). From the beginning, Satan's goal has been to ["be like the most High"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 14:14), so the Devil wants the worship that belongs to God alone.

Temptation 3---[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_138}["And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence"]{.class_4723} (vs. 9). One should not miss the Second Advent cross-reference in Malachi 3:1.

["Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me\...."]{.class_4723} There's John the Baptist at the First Advent (Mark 1:2), but look at the next part of the verse: ["\...and the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class_4723} What's that? It's the Second Advent (Mal. 3:2).

You talk about subtle, boy: that's the Devil trying to get Christ to do exactly what Daniel said He would do in Daniel 2:34 (cf. Matt. 21:24). And this time, Satan even quotes Scripture to back up what he says.

["For it is written ]{.class_4723}\[Christ has been slapping down the Devil by quoting Scripture, so the Devil's going to quote it right back at Him\][, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee: And in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone"]{.class_4723} (vss. 10--11).

Satan will quote Scripture; sure he will! He knows the Bible better than any Greek or Hebrew scholar. Every false doctrine in the world is based on some Scripture, and all heretics appeal to the Scriptures to support their heresies. Of course, to create the false doctrine and heresy from the Scriptures, you have to do one of three things:

1\) [Add]{.class_4725} to what the Scripture says.

2\) [Subtract]{.class_4725} from what the Scripture says.

3\) Take the Scripture [out of context.]{.class_4725}

Here, the Devil opts for number 2: [subtract]{.class_4725} from the Scripture. Satan quotes Psalm 91:11--12, and you will note that he [leaves out]{.class_4725} ["in all thy ways"]{.class_4723} from verse 11 of that passage. Subtracting from what God said was the first sin ever committed on this earth (see how Eve leaves out the word ["freely"]{.class_4723} from what God said in Gen. 2:16---see Gen. 3:2 and comments in that Commentary). [Omission]{.class_4725} is the hallmark of Satanic New Age versions of the Bible (see our comments on Psa. 91:11 in Vol. I of that Commentary).
