Now if the first temptation was Arminianism (do it yourself), this temptation is [Calvinism]{.class_4725}---[let God do the whole thing for you.]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_139} If you won't do it yourself and you won't let the Devil do it for you, then let God do the whole thing while you sit back on your dead can and do nothing. Don't receive Christ as you are commanded to do in John 1:12; just wait till God elects you through irresistible grace against your will---that's Calvinism.

Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "God won't do anything for a man that he can do for himself." God knew you couldn't save yourself, so He made the payment for your sins for you. But there's something you have to do in salvation, and that's [believe]{.class_4725} (Acts 16:31) and [receive]{.class_4725} (John 1:12). God's not going to [make]{.class_4725} you or [force]{.class_4725} you to do either one of those things. That's [your]{.class_4725} part in salvation.

One time, two guys were out on a lake in a rowboat when one of those big Summer thunderstorms came up. One guy said, "Uh oh, there's a storm coming; we'd better pray." The other guy said, "OK, you pray and I'll row." It takes both, see. You do your part, and God does His part---AND YOU DO HAVE A PART TO DO.

Jesus doesn't flinch a bit when Satan pulls the Scriptures on Him; He quotes an appropriate text right back at him: ["It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God"]{.class_4723} (cf. Deut. 6:16). In other words, don't intentionally do something stupid and expect God to bail you out. A lot of young people sow their wild oats and spend the rest of their lives praying for a crop failure. The rule is: ["Whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap"]{.class_4723} (Gal. 6:7). If you are stupid enough to run a red light, don't be surprised if God lets someone "T-bone" you.

Note in verse 13 that Satan's cessation of the temptation is only [temporary:]{.class_4725} the Devil leaves Christ ["for a season."]{.class_4723} He returns later on in the person of the Pharisees and the Sadducees (10:25, 20:23). Christ's complete victory over Satan doesn't take place until the Crucifixion and Resurrection (Col. 2:14--15).

14 And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.

15 And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.

16 And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.

17 And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,

18 The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,

19 To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.

20 And he closed the book, and he gave it again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.

21 And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.

Jesus begins His ministry in verses 14--15. The reason His ["fame"]{.class_4723} spreads throughout Galilee is that He's healing people right and left until He had the whole populace of Israel on both sides of Jordan coming out to see Him (Matt. 4:23--25). He's not setting up a tent and hiring folks to thrown down their crutches to work up a crowd and get a big offering. He goes into a synagogue, and if there is anyone there sick or full of the devil, He touches them, and they are immediately healed (Mark 1:23--31). The sick folk and the demon-possessed didn't have to have faith, and they didn't have to pray. In fact, in Mark, when He visits His hometown, He marvels at ["their unbelief,"]{.class_4723} yet He still heals ["a few sick folk"]{.class_4723} (Mark 6:5--6).

If these nutty Charismatics actually had the New Testament signs and wonders, they would be able to go into a hospital and empty out every bed in less than 24 hours. Don't you know that would get the attention of CBS, NBC, and ABC? These faith healers wouldn't have to take up one offering for the meeting, another for the song books, another for the evangelist, and another to "plant your seed faith." All they'd have to do is hit some of those retirement homes in St. Pete and Tampa, and they'd have a bunch of rich retired folks gladly []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_141}giving them their money for ending the drugs, the doctor visits, the surgeries, and the rehabilitation.

["And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read"]{.class_4723} (vs. 16). Congregational participation was the custom of the synagogues of that day, and Paul took advantage of that on several occasions to preach the Gospel to the Jews (Acts 13:14--15, 17:2, 18:4, 19:8).

["And there was delivered unto him the book of Esaias. And when he had opened THE BOOK, he found the place where it was written"]{.class_4723} (vs. 17). [Ho Biblios]{.class_4725}---the Book. Verse 21 calls it ["scripture."]{.class_4723} The reason that is so important is because what Jesus read was [not]{.class_4725} the original autograph of the book of Isaiah, yet He called it ["scripture."]{.class_4723}

The Apostle Paul wrote, ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration of God" ]{.class_4723}(2 Tim. 3:16). If you go to any Conservative or Fundamental College, University, or Seminary anywhere in the United States, you will be told that the only Scriptures that were ever inspired are the [original autographs.]{.class_4727} Right in the context of what Paul said about the inspiration of the Scriptures, he says to Timothy, ["And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:15). Well, do you think Timothy had the originals of Genesis, Exodus, Judges, Job, Psalms, Isaiah, Jeremiah, Malachi, etc.? Of course not! Yet, what he had was ["holy scriptures,"]{.class_4723} and ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration."]{.class_4723}

The Ethiopian eunuch read the Scripture (Acts 8:32). The Bereans ["searched the scriptures"]{.class_4723} (Acts 17:11). The words ["scripture"]{.class_4723} or ["scriptures"]{.class_4723} occur 55 times in the Bible; and [not once]{.class_4725} does either word mean what the staffs and faculties of Pensacola Christian, Bob Jones, Tennessee Temple, Crown, Moody, Northwestern, Midsouth, Dallas, Santa Rosa, etc., say it means.

People, you are living in a day and age when the apostasy isn't just in the Liberals and Modernists; it's in the [Body of Christ.]{.class_4725} The people who deny the Bible now are [saved ]{.class_4725}people connected with [higher Christian education. ]{.class_4725}I ought to know; I'm one of their boys. I graduated from Bob Jones University with only one C in six years. I hold earned Bachelor's, Master's, and Doctor's degrees. They just []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_142}don't love me anymore because I didn't turn out like them. I should have come out believing only the originals were inspired and correcting the Book. Instead, I came out believing that [King James]{.class_4725} Bible and calling them a bunch of dirty, lying scoundrels because they don't, even though they say they do.

They will get up behind a pulpit and wave a [King James]{.class_4725} Bible in the air and say, "This Book doesn't [contain]{.class_4725} the word of God; it [is]{.class_4725} the word of God. The Bible is the [infallible]{.class_4725} word of God." Then when you get them out of the pulpit and press them on the subject, they will tell you only the originals were inspired and show you mistake after mistake just like that. They didn't mean a thing they said; they were just lying through their teeth.

For fifty years I put out a challenge to those apostates who [used]{.class_4725} to believe the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible was the word of God and now believe that only the originals were inspired. The challenge was this: if any of them could recommend a copy of the [Holy Scriptures]{.class_4725} that you could get, read, and hold in your hands (not a pile of lost papers no one has ever seen in the past 1,900 years), I would put the guy's name on the front page of the [Bulletin]{.class_4725} and send him a certified check for 500 bucks. Do you know how many "takers" I had in fifty years? [Not one.]{.class_4725}

I got a letter from a fourteen-year-old boy one time who wrote Shelton Smith, the present editor of [The Sword of the Lord,]{.class_4725} and asked him that. Do you know what Shelton Smith wrote back to that kid? He sent a letter saying the [King James]{.class_4725} is "the Book" and "the best Book," [without one word]{.class_4725} about it being the [Holy Scriptures.]{.class_4725}

Shelton Smith, Curtis Hutson, and John R. Rice were three gutless wonders. Not one of them would take a clear-cut stand on the [Scriptures.]{.class_4725} They didn't take it then, and Shelton Smith won't take it now. If you don't believe me, just write him and ask him. Do you know why they won't dare call the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible "Holy Scripture"? Because the moment they do, they have to admit it is ["given by inspiration of God"]{.class_4723} because ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration of God."]{.class_4723} They will say anything BUT the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible is the Holy Scriptures; they all back off at the last minute.

Question: do you have a copy of the Holy Scriptures? You either [do]{.class_4725} or you [don't.]{.class_4725} If you believe only the originals were inspired, [you don't.]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_143} These birds who say "the Bible is the inspired word of God," when what they really mean is "the [originals were]{.class_4725} the inspired word of God," are nothing but a pack of liars. Do you know why? Because there has never been a single time since 1500 B.C. when all the originals were gathered together in one book. When Moses wrote the Pentateuch, he didn't have the original of Job. When David wrote the Psalms, he didn't have Moses' originals. When Isaiah and Jeremiah wrote their prophecies, they didn't have the original Psalms. The word [Bible]{.class_4725} means "book."

Now I believe the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible is the [Holy Scriptures in English.]{.class_4725} I am not saying that the Holy Scriptures aren't in other languages. God is not "tongue-tied"; He knows how folks of all nations speak (see Acts 2:8--11). But I speak [English.]{.class_4725} Do I have the Holy Scriptures in [English?]{.class_4725} Yep, it's right here on my desk: it's the [King James Authorized Version]{.class_4725} of the Holy Bible. If that fourteen-year-old kid had written me for a copy of the Holy Scriptures, I would have sent him a [King James]{.class_4725} Gideon Bible. There's no problem answering the question in a straightforward manner unless you have no guts.

Listen, folks, it's not the ["word\...settled in heaven"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 119:89) that's the infallible word of God; it's the words written in a Book [where you can get them]{.class_4725} (vs. 17). You don't need to go up to Heaven to get it (Rom. 10:6); you don't need to go digging around in some cave or in some desert in the Middle East to get it (Rom. 10:7). It's right here where you can get it [now:]{.class_4725} ["The word is NIGH THEE, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is the word of faith, which we preach"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 10:8).

Now what Jesus reads from the book of Isaiah is chapter 61, verses 1--3.

["The Spirit of the Lord is upon me ]{.class_4723}\[you just read it in 3:22 and 4:1, 14\][, because he hath anointed me ]{.class_4723}\[the words [Messiah]{.class_4725} in Hebrew and [Christ]{.class_4725} in Greek mean "anointed one"\][ to PREACH the gospel to the poor\...to PREACH deliverance to the captives\...To PREACH the acceptable year of the Lord"]{.class_4723} (vss. 18--19). ["Preach\...preach\...preach."]{.class_4723} Like John, Jesus was a [preacher,]{.class_4725} not just a teacher.

["From that time Jesus began to PREACH"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 4:17).

["Let us go into the next towns, that I may PREACH there also"]{.class_4723} (Mark 1:38).

["I must PREACH the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent"]{.class_4723} (Luke 4:43).

Verse 19 lays down a great principle of Bible study, and you might know that it goes completely against the move of the world system to get everyone and everything together in one huge, mongrelized, integrated mass. The principle is so abhorrent that all the new "Bibles" change the verse where it is found.

["Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, RIGHTLY DIVIDING the word of truth"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 2:15).

The [NASV]{.class_4725} and [NIV]{.class_4725} get rid of the division and change it to ["accurately handling"]{.class_4725} or ["correctly handling."]{.class_4725} The [New King James]{.class_4725} may keep the ["rightly dividing"]{.class_4725} part, but it will remove the command to ["study."]{.class_4723}

The author of segregation is not any Southerner or "White Supremist"; it's [God Almighty.]{.class_4725}

["When the most High DIVIDED to the nations their inheritance, when he SEPARATED the sons of Adam"]{.class_4723} (Deut. 32:8).

["Suppose ye that I am come to give peace on earth? I tell you, Nay, but rather DIVISION"]{.class_4723} (Luke 12:51).

["Fences]{.class_4725} make good neighbors," not "building bridges." The Lord has proper [divisions.]{.class_4725} He has proper divisions for [nations,]{.class_4725} He has proper divisions [spiritually, ]{.class_4725}and He has proper divisions for [His Book.]{.class_4725} I'll give you some examples.

1\) Never confuse [discipleship]{.class_4725} with [salvation.]{.class_4725} The way to confuse a congregation is to tell them: "If Jesus is not Lord of all, He's not Lord at all." That's the way to get "retreads." You browbeat a bunch of Christians with: "If you don't attend church Sunday morning and Sunday night and come out to prayer meeting on Wednesday night, and if you don't tithe or drive a bus or teach Sunday School, etc., then Christ is not Lord of your life completely, and you can't possibly be saved."

That's [bunk!]{.class_4725} You don't get saved by letting Jesus Christ take complete control of your life; that's [discipleship.]{.class_4725} You get saved by [trusting Jesus Christ alone]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_145} for salvation. Get the difference; they're not the same. Discipleship is [works;]{.class_4725} salvation [isn't.]{.class_4725}

2\) Never confuse the [Old Testament]{.class_4725} with the [New Testament.]{.class_4725} They aren't the same. The New Testament isn't even in force until the Crucifixion (Heb. 9:16--17), so nearly all of the Gospels are under the Old Testament. When Jesus says He will deny you before the Father if you deny Him before men (Matt. 10:32), that's different doctrinally from what Paul says about Christ denying us if we deny Him (see our comments on 2 Tim. 2:12--13 in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the Pastoral Epistles.]{.class_4725}). Under the Old Testament Law, you couldn't eat pork, catfish, or shrimp (Lev. 11); you can in the New Testament (1 Tim. 4:4--5). In the Old Testament, the Lord set up a theocracy that practiced genocide against God's enemies (1 Sam. 15); that's not true of the Body of Christ in the New Testament (Rom. 12:18).

3\) Never confuse [Israel]{.class_4725} and the [Church.]{.class_4725} Israel is a [physical]{.class_4725} nation with [physical]{.class_4725} promises, [physical]{.class_4725} priests, and [physical]{.class_4725} sacrifices; the Church is a [spiritual]{.class_4725} nation with [spiritual]{.class_4725} promises, [spiritual]{.class_4725} priests, and [spiritual]{.class_4725} sacrifices (1 Pet. 2:5). You'd better get the difference; or you end up with holy water, vestments, rosaries, confessional booths, Masses, papal bulls, and the Vatican State.

4\) You had better get the difference between the spiritual Kingdom of God and the physical Kingdom of Heaven.

5\) You have got to get the difference between Christ's coming to catch out believers at the Rapture and His return in power at the Advent.

You see, brethren, those are [doctrinal]{.class_4725} things that you only get by [study,]{.class_4725} and people don't like to study. They're too busy with the job, the bills, the wife, the kids, taxes, etc. Who's got the time to [study what God said in His Book? ]{.class_4725}You had better make the time; it's the words in that Book that will judge you someday (John 12:48). You had better "bone up" for your "final."

Now the divisions in that Book go far beyond just doctrinal topics. They even go beyond individual books. The Hyperdispensationalists relegate Christian doctrine to the Pauline Epistles (Romans--Philemon) to get rid of water baptism for the believer; some are more particular than that and say that only Paul's []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_146}"prison epistles" apply to the New Testament believer in the Body of Christ. But you can find New Testament doctrine in Genesis 2 and the Song of Solomon (cf. Eph. 5:22--32).

["Rightly dividing the word of truth"]{.class_4723} can sometimes go to verses right next to each other (see our remarks on Isa. 40:1--5 under 1:17); sometimes you have to rightly divide phrases and clauses within a verse at a comma or semicolon, which is the case here in Luke 4. Look at Isaiah 61:1--2.

["The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me]{.class_4723} \[First Advent\][; because the LORD hath anointed me]{.class_4723} \[First Advent\][ to preach good tidings unto the meek ]{.class_4723}\[First Advent\][; he hath sent me to bind up the broken hearted ]{.class_4723}\[First Advent\][, to proclaim liberty to the captives ]{.class_4723}\[First Advent\][, and the opening of the prison to them that are bound ]{.class_4723}\[First Advent\][; To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD\...."]{.class_4723}

And that's where Jesus stops. Why? Look at the rest of Isaiah 61:2. ["\...and THE DAY OF VENGEANCE OF OUR GOD."]{.class_4723} That's the Second Advent. Christ didn't come the first time to take vengeance on anybody. The split occurred right at the comma in verse 2; that comma represented 2,000+ years.

Want to see it again? Look at Genesis 49:10--11.

["The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor a lawgiver from between his feet, until Shiloh come; and unto him shall the gathering of the people be."]{.class_4723} There's the Millennial reign of Christ at the Second Advent. But keep on reading.

["Binding his foal unto the vine, and his ass's colt unto the choice vine\...."]{.class_4723} Why, that's the Triumphal Entry at Christ's First Advent (Matt. 21:4--11). Look at the rest of the verse though.

["\...he washed his garments in wine, and his clothes in the blood of grapes."]{.class_4723} That's Jesus Christ returning at the Second Advent and staining His garments with blood by stomping on 200 million UN troops (Isa. 63:1--4; Rev. 14:18--20, 19:13). The Holy Spirit went from the Second Advent in Genesis 49:10 to the First Advent at the beginning of Genesis 49:11, and then back to the Second Advent at the semicolon in that same verse.

One more time with feeling. Same chapter, verse 24.

["But his bow abode in strength, and the arms of his []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_147}hands were made strong by the hands of the mighty God of Jacob; (from thence is the SHEPHERD]{.class_4723} \[that's Jesus Christ as the Good Shepherd at His First Advent in John 10:11\][, the STONE of Israel ]{.class_4723}\[and there's Jesus Christ as the Smiting Stone of Daniel 2:34, 45 at His Second Advent\][:)"]{.class_4723} (Gen. 49:24).

That time both Advents were separated by only a comma. That happens over and over again throughout the Old Testament.

Verse 21: ["This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears."]{.class_4723} Jesus stopped reading right at the part that was being fulfilled right then. So Luke 4:17--21 is the textbook case in the Scriptures of ["rightly dividing"]{.class_4723}[ ]{.class_4737}the Book.

Note again, that what Jesus was reading is called ["scripture,"]{.class_4723} and it's [not the originals.]{.class_4725} How do you know that? Because He's reading the book of Isaiah, and over in Acts 8:32, the Ethiopian eunuch is reading the same book, and it's called ["scripture"]{.class_4723} there too. Did the eunuch steal the scroll from the synagogue at Nazareth?!

22 And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph's son?

23 And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.

24 And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.

25 But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;

26 But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow.

27 And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.

28 And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,

29 And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_148}him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.

30 But he passing through the midst of them went his way,

Remember, this is Jesus' hometown; this is where He grew up. Up to this point, neither He nor Mary has given any indication to anyone that He's [God's Son.]{.class_4725} That's why back when Luke gave Christ's genealogy, he wrote: ["being (as was SUPPOSED) the son of Joseph."]{.class_4723} He wasn't Joseph's son, but everybody thought He was. That's why when He claimed to be the fulfillment of Isaiah 61:1--2, the book of Mark records: ["And they were offended at him"]{.class_4723} (Mark 6:5). They thought they knew Him and His family, and they believed He was insulting their intelligence with such an outlandish claim as being the Messiah.

["And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country"]{.class_4723} (vs. 24). The companion passage in Mark says, ["A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house"]{.class_4723} (Mark 6:4). That is, unless you are Billy Graham; then you are ["A Prophet WITH Honor."]{.class_4725} That's the title of Billy Graham's biography. The fellow who wrote that book about Billy Graham just claimed Jesus Christ lied like a dog. He figured that even though Elijah, Micaiah, Amos, Jeremiah, Paul, and JESUS CHRIST were without honor in their own countries, that Billy Graham was the [exception to the rule.]{.class_4725} Folks, you are now in apostasy up to your neck, and like I said earlier, it's not the apostasy of the Liberals and the Modernists. It's apostasy among saved, Evangelical, Conservative, Fundamental Christians. The fellow who wrote that book was more than likely just as saved as I am.

The old saying is: "familiarity breeds contempt." Those Jews in Nazareth are thinking, "We've heard what you've been doing and saying all over the country. If you're such a big boy over in Capernaum, why don't you do the same thing here in Nazareth. You won't do it because we know you. We know your momma, your daddy, and your brothers and sisters. Who you trying to kid?!"

Then Jesus gives two examples from the Old Testament of []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_149}prophets who were rejected in their own country, and lo and behold, both of them end up dealing with [Gentiles]{.class_4725} after being rejected by the Jews. [Elijah]{.class_4725} (["Elias"]{.class_4723}) deals with the widow of Zarephath, a [Zidonian]{.class_4725} (vss. 25--26), and [Elisha]{.class_4725} (["Eliseus"]{.class_4723}) deals with Naaman the [Syrian]{.class_4725} (vs. 27). Jesus is warning those Jews that if they reject Him like those Old Testament Jews rejected Elijah and Elisha, that God will bypass them in order to minister to the Gentiles like the Lord did with Elijah and Elisha.

Now look at the reaction of the Jews to what Jesus said:[ "And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things ]{.class_4723}\[what things? What Jesus said about Elijah and Elisha ministering to the Gentiles---vss. 25--27\][, were filled with WRATH"]{.class_4723} (vs. 28). When Jesus implies that God will bypass His chosen people to deal with Gentiles, all the Orthodox Jews in that synagogue "hit the roof," boy.

That's what happens to Paul in Acts 22. He's outside the Temple giving his testimony, and the crowd is standing there listening to him. Then in Acts 22:21, he says one magic word, ["Gentile,"]{.class_4723} and when he says that one magic word, the crowd goes to pieces. They start tearing their clothes, throwing dust in the air, and yelling and screaming, "KILL HIM!" (Acts 22:22--23).

If you want to see the "brethren" go spastic, just pull one word on them. One word---that's all it takes. Just say the word ["Ruckman"]{.class_4725}[ ]{.class_4737}and watch them throw a "hissy fit" like a two-year-old child.

["And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong"]{.class_4723} (vs. 29). Now this is not Jerusalem, it's Nazareth; but like Jerusalem, Nazareth was built on a hill. Jerusalem was built on Mt. Zion, and to the south of the city is a valley called Hinnom. That is where the city dump was; it's ["gehenna"]{.class_4725}---a picture of Hell. That's where Judas fell after he hung himself (Acts 1:18--19). So the Jews are carrying Jesus out of the city to throw Him in the city dump. The common practice of stoning in those days was to put someone in a valley or pit and cast rocks the size of bowling balls on him (see Josh. 7:24--26). So the Jews in Nazareth are preparing to stone their "native son."

["But he passing through the midst of them went his way"]{.class_4723} (vs. 30). This was a supernatural deliverance by God just as []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_150}you find it in John 7:30, 8:20. The time, place, and purpose of Jesus' death were set by the Father, and until then, nobody could touch Him (John 7:6).

31 And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.

32 And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.

33 And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,

34 Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.

35 And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.

36 And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word is this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.

37 And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.

Verse 32 should be compared with Matthew 7:28--29. Jesus was known for His ["doctrine"]{.class_4723} because the first purpose of the Scripture is ["for doctrine"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:16). Jesus taught in the ["power"]{.class_4723} of the Holy Ghost. That same power accompanied Paul in his preaching.

["And my speech and my preaching was not with enticing words of man's wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power: That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 2:4--5).

["For our gospel came not unto you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Ghost"]{.class_4723} (1 Thess. 1:5).

["Good words and fair speeches"]{.class_4723} are not the marks of Holy Ghost preaching, but of a smooth, slick [liar]{.class_4725} (Rom. 16:18). Preaching that has the power of the Holy Spirit behind it may be ["rude"]{.class_4723} (2 Cor. 11:6) and ["contemptible"]{.class_4723} (2 Cor. 10:10), but it is evident (1 Cor. []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_151}4:19--20). It burns and breaks things up (Jer. 23:29).

But not only was there power behind Jesus' teaching; there was ["authority"]{.class_4723} (Mark 1:22). That's because Jesus' authority was not the opinions of the Rabbis but the word of His Father (John 8:28, 38). People tell me all the time that they can spot the graduates of our school because they [speak with authority.]{.class_4725} That's because I teach my students that the authority [isn't me; ]{.class_4725}it[ isn't the school;]{.class_4725} it's [the Book.]{.class_4725} When a man graduates from PBI, he carries his authority with him; he doesn't have to consult the school or me about anything.

Matthew 7:29 says, ["For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes."]{.class_4723} The scribes were the ones in charge of copying the Scriptures. Today, they would be the Bible revisers, translators, and editors. The scribes who have no authority say things like: "A better translation should be\...," "This is an unfortunate rendering in the [King James]{.class_4725}\...," "Most scholars agree that it should read thus\...," "The original Greek (or Hebrew) says\....," etc. Those are the statements of someone who has no authority because his authority is [himself]{.class_4725} or the [opinions]{.class_4725} or [preferences]{.class_4725} of someone he likes. A preacher's or Bible teacher's authority should be "Thus saith the Lord," but how can he ["speak as the oracles of God"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 4:11) when all he has are 26 conflicting Greek texts, two or three conflicting Hebrew texts, 233 conflicting English versions (1880--2008), and 22 commentaries? My authority is the words God the Holy Spirit recorded in a Book on which He put His stamp of approval through a period of 400 years. It is the [standard]{.class_4725} to which all other "Bibles" and translations have to compare themselves to get a hearing. Why is that? Because [it's]{.class_4725} the authority. It's the FINAL AUTHORITY in all matters of faith and practice. [Sola Scriptura]{.class_4725}---the Scriptures alone. What are those "Scriptures"? Well, in English, they're the [King James Authorized Version]{.class_4725} of the Holy Bible, and ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration of God"]{.class_4723} (see our remarks under vs. 17).

["And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil"]{.class_4723} (vs. 33). Now the Greek word for ["devil"]{.class_4723} in that verse is [daimonion,]{.class_4725} from where we get the word [demon.]{.class_4725} But the word [demon]{.class_4725} is a [transliteration]{.class_4725} of the Greek [daimonion;]{.class_4725} it's not a []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_152}[translation.]{.class_4725}

All the new "Bibles" are afraid to translate the Greek word [hades]{.class_4725} as [Hell,]{.class_4725} so they just give you the word [Hades.]{.class_4725} The rotten punks who put out those corrupt pieces of garbage are full of the devil (and I say that with charity, of course) because when they hit the word [Heaven,]{.class_4725} they don't transliterate the word [ouranos.]{.class_4725} Well, why let a person go to Hades and then not send him to Ouranos (or Uranus); that's awfully hypocritical, don't you think? The problem is, they can't stand that fire and torment in "Hades" (see our comments on 16:23--34). "All is well; there is no Hell"---that's the message they're giving you.

The reason the [King James]{.class_4733} translates the word [daimonion]{.class_4733} as [devil]{.class_4733} instead of [demon]{.class_4733} is because devils are part of[ the]{.class_4733} Devil (see our remarks about those birds in the Parable of the Sower under 3:22). Also in Greek philosophy, a demon could be either [good or bad;]{.class_4733} if a man were smart, he got his brilliance from a [demon.]{.class_4733} The [King James]{.class_4733} lets you know that [all]{.class_4733} demons are bad by translating the word as [devil]{.class_4733} or [devils.]{.class_4733} When in doubt, correct the Greek with the English.

The Bible expression, then, is not "demon-possessed," but ["possessed with the devil"]{.class_4723} (Mark 5:16) or ["possessed of the devil"]{.class_4725} (Luke 8:36). That means not only that a man has a devil, but the devil has him as well. The standard teaching is that a Christian can't be demon-possessed because ["greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world"]{.class_4723} (1 John 4:4). But a Christian can yield the members of his body as ["instruments of unrighteousness unto sin"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 6:13) despite the fact that the Holy Spirit lives in him (Eph. 4:30). If ["no good thing"]{.class_4723} dwells in your flesh (Rom. 7:18), do you think the Devil can't get control of it? If you are a Christian, Satan may not be able to get your soul; but he can get your brain, your mind, your liver, your kidneys, your arms, your legs, your hands, your feet, your stomach, you gall bladder, your nervous system, and your excretory system (1 Cor. 5:5). He can get your health, your joy, your assurance of salvation, your testimony, your rewards at the Judgment Seat of Christ, and your life. If that isn't "possession," what is it? Brethren, your body won't be saved until the Rapture (Rom. 8:23).

Notice in verse 34 that the ["spirit of an unclean devil"]{.class_4723} (singular) is [plural]{.class_4725} (["us," "we"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_153} ). That's not some kind of "royal we" or "editorial we" as you find in literature; that's a singular spirit composed of multiple spirits as you find in Mark 5.

["And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with AN unclean spirit" ]{.class_4723}(Mark 5:2). Note the singular, but look at what the unclean spirit tells Jesus:[ "My name is Legion: for WE are MANY"]{.class_4723} (Mark 5:9).

["I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God"]{.class_4723} (vs. 34). In verse 41, the devils say, ["Thou art Christ, the Son of God."]{.class_4723} That is, demons have more sense than Mohammed (Sura 3:58, 9:30, 17:39, 2:116, 72:3, 6:102, 37:152). The main confession of Mohammedanism is that there is one God---monotheism. Well, if that's the case, Mohammed is no better than a devil.

["Thou believest that there is one God ]{.class_4723}\[that's what Mohammed said he believed\][; thou doest well: THE DEVILS ALSO BELIEVE, and tremble"]{.class_4723} (James 2:19).

Americans think that if you just believe in God, you're OK. [You'll go to Hell.]{.class_4725} Every devil in that Bible is a Fundamentalist: they all believe in God, they all believe in the Virgin Birth, they all believe in the Deity of Christ, and they all worship Jesus Christ (Mark 5:6). [And they're all headed for Hell,]{.class_4725} I might add (Matt. 8:29). Affirming the fundamentals of the faith in the Apostles' Creed every Sunday morning at eleven o'clock is not enough to get you to Heaven. You have to personally accept ["the gift of God\...through Jesus Christ"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 6:23 cf. John 1:12), and I don't mean some starchy cookie held up by a Priest.

["I know thee who thou art."]{.class_4723} Over in Acts 19:13--15, some demons told a group of exorcists: ["Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are ye?"]{.class_4723} The question is not do you know Jesus; it's does [Jesus know you]{.class_4725} (Gal. 4:9). Over in 2 Peter 2:21, there are unsaved people who knew ["the way of righteousness"]{.class_4723} and turned it down. That is, they knew intellectually and academically how to be saved and rejected God's righteousness in Jesus Christ.

Over in Matthew 7:21--23, Jesus tells some people who did works in His name and called Him ["Lord"]{.class_4723}: ["I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity."]{.class_4723} Well, isn't Jesus omniscient []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_154}(all knowing)? Doesn't He know the number of hairs on your head (12:7)? Then obviously this is a particular kind of knowledge. He means He never knew them in the sense of sons or friends or believers.

Paul said, ["The Lord knoweth them THAT ARE HIS"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 2:19).

Jesus said, ["My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish"]{.class_4723} (John 10:27--28).

Notice in verse 36 that there is a difference between ["power"]{.class_4723} and ["authority"]{.class_4723} in the Scriptures. All the apostate pieces of trash on the market that claim to be "Bibles" change John 1:12 from ["to them gave he POWER to become the sons of God" ]{.class_4723}to ["to them gave he AUTHORITY to become the sons of God."]{.class_4725} You'll even hear Bob Jones Sr. on his radio program make the change. But there is a difference between authority and power. Authority is a legal right to do a thing; power is the ability to bring the thing to pass. Listen to what Paul writes in Ephesians 3:20.

"Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us."

That's not any "authority." That's the power of the resurrected Christ (Matt. 28:18) working in you. That's the power that's going to get you bodily to the third heaven some day.

["But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 8:11).

["And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word is this!"]{.class_4723} (vs. 36) Matthew 7:28 says, ["the people were astonished at his doctrine."]{.class_4723} It was those [words]{.class_4727} that made an impression on folks. And while here it was Jesus' words over unclean spirits, in John 6:68, Peter said, ["Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the WORDS OF ETERNAL LIFE."]{.class_4723}

38 And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_155}Simon's house. And Simon's wife's mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.

39 And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.

40 Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

41 And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.

42 And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.

43 And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.

44 And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.

Verse 38 causes great consternation for the papists, for if Peter were the first Pope, then he was a Pope who was [married]{.class_4725} and had a [wife]{.class_4725} (1 Cor. 9:5). After all, a Bishop is to be ["the husband of one wife"]{.class_4723} (1 Tim. 3:2), and all Peter claimed to be was ["an elder"]{.class_4723} among other ["elders"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 5:1). As ["an elder,"]{.class_4723} he was married and took his wife with him when he went to preach somewhere. What's "the Bishop of Rome" doing as the head of a [bachelor]{.class_4725} priesthood?

["And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her"]{.class_4723} (vs. 39). Notice there that Jesus didn't have to touch the woman for her to be healed. The old-time Charismatics used to talk about a "point of contact," meaning they had to touch a person in order for the healing to be transferred. When television came along, they would tell the people at home watching the boob tube to put their hands on the television set for a point of contact. I had a buddy in the ministry who used to say, "If you want a point of contact, put your hands [in the back]{.class_4725} of the television set." You'll really feel "the power" that way.

Verse 39 is where the Charismatics get the practice of "rebuking" things. "I rebuke this sickness in the name of Jesus." "Satan, I rebuke thee." "I bind you, Satan, in the name of Jesus." He ain't rebuked and he ain't been bound. He's still walking about ["as a roaring lion\...seeking whom he may devour"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 5:8). He won't be "bound" until Revelation 20:1--3 at the beginning of the Millennium.

You see the gap between "contemporary Christianity" and that Book? The Christians aren't in their Bibles, so when some fakir comes along breathing hot air, they swallow it hook, line, and sinker.

Jesus is getting those Jews ready for His Second Advent, and He's exercising the sign gifts given to Moses in Exodus 3--4 to do it. Look at verses 40--41 here in Luke 4: He's healing the sick and casting out devils. Why? [To show them He was the Christ.]{.class_4725}

Matthew 8:17, ["That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses."]{.class_4723} That's a free quote by the Holy Spirit of Isaiah 53:4. Isaiah 53 is a Messianic prophecy on the blood atonement. So the Charismatics teach what is known as "Whipping Post Theology" (["with his stripes we are healed"]{.class_4723}---Isa. 53:5); that is, there is physical healing in the blood atonement of Jesus Christ.

But as in so many prophecies in the Old Testament, there is a [double application]{.class_4725} to Isaiah 53:4--5. One should note that the application in Matthew is strictly to [Israel.]{.class_4725} It is true that Jesus deals several times in the Gospels with Gentiles, as He did with the centurion in Matthew 8:5--10, but that does not change the fact that the ["our"]{.class_4723} in both Isaiah and Matthew is [a Jew writing to Jews.]{.class_4725}

Furthermore, you do not get all of the benefits of the atonement right now. Jesus died to redeem the whole creation (Rom. 8:19--23), but that redemption isn't accomplished until His return (Matt. 19:28; Isa. 11:4--10). [Right now,]{.class_4725} the healing that is in the atonement is a [spiritual]{.class_4725} healing that takes place at salvation.

["Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 2:24).

That time, Peter applied Isaiah's prophecy to Gentile believers in the Body of Christ (see our comments on 1 Pet 1:1 in the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_157}[Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles,]{.class_4725} Vol. I), but the application had nothing to do with physical healing. The sign gift of healing was already on the way out at the end of Paul's ministry (see 2 Tim. 4:20 and our comments on the verse in that Commentary). That doesn't mean that God won't heal you or that you shouldn't pray for healing---I have even prayed for my German Shepherd to be healed. But it does mean that until Moses and Elijah show up in the Tribulation (Rev. 11), you aren't going to see the miraculous healings like you read of in the Gospels and Acts in the ministries of Jesus and the apostles. Right now, you have divine healing, [not divine healers.]{.class_4725}

Notice in verse 41 that Jesus refuses the testimony of devils as to His identity. I have gone into the reasons for this in my comments on Acts 16:17 in that Commentary (which see).

Finally, notice the peculiarly [Jewish]{.class_4725} ministry of the Lord Jesus Christ in verses 43--44: ["And he preached in the SYNAGOGUES of Galilee"]{.class_4723} (vs. 44). He's not preaching the Kingdom of God to the Gentiles. In fact, when He sends out the twelve apostles, He specifically commands them NOT to preach to the Gentiles.

["These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 10:5--6).

Of course, there are notable exceptions such as John 4, but the exceptions [prove]{.class_4725} the rule; they don't overthrow it. As a group, the Kingdom of God was not opened to the Samaritans until Acts 8, and it was not opened to the Gentiles until Acts 10.

The chapter ends with what Isaiah 61:1--2 said Christ would do---[preach]{.class_4725} (see our remarks under vss. 18--19). ["I must PREACH the kingdom of God to other cities also"]{.class_4723} (vs. 43); ["And he PREACHED in the synagogues of Galilee"]{.class_4723} (vs. 44).

["PREACH the word; be instant in season, out of season"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 4:2). ["Go ye into all the world, and PREACH the gospel to every creature"]{.class_4723} (Mark 16:15).

Why? Because ["how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a PREACHER?"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 10:14), and because ["it pleased God by the foolishness of PREACHING to save them that believe"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ917.xhtml_page_158} (1 Cor. 1:22).

["Preach the word"]{.class_4723}; ["preach the gospel"]{.class_4723}; PREACH!

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml}

# Chapter 5 {.heading_78-0}

5:1 And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,

2 And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

3 And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon's, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.

4 Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.

5 And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.

6 And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.

7 And they beckoned unto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.

8 When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.

9 For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:

10 And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.

11 And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.

This passage covers the miracle of "the great draught of fishes." []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_160}It takes place twice in Christ's ministry: once at the beginning (here) and once at the end, after the Resurrection (John 21).

Now in reconciling the events recorded here in Luke 5 with those in John 1:40--42 and Matthew 4:18--20, what you get is a personal introduction and invitation (John 1), an initial calling (Matt. 4), and finally, a deeper, more committed call to discipleship (Luke 5). That the events of Luke 5 take place [after]{.class_4725} those in Matthew 4 is obvious, for Jesus healed Peter's mother-in-law back in Luke 4:38--39, which took place in Matthew 8:14--15.

Verse 1: ["And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret."]{.class_4723} Christ's ministry is getting off to a bang, after experiencing some opposition back in chapter 4. His congregation is so big now that He has to get into a boat to preach if He doesn't want to be pushed into the water by them.

You know ["the lake of Gennesaret"]{.class_4723} better by its other name: the Sea of Galilee. It's a lake large enough to be an inland sea like the Great Lakes here in the United States and Canada. It is also called ["the sea of Tiberias"]{.class_4723} in the Scriptures (John 21:1) and ["the sea of Chinnereth"]{.class_4723} (Josh. 13:27; also spelled ["Chinneroth"]{.class_4723} in Josh. 12:3); so it has four names.

Now there is a reason that the first disciples Christ calls are ["fishermen"]{.class_4723} (vs. 2), and it all goes back to Genesis 1. When God divided those waters by a firmament back there in Genesis 1:7, He said there were ["waters which were under the firmament"]{.class_4723} and ["waters which were ABOVE the firmament."]{.class_4723} Those ["waters\...above the firmament"]{.class_4723} aren't the clouds and water vapor in the sky. How do you know that? Because that firmament is more than just the atmosphere surrounding the earth (Gen. 1:20); it contains the sun, moon, and stars (Gen. 1:14--16). Psalm 148:4 talks about the ["heavens of heavens"]{.class_4723} and ["waters that be above the heavens."]{.class_4723} That's water out there beyond Alpha Draconis somewhere; it's not any nimbus, cumulus, or stratus clouds in the atmosphere or any "ice canopy" surrounding the earth before Noah's Flood.

Right now, this whole universe is sitting under a body of water so big that it makes the Atlantic, Pacific, Indian, and Arctic Oceans combined look like a drop in a bucket. You are [under water]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_161} like a fish, so Christ calls His disciples to be ["fishers of men"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 4:19). In Matthew 13:47--48, Christ gives a parable likening the Kingdom of Heaven to a net that catches all kinds of fish that are separated into good fish that are gathered into vessels and bad fish that are thrown away. Those fish are [men,]{.class_4725} some of whom are saved and go into the kingdom and some of whom are lost and go to Hell.

When Christ comes down from Heaven to this earth, He has to pass through that body of water, and when He returns to Heaven, He has to pass through it again. So when Christ begins His ministry to ["catch men"]{.class_4723} (vs. 10), He goes down under the water and comes back up (John's baptism). That's pictured in the Old Testament by Israel passing through the Red Sea (1 Cor. 10:1--2).

Have you ever noticed how many hymns are about you going through water to dry land? "In the sweet by and by, we shall meet on that BEAUTIFUL SHORE." "I won't have to CROSS JORDAN alone." "I've anchored my soul in the HAVEN OF REST; I'll SAIL THE WILD SEAS no more." You see that stuff? One of these days, the Lord's going to call you home if you're a believer, and you'll pass through that water with your Saviour to land safe in Glory. That means every saved Christian is a "Baptist" no matter to what denomination he belongs.

Now there are three kinds of nets (vs. 2) in the Scriptures. There is a [seine]{.class_4725} net (Gk.--[sagana,]{.class_4725} Matt. 13:47), which is the [drag]{.class_4725} net of Habakkuk 1:16 (see our comments on that verse in that Commentary). It is a large net, sometimes 400 feet long, which is held in place on one end while the other end is swept around the length of the net, bringing in whatever is caught in the sweep. Another kind of net is a [cast]{.class_4725} net (Gk.--[amphiblastron,]{.class_4725} Matt. 4:18); it is a net thrown from the shoulder. The net here in Luke 5 is a [trammel]{.class_4725} net (Gk.--[diktuon]{.class_4725}). It is a three-layered net that is a variation on the dragnet for deep water. The boat drops one end of the net down in the water, and then the boat circles with the other end so that the fish are caught in a bag or bowl in the water.

The picture given by Jesus in verse 10 is to soulwinning. Sinners may be "caught" [one on one]{.class_4725} in personal work (see that ["hook"]{.class_4723} in Matt. 17:27), in a [small group]{.class_4725} (the cast net), or [en masse]{.class_4725} (the seine []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_162}and trammel nets). The question is not how many are you catching; it is [are you fishing?]{.class_4725} You can't be a follower of Jesus Christ if you're not fishing for men (Matt. 4:19).

Historically, fishing on the Sea of Galilee occurred at night when the fish were active. In the morning, the fishermen would bring their catch to shore and sort it. Then they had to spend time washing the nets (vs. 2) to get the junk off them that they picked up the night before, and mending the nets (Matt. 4:21) from the rips and tears picked up on the rocks and other sharp objects in the water. After that, they could go home and get some rest till sundown when the whole thing started again.

Verse 3: ["And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon's, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship."]{.class_4723} Jesus doesn't just preach in synagogues (4:44); He's an "open-air" preacher---a [street]{.class_4725} preacher. Every preacher in both Testaments is a street preacher. Baruch is not reading the book of Jeremiah in the Temple building; he's reading it out in a courtyard at one of the gates as the people were going in and out. Ezekiel's not preaching in any synagogue; he's out there by a river with the captives (Ezek. 3:15). Peter, James, John, and Paul never preached in a church building a day in their lives. You know why? [There were no church buildings in the book of Acts;]{.class_4725} church buildings didn't show up until the Third Century A.D. The apostles are preaching outside on one of the porches of the Temple (Acts 5:12); when Paul gets thrown out of the synagogues in the cities, he goes out into the forums and market places and preaches.

The Reformation in England was brought about by a group of street preachers called "Lollards." When John Wesley got thrown out of the Anglican churches, he preached in the fields and on the streets and trained his Methodist preachers to do the same. George Whitefield preached out in the open air; Benjamin Franklin paced him off a mile when he was preaching in Boston Common one time and could hear every word he said clearly---[without]{.class_4725} any P.A. system. General William Booth and his Salvation Army held meetings out on the streets. If you are called to preach and don't preach on the street outside of the four walls of a church, you are not []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_163}carrying on a New Testament ministry.

["And taught the people out of the ship."]{.class_4723} [Anywhere]{.class_4725} is a good place to put out the word of God if you can put it out. Back when I first started out in the ministry, I got to preach in all kinds of places. I have preached on tennis courts and skating rinks at 11:00 at night; the guy in charge of the skating rink let me set up my drawing board in the middle of the skating rink and made the skaters sit there and listen to me for thirty minutes, and then let them skate again. I have preached to guys getting their hair cut in barber shops. I have preached in pool halls while the fellows were shooting pool. (One fellow got a big bruiser in a pool hall to pull the plug on the juke box and hold everyone off while he got up on a pool table and preached.) I have preached on the projection booth of a drive-in theater in the daytime for people. One time I even preached from the bottom of an empty swimming pool: I put up the drawing board and some chairs down there and preached to the people who climbed down in there to hear me. Of course, I realize that you might have a hard time doing some of those things now, but that's the way things were back in the 50s and 60s in the Carolinas, Mississippi, and Georgia.

When Jesus finished teaching, He told Peter, ["Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught"]{.class_4723} (vs. 4). The word ["draught"]{.class_4723} means "to draw out" as a verb and "that which is drawn out" as a noun. It is a reference to the fish that were going to be drawn out of the nets.

But look at what Peter's reply to Jesus is: ["Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the NET]{.class_4723} \[singular\]["]{.class_4723} (vs. 5). Jesus told Peter to let down the NETS, and Peter agreed to let down [one]{.class_4725} to get the Lord off his back. Do you know what difference that "s" makes? It was the difference in the net breaking and the ship sinking. The catch Jesus intended for more than one net broke the one net Peter begrudgingly let down (vs. 6), and because of that, they had to put the catch in their ships right then, and it almost sunk both vessels (vs. 7).

The lesson is to pay attention to details when the Lord tells you something. When Elisha lay dying, the King of Israel came to see the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_164}old prophet. Elisha ordered the King to take his bow and arrows, and Elisha laid his hands on the King's hands as he held them. Elisha had the King shoot an arrow out the window and said, "That's the arrow of the Lord's deliverance from Syria." Then Elisha told the King to take the rest of his arrows and hit the ground with them. The King struck the ground three times and stopped.

"You fool! You fool! You fool!" exclaimed the prophet.

"What do you mean 'You fool'?" asked the King in consternation.

"You only struck the ground three times," said Elisha.

"You never told me how many times to hit it," said the King.

"Didn't you listen to what I told you?" said Elisha. "I told you those arrows were [the arrows of the Lord's deliverance from Syria.]{.class_4725} If you had kept striking until I told you to stop, you would have wiped Syria off the face of the earth. Now you'll only whip them three times."

You can read that account in 2 Kings 13:14--19. That King thought he was humoring a dying old man. He didn't pay attention to what God told him through the prophet, and he paid the price for his flippancy.

Now look at the second time this miracle is performed, and notice how the Lord changes what He says because of Peter's partial obedience here in Luke 5.

["And he said unto them, Cast the NET]{.class_4723} \[singular\][ on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes\....and for all there were so many, YET WAS NOT THE NET BROKEN"]{.class_4723} (John 21:6, 11).

That was the Lord getting a little friendly jab in at Peter for what happened in Luke 5. That second time, the net didn't break, and they were able to tow their catch behind the boat to shore (John 21:8).

Back to Luke 5:[ "And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes\...and filled both the ships"]{.class_4723} (vss. 6--7). I sure would like to see a catch like that of sinners. Of course, the Lord blesses me with souls saved on a regular basis, but nothing like some of the brethren report in [The Sword of the Lord.]{.class_4725} Of course, not all of those reports are what they seem to be.

I'll never forget in the early days of my ministry when I came back from a week-long meeting in Tennessee where I preached in a Presbyterian church and only saw two girls saved. I picked up [The Sword of the Lord]{.class_4725} and read about an evangelist friend of mine named Monk Parker who held a city-wide meeting with 33 cooperating churches and reported 330 first-time decisions for Christ. I got talking to my buddy Glen Schunk, the fellow who got me into the ministry, about that thing and said, "I must not be called to preach."

"Why not?" he asked.

"Well," I said, "I preached a whole week and only had two girls saved the whole time. Here's Monk Parker preaching this city-wide campaign, and he has 330 professions of faith. That's ten per church, and I only had two saved."

Glen Schunk told me, "Oh, all those weren't saved, Pete."

"It says 330 first-time decisions," I replied.

"Well," he said, "200 of those were first-time decisions to have a family altar, and a hundred more were decisions for rededication or full-time Christian service. There were [thirty]{.class_4725} people saved in a Sunday school rally in the afternoon."

I thought to myself, "Thirty saved?! Why, I did better than that." That was thirty saved between 33 churches: that's less than one per church. I had two saved in one church, so my results were better percentage-wise than that big city-wide revival meeting.

The brethren are really bad about doctoring the numbers so the results look better than they are. When you see those big crowds go down in a Billy Graham crusade, not all of those people are coming down to get saved. Over half of them are [personal workers]{.class_4725} who were told to come down at the invitation to "encourage others" to come down.

Back in the 90s here in Pensacola, there was a three-ring circus going on called the "Brownsville Revival." The pastor of the Brownsville Assembly of God (John Kilpatrick) was reporting hundreds of thousands saved. A reporter from the [Pensacola News Journal]{.class_4725} went down there and for two days interviewed the crowds camped outside the church for the "revival" services. In two days, he couldn't find [one person]{.class_4725} who said he got saved in the meetings.

Do you know what was going on? The evangelist Kilpatrick brought in to preach (Stephen Hill) was telling people who came down to rededicate themselves to the Lord, "You're not rededicating! You're not rededicating! You're getting saved, you hear me; you're getting saved!" You had a bunch of stupid Charismatics "getting saved" [six and seven times]{.class_4739} just so the evangelist and pastor could turn in some kind of "Billions Served" report like a McDonald's hamburger sign.

Now I have nothing to complain about. The Lord has blessed me through the years with stringer after stringer of "fish." I preached a revival in a Southern Baptist church in Bay Minette, Alabama one time where so many people were walking the aisle to get saved that the church secretary got upset because she couldn't get all the names written down.

In the 90s, the Lord opened doors in Texas and Florida for what I call "prison junkets"; that is, for about two weeks every summer, I go around to prisons and jails and set up the chalk board where I can and preach to the inmates. In those junkets in Texas, I was covering the distance from New York City to Phoenix, Arizona. Since I started doing that, there has never been a summer where I haven't seen anywhere from 100--500 [saved.]{.class_4725} And those aren't "retreads" either; I only count the ones who are praying to get saved for the [very first time.]{.class_4725} But, you see, those all aren't saved in one "catch"; it's twenty at one prison, thirty at another, fifteen at another, etc.

Brethren, the key to catching fish is [to fish.]{.class_4725} You'll never catch any fish unless [you fish.]{.class_4725} ["Let down your nets for a draught."]{.class_4723} Pass out the tracts; go to the nursing homes; get out there on the street corner and wave your Bible around and yell, "Repent or perish! Turn or burn! Rome enslaves; Jesus saves!" Go to the jails and rescue missions; get out there and knock on doors. FISH!

Verse 8: ["When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord."]{.class_4723} This is real conviction and real repentance. In theology, this is known as [evangelical]{.class_4727} repentance; that is, it is repentance that leads to salvation. Look at 2 Corinthians 7:10 and notice that there are two kinds of repentance.

"For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_167}to be repented of: but the sorrow of the world worketh death."

Repentance is more than just sorrow over what you have [done;]{.class_4725} it is sorrow for what you [are.]{.class_4725} Nowhere is this better illustrated than in contrasting [Peter]{.class_4725} and [Judas Iscariot.]{.class_4725} Both were apostles and ministered with the Lord, and both betrayed their Lord in some way. Peter denied Christ three times before His enemies, and Judas turned Christ over to His enemies to be killed. Both "repented." Peter went out and ["wept bitterly"]{.class_4723} over his sin (Matt. 26:75), and Judas ["repented himself"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 27:3). The difference between them is that Judas went and confessed his sins to a priest for "penance" and ended up hanging himself (Matt. 27:3--5); Peter went to Jesus and received forgiveness and restoration (John 21:7--17). Judas was like Pharaoh (Exod. 9:27) and Balaam (Num. 22:35): he had [regret]{.class_4725} over being [caught]{.class_4725} in his sin. Peter was truly sorry for [what he was,]{.class_4725} as you read here in 5:8.

Brethren, when a sinner comes in contact with God, he immediately takes a completely [negative]{.class_4725} attitude toward himself and loses all faith and confidence in himself. When Job finally comes face to face with God, he says, ["I ABHOR MYSELF, and repent in dust and ashes"]{.class_4723} (Job 42:6); Isaiah says, ["Woe is me! for I am undone; because I am A MAN OF UNCLEAN LIPS"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 6:5).

The reason America is in the shape it is in is because the preachers have stopped telling the people they're [no good;]{.class_4725} they are being told that God loves them no matter what, so don't worry about judgment or Hell. [You had better worry about it.]{.class_4725} You can't be saved until you are [lost,]{.class_4725} and you'll never think you are lost as long as you hold onto the hope that if you just do this or that you will make it. As long as you believe you are all right the way you are, you are dead meat. You will never trust [God's]{.class_4725} righteousness---the Lord Jesus Christ---as long as you trust your [own]{.class_4725} righteousness (Rom. 10:3).

["For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of fishes which they had taken\...And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him"]{.class_4723} (vss. 9, 11).

As I pointed out under my prefatory remarks to the passage, this calling is a later calling than that found in Matthew 4:19--20 and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_168}Mark 1:16--20. In Matthew 4, when Jesus called Peter, James, John, and Andrew to follow him, they merely left their nets; but here, they ["forsook all."]{.class_4723} This presents a great spiritual truth: you can follow Christ without forsaking all, but [all]{.class_4725} is the call to true discipleship. This business of "If He's not Lord of all, He's not Lord at all" is nothing but Calvinistic nonsense used to produce "retreads" out of a bunch of backslidden Christians. Simon Peter was still having trouble submitting to the Lordship of Christ in Acts 10, as was Paul in Acts 21.

12 And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on his face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

13 And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.

14 And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

15 But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

16 And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.

["Behold a man full of leprosy"]{.class_4723} (vs. 12). The first time leprosy shows up in the Bible is Exodus 4:6--8 where its miraculous healing was given to Moses as a sign to Israel (cf. 1 Cor. 1:22). Since Jesus was the Prophet like unto Moses (Deut. 18:18 cf. Acts 3:22--26), He exercises the sign gifts given to Moses (see our comments on 4:40). Now go back and read chapter 4, verses 24--27, and note that when Jesus presents Himself as the Messiah to the people of His hometown, He uses the story of Elisha healing Naaman of his leprosy as a picture of God turning to the Gentiles because the Jews reject Christ.

It is apparent from studying passages like Numbers 12:10--15 and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_169}Leviticus 13--14 that leprosy is a type of sin, and it is connected with [Egypt,]{.class_4725} a type of the [world]{.class_4725} (see Exod. 15:26). It is an [African]{.class_4725} disease, like AIDS, Ebola, syphilis, and gonorrhea. As a picture of sin, leprosy in the Bible defiles its victim, and its healing is said to be a [cleansing.]{.class_4725} In the Bible, leprosy is [incurable.]{.class_4725} All the priest could do was declare the leper unclean or clean; if the leper was cured, he had to be cured by [God.]{.class_4725} Like sin, leprosy requires an ["atonement"]{.class_4723} (Lev. 14:18--21, 29, 31, 57); that atonement is made by the blood of the Lamb and the oil of the Holy Ghost (Lev. 14:14--15).

Nowadays, leprosy isn't as prevalent as it was in Bible days. It is called Hansen's disease, and as long as it is caught in its earliest stage, there is a cure for it. But after the disease reaches a certain point in its development, all that can be done for the patient is to place him or her in a leper colony so he or she will not infect others. The disease slowly rots the flesh of its victim, starting with the extremities, but no pain is felt because it kills the nerves. A person can sin so much that he sears his conscience (1 Tim. 4:2).

But leprosy makes a comeback in the Tribulation (see our comments on 2 Pet. 2:13; Jude 23 in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles,]{.class_4725} Vols. I & II). It is the disease inflicted on those who receive the Mark of the Beast (Rev. 16:2). If I don't miss my guess, in the Tribulation, when a Gentile gets leprosy from taking the Mark of the Beast, there is a way to get rid of it. He has to go down into the water like Naaman did in 2 Kings 5 and be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins. It will be Acts 2:38 all over again; the Campbellite will finally come into his own.

Now why is that? Because in the Old Testament, water was connected with the cleansing of the leper (Lev. 14:5, 6, 8), and in the New Testament, those Jews associated water baptism with the purification of the flesh (John 2:6, 3:25--26; Acts 22:16; Heb. 9:19). That is [not]{.class_4725} your case if you are a born-again Christian in the Body of Christ. Peter says water baptism is ["NOT the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 3:21). But it will be in the Tribulation when the Body of Christ is gone, just as it was in 2 Kings 5:14.

Back to Luke 5:12, ["Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean."]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_170} Like the penitent thief on the cross (Luke 23:42) and Saul on the road to Damascus (Acts 9:6), this leper prefaces his request by acknowledging the Lordship of Christ. This marks this miracle as a type of New Testament salvation. The corrupt, apostate modern versions take the word ["Lord"]{.class_4723} out of the mouths of the thief and Saul so you will lose the connection. Evidently, the Satanic New Age Bible versions have a "knack" for snatching salvation out of the mouth of a sinner the moment he is trusting Christ (see our comments on Acts 9:7 in that Commentary).

["And he put forth his hand, and touched him"]{.class_4723} (vs. 13). That was a no-no under the Law. That leper was to live outside the camp and cover his mouth and cry ["Unclean, unclean"]{.class_4723} so no one would come near him and be contaminated (Lev. 13:45--46; Num. 5:2--3). Whoever touched someone who was unclean was unclean himself (Num. 19:22). But Christ is acting as the great High Priest (Heb. 3:1). In the Old Testament, the Aaronic high priest was allowed to touch the cleansed leper by means of shed blood (Lev. 14:14). Christ is operating in His Messianic role by bearing the infirmities of His people through the blood He will shed for them (Isa. 53:5 cf. Matt. 8:17).

We have a missionary we support here at Bible Baptist named Tommy Tillman. Among his ministries are leper colonies he has started in Thailand. He goes and sees those lepers and touches them all the time. So far, the Lord has taken care of him, and he has not gotten the disease. I read a testimony of one of those lepers where he thanked Bro. Tillman for being the first man ever to touch him. By the time I read that letter, that dear saint had died and gone home to be with the Lord, but before he passed, he told Bro. Tillman that the reason he had gotten saved was because Bro. Tillman wasn't afraid to touch him.

["Go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them"]{.class_4723} (vs. 14). That offering is found in Leviticus 14:12--24, which see.

["And he charged him to tell no man."]{.class_4723} The reason is obvious from what follows in verse 15: ["But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_171}together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities."]{.class_4723}

Mark 3:20 says, ["And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread."]{.class_4723}

This is why you read from time to time in the Gospels Jesus telling someone not to tell what He did for them. In His human body, Jesus needed food; He needed rest. He needed to be away from the crowds from time to time to get those things.

The problem is that some Christians use those commands in the Gospels to ["tell no man"]{.class_4723} as an excuse not to witness. Of course, the Lord gives no such commands after the Resurrection. The command for the New Testament Christian in the Body of Christ is to ["preach the gospel to every creature"]{.class_4723} (Mark 16:15) and to ["be witnesses unto\...the uttermost part of the earth"]{.class_4723} (Acts 1:8).

["And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed"]{.class_4747} (vs. 16). Over and over again in the Gospels, Jesus gets off alone and prays. That's what He does in Mark 1:34--35. Here in Luke, the Bible says He prayed all night in a mountain before choosing His disciples (6:12). He did it again after feeding the 5,000 (Matt. 14:23). And of course, the night before His crucifixion, He goes off in the Garden of Gethsemane to pray with Peter, James, and John (Matt. 26:36--39). Jesus got strength from God through prayer; He got refreshment of spirit and guidance from His Father. If the Son of God needed these things while here on earth, how much more do we? Like our Saviour, we can get those things through prayer.

17 And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judaea, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.

18 And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought means to bring him in, and to lay him before him.

19 And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_172}with his couch into the midst before Jesus.

20 And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.

21 And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?

22 But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?

23 Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?

24 But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.

25 And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.

26 And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.

Now look at the way verse 17 is worded. Jesus is teaching, and the ["Pharisees and doctors of the law"]{.class_4723} are there listening to Him. The end of the verse says, ["and the power of the Lord was present to heal THEM." ]{.class_4723}Who is the ["them"]{.class_4723}? In the verse, the antecedent is the ["Pharisees and doctors of the law."]{.class_4723} Well, they weren't there to be healed; they weren't sick.

But the Pharisees' problem wasn't [physical;]{.class_4725} it was [spiritual.]{.class_4725} Look at what Jesus told them in John 9:39--41.

["And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not ]{.class_4723}\[physical blindness---He had just gotten through healing a blind man in John 9\][ might see; and that they which see might be made blind ]{.class_4723}\[spiritual blindness\][. And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also? ]{.class_4723}\[the Pharisees understood perfectly what He was saying\][ Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ1117.xhtml_page_173}see; therefore your sin remaineth."]{.class_4723}

In other words, the spiritual blindness of the Pharisees was not some arbitrary decree of reprobation like Calvin taught, nor was it blind ignorance. It was [willful rejection of the truth.]{.class_4725} It's like a fellow shutting his eyes and turning his back to something you are trying to show him. That's what the Pharisees needed to be healed of.

Verse 17 is the second time in the book of Luke where the ["doctors of the law"]{.class_4723} show up (see 2:46). The man who trained Paul (Gamaliel---Acts 22:3) was ["a doctor of the law"]{.class_4723} (Acts 5:34). The Pharisees were the Bible teachers of the nation of Israel, and they had three levels of education: Rab, Rabbi, and Rabbak (a doctor of the Law). That's from where the idea comes of three basic degrees in education. In vocations, there are [apprentice, journeyman,]{.class_4725} and [master;]{.class_4725} in academics, there are a bachelor's degree, a master's degree, and a doctor's degree.

When I went to College, to earn a Ph.D., you had to take four years of undergraduate work and six years of post-graduate work: two for your master's and four for your doctorate. You had to write one thesis for your master's degree and another thesis for your doctorate. To get your doctorate at a Christian or religious institution, you had to be able to translate Greek and Hebrew and a third language of your choice by sight. A lot of these pastors who are "Doctors of Divinity" and "Doctors of Letters" are "honorary doctors"; that is, some institution has conferred a degree on them which they didn't earn in order to get their money or support for the institution.
