10 For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_347}opened.

11 If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if he ask a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?

12 Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?

13 If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?

Now look at verse 9. What is prayer; I mean, basically? It is [asking.]{.class_4725} ["ASK, and it shall be given you; SEEK, and ye shall find; KNOCK, and it shall be opened unto you."]{.class_4723} There it is, spelled out for you in the [English:]{.class_4725} ASK---Ask, Seek, Knock. Talk about an advanced revelation! You can't get that from the Greek. I don't know how many preachers have used that natural outline given in [King James]{.class_4725} English, but it must number in the tens of thousands. Even John R. Rice picked it up.

Prayer is asking. It is petitioning God for supply and making intercession for others. There is nothing wrong with asking God for things. Your Lord and Saviour commanded you to do it (vs. 9), and asking is the only way you are going to get God to meet your needs (vs. 10). Before Paul ever talked about being able to do all things through Christ strengthening him (Phil. 4:13) and God supplying all his needs through Christ's riches (Phil. 4:19), he tells the Philippians: ["in every thing by PRAYER AND SUPPLICATION with thanksgiving let your REQUESTS be made known unto God"]{.class_4723} (Phil. 4:6). James tells you that one of the reasons you don't get anything from God is because you [don't ask:]{.class_4725} ["ye have not, because YE ASK NOT"]{.class_4723} (James 4:2).

And then a very hard passage to reconcile, given Paul's situation in 2 Corinthians 12 (see our remarks under vs. 8). ["If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if he ask a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent? Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?"]{.class_4723} (vss. 11--12).

Well, it sure looks that way sometimes, doesn't it? If you ask God for prosperity and good health, would He give you sickness and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_348}hospital bills you can't pay? Well, He might. You can't tell God what to do; I guess you know that by now. Nevertheless, the general principle holds true: God is not cruel and sadistic in answering prayers. He will supply your [needs,]{.class_4725} not necessarily your [greeds.]{.class_4725} And whatever ["evil"]{.class_4723} He sends your way (Job 2:10) is promised to be for your good (Rom. 8:28).

Verse 13: ["If ye then, being evil\...."]{.class_4723} What a thing to say! About whom is Jesus talking? Why, His own disciples: Peter, James, John, Matthew, et al. Those were the best guys He had, and He called them ["evil."]{.class_4723} That's your Saviour's evaluation of human nature in His children: ["If ye then, being evil\...."]{.class_4723} He says in the Old Testament, ["every man\...."]{.class_4723} Get that. He doesn't say some men; He says [every one of them.]{.class_4725} He's talking about the best man you know, whether he is saved or not: ["Every man at his best state is altogether vanity"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 39:5). Solomon wrote: ["For there is not a just man upon earth, that doeth good, and sinneth not"]{.class_4723} (Eccl. 7:20). The New Birth changed nothing in that regard (Rom. 7:18--20).

["If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him."]{.class_4723} Then verses 9--12 weren't unlimited in their scope. They referred to one specific prayer request: ["the Holy Spirit."]{.class_4723}

Again, you have to watch out for the doctrine here. None of you reading this page, [if you are saved,]{.class_4725} have to ask God the Father to give you the Holy Spirit. You receive the Holy Spirit the moment you trust Christ as your Saviour (Gal. 3:14), and from that point on, the Holy Spirit takes up permanent residence in your body (1 Cor. 6:19). You don't lose Him and get Him back, and lose Him and get Him back, like the stupid Charismatics at the "Brownsville Revival" taught. When the Holy Spirit comes into you at salvation (1 Cor. 12:13 cf. Rom. 8:9), He comes in to stay [forever]{.class_4725} (John 14:16). Now you can pray to be [filled]{.class_4725} with the Holy Spirit, but you don't pray to [get]{.class_4725} the Holy Spirit because you [have]{.class_4725} the Holy Spirit.

You see, the ministry of the Holy Spirit in the New Testament Body of Christ is different than it was to Jews in the Old Testament. In the Old Testament, the Holy Spirit would come [on]{.class_4725} men for certain []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_349}tasks, like He did with Samson (Judg. 14:6). In some instances, the Holy Spirit came [into ]{.class_4725}a man, like Joseph (Gen. 41:38) and Daniel (Dan. 4:9), for the purpose of prophetic revelation. But in the Old Testament, the Holy Spirit could [leave]{.class_4725} a man (Psa. 51:11), like He did with Saul (1 Sam. 16:14).

That's not the case of a New Testament believer. In the New Testament, after Pentecost, the Holy Spirit comes into a person at salvation and seals him tight (Eph. 1:13, 4:30). It is like sealing water in a mason jar. All the water the jar is ever going to get is in there when it is sealed, but you can expand the water to the whole jar by heating up the jar until the water turns to steam and fills the whole thing.

Being filled with the Spirit is not [you getting more of the Holy Spirit,]{.class_4725} because you got all the Holy Spirit you were ever going to get the moment you got saved. Being filled with the Holy Spirit is [the Holy Spirit getting more of you.]{.class_4725} To be filled with the Spirit, you put yourself in a situation where the One inside you takes control of your arms, legs, hands, feet, mouth, eyes, thoughts, imaginations, heart, etc. [That's]{.class_4725} the filling of the Spirit.

When the Holy Spirit begins to move in a meeting, it's[ not]{.class_4725} to get inside of you because He's already there if you are saved. If you have a building full of vessels of the Holy Spirit who are yielding themselves to the Spirit of God and asking God to fill them, something is going to happen, and it [won't]{.class_4725} be doing the "bumps and grinds" or barking like a dog or getting the "holy laugh" or any of that kooky Charismatic nonsense. When a bunch of Christians get filled with the Holy Spirit at the same time, you will definitely feel something, but that comes from praying and surrendering to the Lord and honoring God the way you should. If a congregation makes a church building the kind of a place in which the Holy Spirit feels at home, He will work there. That's the kind of application you can make to verse 13 for the New Testament believer if you are going to apply the thing [spiritually.]{.class_4725}

14 And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb. And it came to pass, when the devil was gone out, the dumb spake; and the people wondered.

15 But some of them said, He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.

16 And others, tempting him, sought of him a sign from heaven.

17 But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth.

18 If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.

19 And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.

20 But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.

["And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb"]{.class_4723} (vs. 14). ["Dumb"]{.class_4723} in the sense that the devil wouldn't let the fellow [speak.]{.class_4725} The word [dumb]{.class_4725} doesn't mean "stupid" in the Bible. Matthew adds that the fellow was both ["blind, and dumb"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 12:22).

["Beelzebub"]{.class_4723} (vs. 15) is another name for [Satan;]{.class_4725} it is the Greek spelling of the Hebrew word ["Baalzebub,"]{.class_4723} the name for a Philistine deity (see 2 Kings 1:2). The word means "Lord of the flies," or "Lord of filth," since a fly is an unclean winged creature. Before he fell, Satan was a [winged]{.class_4725} cherub (Ezek. 28:14 cf. Ezek. 1:5--6, 10:1--22), and after he fell, he is a [winged]{.class_4725} serpent or dragon (Isa. 30:6; Rev. 12:9). So the spirits that make up his spiritual kingdom (Eph. 6:12) are pictured as unclean [birds]{.class_4725} (see 8:5, 12 and our comments on 3:22). These demonic spirits are small, ranging anywhere from the size of a fly to an owl or bittern, which is why seven could fit into Mary Magdalene and a thousand into the maniac of Gadara (see our comments on 8:2, 30). They couldn't possibly be fallen angels, because everywhere an angel shows up in the Bible, he appears as [a full grown man]{.class_4725} (see Rev. 21:17) with no wings. From where these small unclean spirits come, the Scriptures never reveal, but they weren't any angels that fell in Genesis 1 or Genesis 6.

["And others, tempting him, sought of him a sign from heaven"]{.class_4723} (vs. 16). ["Tempting"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_351} as in trying His patience. They want Jesus to give them ["a sign from heaven"]{.class_4723} after He has already cast out devils, healed the sick, restored sight to the blind, made the lame to walk, raised the dead, fed the multitudes miraculously, calmed storms, etc. You can see how such a request would try someone's patience, including the Lord's. They've rejected the signs He has already given them on numerous occasions, and they are rejecting the one He is doing right in front of their faces that moment. It's like an atheist or agnostic wanting proof of the existence of God after rejecting the statistical, mathematical proof of Bible prophecy (see [Science Speaks]{.class_4725} by Peter Stoner).

Verse 17: ["But he, knowing their thoughts ]{.class_4723}\[see our comments on 5:22\][, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth."]{.class_4723} Jesus is talking about [civil war;]{.class_4725} even Abraham Lincoln was able to pick up that much from the text. The idea is that Christ can't be casting out the demons through the Devil's power, because that would be Satan fighting against his own forces (vs. 18).

Interestingly enough, the scribes and Pharisees had no problem with Jews traveling around performing exorcisms (see Acts 19:13--14). What they had a problem with was [Jesus]{.class_4725} [and His followers]{.class_4725} doing it. The Lord is quick to point out this double standard (vs. 19).

Verse 20: ["But if I with the finger of God cast out devils."]{.class_4723} An expression like ["the finger of God"]{.class_4723} is what is known as an "anthropomorphism." It is ascribing the physical characteristics of man to God. ["God is a Spirit"]{.class_4723} (John 4:24), so outside of the incarnation of the Lord Jesus Christ, He has no physical hands or fingers or heart or legs or feet or nose or eyes, etc. But when relating to His creation, God speaks of Himself in men's terms. In the Old Testament, the Lord appears in the form of a man (the Angel of the Lord), and when He shows up in the New Testament, He takes the form of a man (His Son). So even though ["the finger of God"]{.class_4723} is a figurative expression, we understand its meaning.

["The finger of God"]{.class_4723} shows up several times in the Scriptures. David said the heavens (the sun, moon, and stars) are the work of God's fingers (Psa. 8:3). That's God flinging those stars and planets []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_352}out into space with His hands. That's power, boy.

Those magicians in Egypt realized that when they couldn't duplicate the miracle of creating life from dust (cf. Gen. 2:7). God had commanded Moses to hit the dirt with his rod, and the dust would become lice all over Egypt, but when Pharaoh's magicians tried it, they couldn't do it (Exod. 8:16--18). They told Pharaoh, ["This is the finger of God"]{.class_4723} (Exod. 8:19). In other words, only the power of God could accomplish what Moses and Aaron did.

In Exodus 31:18 and Deuteronomy 9:10, you are told the Ten Commandments were ["written with the finger of God."]{.class_4723} God reached down there and carved out those words on those stone tablets Himself. That's what the NEA, the ACLU, and the Supreme Court don't want your children reading in the public schools: the direct writing of God. They'll expose the kiddies to [Playboy, ]{.class_4725}sodomy, bestiality, condoms, fornication, and every other form of perversion, but they can't stand those "Thou shalt nots" from their Creator.

The same finger that wrote the Ten Commandments there on Mt. Sinai shows up at Belshazzar's drunken orgy in Daniel 5 and writes ["MENE, MENE, TEKEL, UPHARSIN"]{.class_4723} (Dan. 5:25). That's God reading the riot act to a sinner; it means "numbered, numbered, weighed, divided." God numbered the days of the kingdom of Babylon, weighed Belshazzar's heart against the revelation he had been given, and finished off both the King and his kingdom by sending in the Medes and Persians to divide the whole thing up.

Finally, when God shows up on earth ["in the flesh"]{.class_4723} (1 Tim. 3:16), He stoops down and writes in the sand with His finger when an adulteress is brought to Him to be stoned (John 8:1--8). Now you know what's interesting about each of the times ["the finger of God"]{.class_4723} shows up in the Scriptures? It's how man acts toward what God does with His fingers. You take the heavens God made to declare His glory (Psa. 19:1). What does man do with them? Why, he changes ["the truth of God into a lie"]{.class_4723} and worships and serves ["the creature more than the Creator"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 1:25). Instead of worshipping and serving God alone, men worship ["all the host of heaven"]{.class_4723} (Deut. 4:19; Jer. 8:2, 19:13). Those pagan magicians recognize ["the finger of God,"]{.class_4723} and what does Pharaoh do with that []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_353}knowledge? He ignores it and goes right on doing what he wants to do, even though he knows it is destroying him.

God writes out what He expects from His creation where anyone can get it, and what happens? All Ten Commandments get busted before 24 hours are up. God warns a sinner in writing of the judgment to come, and what's men's reaction? They couldn't read it or understand it. When Jesus writes there in the dirt, do you know what men do? Eventually, men walked all over it. You couldn't find what Christ wrote on the ground if you went over to Palestine today.

All of that is how men treat God's words: they [change]{.class_4725} them, [ignore]{.class_4725} them, [break]{.class_4725} them, [won't read or study]{.class_4725} them, and [trample]{.class_4725} them under foot. What's the cure for all of that? It's [believing]{.class_4725} and [receiving]{.class_4725} what God said as the words of God and not men (1 Thess. 2:13). It's [listening]{.class_4725} to what God said in His Book (John 8:47) and [doing]{.class_4725} it (James 1:22).

Back to verse 20: ["But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you." "The kingdom of God"]{.class_4723}---a [spiritual]{.class_4725} kingdom. If you check out the cross-reference over in Matthew 12:28 (Matthew is the Gospel that deals with the [physical]{.class_4725} Kingdom of Heaven), you find the same thing. He's dealing with evil [spirits]{.class_4725} through the Holy [Spirit,]{.class_4725} so it's a [spiritual]{.class_4725} kingdom of the inner man here (see 17:21).

21 When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:

22 But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.

23 He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.

In the illustration given by Christ, the ["strong man"]{.class_4723} is obviously Satan who ["keepeth his palace"]{.class_4723}; that is, the sinner he possesses (vs. 21). Naturally speaking, the power of Satan goes unchallenged, so ["his goods]{.class_4723} \[the sinner\][ are in peace."]{.class_4723} Apart from the conviction of the Holy Spirit of God, the sinner is content to abide in his lost state until he hits the Lake of Fire.

The ["stronger"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_354} man who overcomes the first man and takes his armor and divides his spoil (vs. 22) is the Lord Jesus Christ. The Bible says He ["spoiled principalities and powers"]{.class_4723} and ["made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them"]{.class_4723} when He died on the cross (Col. 2:14--15). He is the only one who is able to turn a sinner ["from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan unto God"]{.class_4723} (Acts 26:18).

And then a great statement you need to get: ["He that is not WITH me is AGAINST me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth"]{.class_4723} (vs. 23). You are either [for]{.class_4725} Christ or [against]{.class_4725} Him. There is [no middle ground;]{.class_4725} there is no straddling the fence. You either [accept]{.class_4725} Christ or you [reject]{.class_4725} Him.

You say, "Well, I don't exactly accept Him, but I don't exactly reject Him either." Then you've just turned Him down like a bedsheet, man. When it comes to Jesus Christ, [there is no neutrality.]{.class_4733}

24 When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.

25 And when he cometh, he findeth it swept and garnished.

26 Then goeth he, and taketh to him seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first.

We have already commented on the affinity of demons for [warm, wet]{.class_4725} places in our remarks under 8:31 (which see). Notice that the devil calls the body of the unsaved man ["my house"]{.class_4723} (vs. 24). So just as the body of the believer is ["the temple of the Holy Ghost"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 6:19), the body of the unbeliever is [the temple of the unholy ghost.]{.class_4725}

Verse 25 describes reformation without regeneration. The unclean spirit moved out, but nothing else moved in. The sinner cleaned himself up without any conversion. Moral reformation saves no one. The word ["garnished"]{.class_4723} in verse 25 is a very descriptive term. When you talk about a "garnish" on a plate, it is something that [looks good]{.class_4725} but is [not eaten.]{.class_4725} The sinner has "spruced up" the hog pen, []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_355}[but it is still a hog pen.]{.class_4725}

Without conversion and regeneration, the state of the moralized sinner becomes worse than before he cleaned up (vs. 26). Christ applies what He says to the generation that crucified Him (Matt. 12:45). You look at the Gospels and Acts, and you find those Jews accepting the Messianic prophecies of the Old Testament (e.g., see Matt. 2:5--6; John 7:42). But after they reject Christ, the Rabbis reinterpret those Scriptures so they apply to Israel or Hezekiah. A modern Jew doesn't even believe the Old Testament like the lost Pharisees of Christ's day did.

Peter applies the principle of verse 26 to the individual Christ-rejecter. He talks about people who have ["escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ"]{.class_4723} (2 Pet. 2:20). That is, they've heard about Christ and what the Bible says about sin and righteousness, and they've reacted to it by giving up ["the pollutions of the world."]{.class_4723} They quit smoking; they quit drinking; they get rid of the drugs; they stop fornicating; they may even begin going to church. Like Herod did with John the Baptist, they do ["many things"]{.class_4723} the preacher says (see Mark 6:20). The one thing they don't do is [trust Christ and His righteousness to save them.]{.class_4725} Look at 2 Peter 2:21.

"For it had been better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than, after they have known it, to turn from the holy commandment delivered unto them."

What's ["the holy commandment"]{.class_4723}? First John 3:23---["And this is his commandment, THAT WE SHOULD BELIEVE ON THE NAME OF HIS SON JESUS CHRIST, and love one another, as he gave us commandment."]{.class_4723}

Those people hear the word of God, they clean up their lives, BUT THEY REJECT CHRIST. What happens? They become ["again entangled"]{.class_4723} and ["overcome"]{.class_4723} by ["the pollutions of the world"]{.class_4723} they got rid of, and ["the latter end is worse with them than the beginning"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 2:20). Those aren't "Christians" who lose their salvation; the Bible calls them hogs and dogs, [not sheep.]{.class_4727}

["But it is happened unto them according to the true proverb, The dog is turned to his own vomit again; and the sow that was washed to her wallowing in the mire"]{.class_4723} (2 Pet. []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_356}2:22).

Reformation may do you some good in the flesh. Certainly it will improve your health if you give up the cigarettes and the booze and the drugs and live a clean life. It might improve your family life and raise your respectability in the community. I mean, there are all kinds of advantages to living right. But when it comes to eternity, all reformation does is put you in Hell as a cleaner sinner than the guy who stays on the bottle or the needle, and who smokes and whores around, etc. MORAL REFORMATION IS NOT SALVATION.

27 And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.

28 But he said, Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.

We have already made extensive comment on the passage under 1:28, 48 and 8:21 (which see).

29 And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.

30 For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.

31 The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.

32 The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.

Verse 29 is a good illustration of the principle of Biblical []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_357}hermeneutics that you should never interpret a complete statement in the light of an incomplete statement. Mark 8:12 says, ["There shall no sign be given unto this generation"]{.class_4723}: that's the incomplete statement. The complete statement is found here in Luke 11:29---["there shall no sign be given it, BUT THE SIGN OF JONAS THE PROPHET."]{.class_4723} We will run into a similar thing in 16:18. Always interpret an incomplete statement in the Bible by a complete statement.

Now what is the sign of Jonah? It isn't given here in Luke 11, but it is in Matthew 12.

["For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 12:40).

So the sign of Jonah is [the resurrection from the dead:]{.class_4725} it's a fellow coming up ahead of his time. So Jonah wasn't alive in the belly of the whale, although there have been men who have survived being swallowed by whales and basking sharks (see the Introduction to Jonah in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets,]{.class_4725} Vol. I). Jonah died, was buried, and rose again as a sign to lost Gentiles in Nineveh ( vs. 30). [That's]{.class_4725} the sign that Simeon said would ["be spoken against"]{.class_4723} (see our comments on 1:34).

When Jonah died in that whale's belly, he wasn't in any "grave." He said, ["out of the belly of HELL cried I"]{.class_4723} (Jon. 2:2). He was in a place at the bottom of the mountains that had ["bars"]{.class_4723} (Jon. 2:6) with ["gates"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 16:18) that could be opened with ["keys"]{.class_4723} (Rev. 1:18). He said, ["yet hast thou brought up my life from corruption"]{.class_4723} (Jon. 2:6). Why, that's what was said about Christ's resurrection.

["Because thou wilt not leave my soul in HELL, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption\....He seeing this before spake of THE RESURRECTION OF CHRIST, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption"]{.class_4723} (Acts 2:27, 31).

So Jonah is a picture of the death, burial, and resurrection of the Lord Jesus Christ---literally. Jonah dies, and his body is buried in the whale's belly for three days and three nights. In the meantime, his soul goes down to Hell in ["the heart of the earth"]{.class_4723} and is resurrected when the whale vomits up Jonah. The difference []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_358}between Jonah and Christ is that when the Lord goes down there to Hell, He doesn't pray like Jonah does; He [preaches.]{.class_4725}

["By which also he went and preached unto the spirits in prison; Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 3:19--20).

Christ goes down to Hell and preaches to disobedient spirits from before the Flood. First Peter 4:6 says; ["For for this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead."]{.class_4723}

When Jesus is buried, His soul goes down, and He preaches condemnation to that bunch in Hell. Then He steps over to Abraham's Bosom across from Hell (Luke 16:22, 26) and preaches ["the gospel"]{.class_4723} to the Old Testament saints who had their sins forgiven but not taken away (see our comments on Heb. 9:22, 10:4 in that Commentary). ["The gospel,"]{.class_4723} in this case, is the good news that the blood of the Lord Jesus Christ completely paid for all the sins of the Old Testament saints (see our comments on Rom. 3:25 in that Commentary) so they didn't have to stay down there anymore. When Christ rises from the dead, He comes out with ["the keys of hell and of death"]{.class_4723} (Rev. 1:18), and He's leading a "jail break."

["Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men. (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 4:8--9).

When Christ comes up at the Resurrection, there are Old Testament saints who come up with Him (Matt. 27:52--53).

Now that's ["the sign of Jonas."]{.class_4723} Jonah comes into Nineveh as a man who died and was resurrected, and Jesus tells those Jews standing before Him: ["so shall also the Son of man be to this generation"]{.class_4723} (vs. 30). The difference between those heathen Ninevites and the Jews of Christ's day was that ["the men of Nineve\...repented at the preaching of Jonas"]{.class_4723} (vs. 32). Everyone in the entire city from the King down to the animals repented (Jon. 3:5--8). Not so with those Jews. Jesus said, ["a greater than Jonas is here,"]{.class_4723} and they end up taking Him and turning Him over to the Romans to be crucified. It is the leaders of the nation who do this, and the people follow them (Matt. 21:37--39, 27:20). So Jesus tells them, []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_359}["The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall CONDEMN IT."]{.class_4723}

The implication of verses 31--32 is that at the White Throne Judgment, there will be saved heathen from the Old Testament, who lived by the only light they had---the light of [conscience]{.class_4725} (Rom. 2:14--15), who will step forward to testify [against]{.class_4725} those Jews who rejected their Messiah. All the Queen of Sheba had was a hedonistic King (Solomon), and she believed. All the Ninevites had was a rebellious prophet (Jonah), and they repented. Those Jews in the Gospels had the sinless Son of God before them performing miracles, and they rejected Him and had Him killed. That's what made them ["an evil generation"]{.class_4723} (vs. 29).

33 No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth it in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.

34 The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when thine eye is evil, thy body also is full of darkness.

35 Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.

36 If thy whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.

The passage here is another part of the Sermon on the Mount given at a different time during Christ's earthly ministry (see our remarks under 6:17). In fact, verse 33 is a repeat of the material we have already covered in 8:16 (which see).

["The light of the body is the eye"]{.class_4723} (vs. 34). Without your eyes, you wouldn't be able to see anything. The old saying is: "The eye is the window to the soul." When I look at you, I see your body. [That's not you.]{.class_4725} The real you is inside your body, peeking out at me through your eyeballs.

The closest you get to seeing a man's soul is when you look a guy straight in the eye. You don't see the real man when you look down []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_360}his throat or up his nose or in his ear, but you do when you look in his eyes. Your soul is fed by your eye. Your spirit is fed by your ear---["So then faith cometh by HEARING, and hearing by the word of God"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 10:17). That's [spiritual,]{.class_4725} see. What enters your eye is [tangible:]{.class_4725} you can see it and touch it. But what enters your ear is [intangible:]{.class_4725} it can't be seen or touched. That's why the closest thing to spiritual activity you can get is classical music. Classical music is "pure" music. It is music for [music's]{.class_4725} sake; it isn't to create some visual image in your mind or to accompany some visual image you are watching.

Every person is a [body,]{.class_4725} a [soul,]{.class_4725} and a [spirit.]{.class_4725} If you want to develop your body, you watch what you eat and you exercise; if you want to develop your soul, you deal with art, literature, and music; if you want to develop your spirit, you do it through Bible reading and prayer. A man's character is determined by what enters the [eye,]{.class_4725} the [ear,]{.class_4725} and the [mouth;]{.class_4725} but mostly the [eye.]{.class_4725}

What goes in your mouth affects you, but not as much as what goes in your ears and your eyes. What enters your ears and eyes determines how you [think.]{.class_4725} And when it comes down to the ears and the eyes, what enters the eyes is always stronger than what enters the ear. Radio can never do the damage television does, because with the television, you can see the junk that tempts you. Achan [saw]{.class_4725} the garment and the silver and gold before he coveted them and took them (Josh. 7:21). David would never have committed adultery with Bathsheba if his eyes had not been looking in the wrong place.

Television is made to put images before your face to make you covet. That's why before Israel entered the land of Canaan, the Lord told them to ["destroy all their PICTURES"]{.class_4723} (Num. 33:52). That has been changed in all the new "Bibles" because the guys making the translations are all watching pictures right in their living rooms.

["The light of the body is the eye."]{.class_4723} Do you want light in your soul? Then get in that Book: ["The entrance of thy WORDS giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 119:130). The Scriptures help a Christian to "see" things the way his Lord sees them (see our comments on Song of Sol. 1:15 in that Commentary).

["Therefore when thine eye is single."]{.class_4723} He's talking about singleness of [purpose.]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_361} You are to keep your spiritual eyes focused on your leader: ["Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 12:2). Like a runner in a race, you are to keep your eyes focused on the finish line: ["I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus"]{.class_4723} (Phil. 3:14).

["When thine eye is single, thy whole body is full of light; but when thine eye is evil ]{.class_4723}\[i.e., looking at the wrong things\][, thy body also is full of darkness. Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness"]{.class_4723} (vss. 34--35). The "light" you take in through your eye can be a "black light." David wrote, ["I will set no wicked thing before mine eyes"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 101:3).

["If thy whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light"]{.class_4756} (vs. 36). A picture for the Christian of the filling of the Holy Spirit. When I was a teenager, my buddies and I would sneak into movie theaters by the fire escape when the janitor had the door open. We would be lying behind the theater seats waiting for the movie to start, and I would look back toward the open door. Sunlight would be streaming in through that door, and I could see all the dust in the air. I remember thinking to myself, "Good night! Are we breathing all that dust while we're watching the movie?" I guess we were. Now that's the condition of your heart. It's full of the dust and grime of sin, and the only way to expose it is to let the Holy Spirit shine the light of the word of God through there and clean it up. The more of the word you get in you, the more convicting and cleaning the Holy Spirit can do.

["The whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth bring forth light."]{.class_4723} Now that's the goal. It is to become so full of the light of the word of God that it comes out in the way you live, the way you talk, the way you act, etc. It becomes impossible to hide ["it in a secret place"]{.class_4723} (vs. 33).

37 And as he spake, a certain Pharisee besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sat down to meat.

38 And when the Pharisee saw it, he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner.

39 And the Lord said unto him, Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.

40 Ye fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?

41 But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.

42 But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

43 Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.

44 Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over them are not aware of them.

Once more a Pharisee invites Jesus to dinner (see our remarks under 7:36), and this one doesn't fare any better than the last one. Unlike the publicans and harlots who came to hear Jesus (see 15:1--2), all the Pharisees were trying to do was catch Him in His words (see vs. 54) or find some fine nuance of the Law they thought He was violating. In this case, Jesus hadn't washed His hands before eating. We have already commented on this hypocritical nitpicking under our remarks on 6:41 (which see).

Again, Jesus' response is not "the sweet spirit of Christ" propagated by men like Stewart Custer and Joel Osteen (see our remarks under 3:8). He says to His host, ["Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness"]{.class_4723} (vs. 39). Over in Matthew 23:27, He calls them ["whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men's bones, and of all uncleanness"]{.class_4723} (see also vs. 44 here in Luke 11). Jesus says, "You Pharisees clean up nice on the outside, but you are filthy and wicked and dead on the inside."

Verse 40:[ "Ye fools]{.class_4723} \[what a thing to say to your dinner host!\][, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_363} In other words, the Lord made it all, and He deserves it all: the inside [and]{.class_4725} the outside. The old song says, "Not just a [part,]{.class_4725} but [all]{.class_4725} of my heart."

["But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you"]{.class_4723} (vs. 41). Notice how often Christ deals with the Pharisees about [money]{.class_4725} and [giving]{.class_4725}. He told that rich young ruler to sell all he had and give the money to the poor if he wanted to be perfect (Matt. 19:21). Why was that? The problem was that those Pharisees were [covetous ]{.class_4725}(see 16:14). In the Old Testament, if a man lived right, it was taken for granted that God would bless him [materially.]{.class_4725} Those Pharisees weren't living right to please God or help others; they were living right in hopes of it paying off in cold, hard cash. They would meticulously count out the tithe of herbs in the garden (vs. 42 cf. Matt. 23:23) and ignore loving their neighbor (see the Parable of the Good Samaritan back in 10:23--37). Jesus is hitting them where it hurts:[ in the wallet.]{.class_4725}

Verse 43 should be compared with 20:46. The scribes and the Pharisees were marked not only by a love of money, but a desire for attention---["the pride of life"]{.class_4723} (1 John 2:16). Note in 20:46 that these scribes and Pharisees walk around ["in long robes"]{.class_4723} and ["devour widows' houses"]{.class_4723} and[ "make long prayers"]{.class_4723} (20:46--47). One cannot help thinking when reading such descriptions of Roman Catholic Priests in their "vestments" droning on and on in "high Masses" and "low Masses" for which some poor widow had to pay in order to get her dead husband out of Purgatory. If you think that comparison is farfetched, look over at Matthew 23:8--9, where such religious leaders took the title of ["father." ]{.class_4723}The difference between Jews and Catholics is no Jew would think of doing what a Roman Catholic Pope does: [usurp the title of God the Father]{.class_4725} (see our remarks on John 17:11 in that Commentary).

45 Then answered one of the lawyers, and said unto him, Master, thus saying thou reproachest us also.

46 And he said, Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.

47 Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_364}prophets, and your fathers killed them.

48 Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.

49 Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute:

50 That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

51 From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.

We have already commented on ["lawyers"]{.class_4723} under 10:25 (which see). One time I was invited out to eat by a lawyer in Panama City, Florida. He ordered two lobster dinners for us, and as the food was being served, I brought up the subject of religion. He was a Methodist who knew little to nothing about the Bible, so to get an inroad to talking to Him about Christ, I said, "Did you know the Bible talks about lawyers?"

"It does?" he said, "What does it say about us?"

And I hauled out my New Testament and opened it to Luke 11:46 and read him, ["Woe unto you also, ye lawyers!"]{.class_4723} That lawyer got so interested in what I was saying that he didn't touch his lobster dinner. For twenty minutes I sat there and ate my lobster and answered his Bible questions. I finished my meal, and he bowed his head over the lobster dinner he hadn't touched and received Christ as his Saviour.

Now what was the problem with the lawyers? First of all, they created heavy, grievous burdens for the people to bear by adding [tradition]{.class_4725} to the Law (Mark 7:7--13)---another mark of Roman Catholicism. Second, they built monuments to the prophets their forefathers killed, while bragging that they themselves would not have done it (Matt. 23:30). Of course, they have been plotting since 6:11 how they can put Jesus to death. They aligned themselves with their fathers when it came to killing God's prophets, even as they []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_365}tried to distance themselves. How different from Daniel (Dan. 9:3--19) and Nehemiah (Neh. 1:4--11) who took their place with the idolatrous Israelites of 1 & 2 Kings in repentance and humility, and received forgiveness and blessing from the Lord.

["The wisdom of God"]{.class_4723}[ ]{.class_4737}in verse 49 is obviously a reference to the word of God, but the quote is not anything you will find in the Old Testament. That being the case, the best explanation is that it is a reference to Jesus Christ as God's wisdom incarnate (see our comments on 7:35).

The Lord puts a pretty hefty "blood guilt" on those Jews. He says, ["the blood of ALL the prophets, which was shed from the FOUNDATION OF THE WORLD]{.class_4723} \[Gen. 1:1\][, may be required of this generation"]{.class_4723} (vs. 50). He puts that on them even though they only ["slay and persecute" "SOME"]{.class_4723} of the ["prophets and apostles"]{.class_4723} sent to them (vs. 49). If there is any doubt that Jesus means exactly what He says, look at verse 51: ["from the blood of ABEL."]{.class_4723} That's the first "prophet" ever slain in the Bible, and God attributes his blood guilt to the generation of Jews to whom Christ is speaking. How could that be?! How could the blood of every righteous man slain from Genesis to 2 Chronicles (the Jewish arrangement of the canon of the Old Testament) be attributed to [one]{.class_4725} generation?

There's another verse just like it in Revelation 18:25. The Roman Whore (see our comments on Rev. 17 in that Commentary) is said to be responsible for ["the blood of prophets, and of saints, and of all that were slain upon the earth."]{.class_4723} Of course, that might be a reference to the ones that die in the Tribulation, but over in Revelation 17:6, it says that ["woman"]{.class_4723} is ["drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus."]{.class_4723}

So what you have here are two cities---[Jerusalem and Rome]{.class_4725}---that are responsible for the deaths of saints in both Testaments: Jerusalem is responsible for the deaths of the [Old Testament]{.class_4725} saints and prophets, and Rome is responsible for the deaths of the [New Testament]{.class_4725} saints and prophets. Both cities are part of a religious system that relies on good works [apart from what God said]{.class_4725} for salvation (Gen. 4:3--7; Mark 7:7--13), that has priests called "fathers" []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_366}(Judg. 17:10; Matt. 23:9), that involves idolatry (Judg. 18:17--19; 2 Kings 10:19--22), and that makes long prayers of ["vain repetitions"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 23:14, 6:7). And both cities are responsible for [the death of Jesus Christ:]{.class_4725} Jerusalem turned Him over for execution, and Rome carried it out. It was the [Romans]{.class_4725} who nailed Him, not the Jews; it was the [Romans]{.class_4725} who put the crown of thorns on His head, not the Jews; it was the [Romans]{.class_4725} who stole His clothes, not the Jews; it was the [Romans]{.class_4725} who put the spear in Him, not the Jews. Nevertheless, the Jews were the ones who took Him and delivered Him to Pilate and demanded that He be crucified.

Now what's going on with Jerusalem and Rome is that when they crucify Jesus Christ, they became responsible for the blood of the saints in both directions---past and future---because those saints were connected with God's Son. In the Parable of the Vineyard in Matthew 21, Jesus makes it clear that all the Old Testament prophets were forerunners of ["the heir"]{.class_4723} to the vineyard. In Matthew 23:37, those prophets were Christ's attempt to gather those Jews under His ["wings,"]{.class_4723} and they would have none of it. In the New Testament, after Pentecost, those believers are part of Christ's Body (John 17:21; 1 Cor. 12:12--27; Col. 1:24--27), so when a Christian is persecuted, it is actually Jesus Christ being persecuted and vice versa (Acts 9:5). So those Jews and Romans become responsible for the blood guilt of the forerunners and followers of Jesus when they crucify Christ. That's why Jesus says ["THIS generation"]{.class_4723} in verses 50--51 here in Luke 11: He's talking about the generation that had Him crucified.

We will have more to say about the Old Testament canon under 24:44, so we will forego any further comments on verse 51 until we get to that passage.

52 Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.

53 And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge him vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:

54 Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_367}out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.

Verse 52 is the third ["woe"]{.class_4723} placed upon the lawyers (see our remarks under vs. 46). This time they took away ["the key of knowledge."]{.class_4723} That's something that could have opened the truth to a sinner, and they took it away so that it couldn't be found. And if that were not enough, when someone did find the door to the truth open, they stopped him from going in: ["ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered."]{.class_4723}

A key, in the Scriptures, is given to someone to open and close something. A key is also a symbol of government (as in "the key to the city"). Back in ancient times, the keeper of the key to a city had the key fastened in a shoulder strap, like an epaulet on a military uniform. That explains the statement about Christ in Isaiah 9:6---["and the government shall be upon his shoulder."]{.class_4723}

Now there are several keys found throughout the Scriptures. The first is in Isaiah 22:21--22.

["And I will clothe him]{.class_4723} \[a prophetic reference to Christ\][ with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah. And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder ]{.class_4723}\[see our remarks above\][; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open."]{.class_4723}

That's the ["key"]{.class_4723} He uses on the church at Philadelphia in Revelation 3:7--8.

"And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write; These things saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the KEY OF DAVID, he that openeth, and no man shutteth; and shutteth, and no man openeth; I know thy works: behold, I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it: for thou hast a little strength, and hast kept my word, and hast not denied my name."

Now what's that ["open door"]{.class_4723}? It's an [opportunity to preach]{.class_4725} (1 Cor. 16:9). When Peter is given the keys to the Kingdom of Heaven in Matthew 16:19, he isn't given the keys to Heaven. Simon Peter isn't at the "pearly gates" waiting to let you in. Those keys were []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_368}opportunities to preach and open the Kingdom of Heaven to the Jews in Acts 2 and then to the Gentiles in Acts 10.

Bob Jones Sr. used to say that when God sets an open door before you, go through it. It doesn't mean that you won't get [kicked]{.class_4725} going through it or that you won't get [cussed]{.class_4725} going through it or that someone won't try to [prevent]{.class_4725} you from going through it. But if God sets an open door before you, go through it.

Now, God set ["an open door"]{.class_4723} before that Philadelphian church because they [kept His word.]{.class_4725} It's the only church out of the seven in Revelation 2--3 about which God said that. That's the period of church history from 1500--1900. That's Martin Luther and the Reformation; that's the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible and the British Empire taking it to every corner of the globe. That's the era of the great revivals, and the mission fields opening all over the world. Those Christians kept God's word in the [King James]{.class_4725} Bible and other faithful translations like Luther's [Heilige Schrift]{.class_4725} and the [Valera]{.class_4725} Bible in Spanish and the [AV]{.class_4725} text translated into 160 major languages and over 200 minor ones. When the British and Foreign Bible Society dumped the [AV]{.class_4725} text for that of the [RV, ASV, RSV, NIV,]{.class_4725} etc., God began shutting the doors; and the church entered the greatest age of apostasy it had ever known---Laodicea.

Your job, Christian, is to go through whatever door the Lord has opened for you and perform that task He has given you to do---regardless of results or consequences---until He calls you home. I heard Bob Jones Sr. say on many an occasion: "All my life I have been going through doors and cleaning up rooms. Sometimes it has been a big door to a big room; sometimes it has been a little door to a little room. But I've gone through each door and cleaned up each room the best way I knew how and went on to the next one. Sometimes there would be a big mess when I went into a room, and sometimes it stayed a mess after I left."

"All my life I have just been walking from room to room. One day I'll be standing in a room getting ready to work, and about that time it will get dark. I'll say to myself, 'It isn't anywhere near night time; it's the middle of the day. I don't know why it's so dark in here.'"

"I'll look around that place, and there won't be any windows. But then a little door will open up in the ceiling, and a light will come []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_369}through it into that room. I will hear a voice say, 'Bob, you've been cleaning a long time, and I've been building you something up here. Now it's time to come on up here.' Then I'll go up through that door into that room, and I won't have to clean anything else ever again."

And to that I say, "AMEN! Let 'er rip." I'm ready to go home and leave the whole mess behind for someone else to clean up. But until that happens, I'll keep going through those doors God opens to me and doing what He's called me to do.

Now here are some more keys, and these are keys the Pope has [stolen]{.class_4725} from Jesus Christ. On the Pope's coat of arms, you will see two keys crossed. That was the symbol of the god Janus back in pagan Rome which the Pope converted into a symbol of authority for him and his church by twisting two passages of Scripture. The first is Revelation 1:18.

"I am he that liveth, and was dead; and, behold, I am alive for evermore, Amen; and have the keys of hell and of death."

Those are keys to a place with ["bars"]{.class_4723} and ["gates"]{.class_4723} (see our remarks under vs. 29). That's why over in Matthew 16:18, Jesus told Simon Peter: ["upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it."]{.class_4723} That's not the Church trying to break into Hell. Who would be stupid enough to want to do that? That's talking about the ["rock"]{.class_4723} (Jesus Christ---1 Cor. 10:4; 1 Pet. 2:6--8) busting out at His resurrection. So the new "Bibles" will change ["gates of hell"]{.class_4723} to ["powers of hell"]{.class_4725} to take the credit away from Jesus Christ and give it to a bunch of sinners (saved or lost depending on how you define "church" there)---anything to obscure the truth.

It's [Jesus Christ]{.class_4725} who has the keys of Hell and death. [He's]{.class_4725} the only one who can get you out of Hell and bring you up after you die; [not]{.class_4725} the Roman Catholic Church or any other "church."

The second set of keys the Pope usurps are those ["keys of the kingdom of heaven"]{.class_4723} given to Simon Peter in Matthew 16:19. Those are keys to a [physical]{.class_4725} kingdom set up when Jesus Christ comes back as King. When Peter gets up in the first part of the book of Acts, he's preaching the Second Advent and the Millennial reign of Christ (see Acts 2:16--19, 3:19--21 and our comments on those []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_370}passages in that Commentary). He knows nothing about the Church Age or ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4723} (Acts 20:24) at all. He tells those Jews if they want to get into that physical kingdom they were promised back there in the Old Testament, they had better repent of crucifying their Messiah and go down and get under that water and come up again to get the Holy Ghost (Acts 2:38). He's about to do the same thing to those Gentiles in Acts 10 when the Lord stops him before he can get to the baptism part (Acts 10:42--47). When James is commenting on Peter opening the door to the Gentiles, do you know what he does? He takes you right to a [Second Advent]{.class_4725} passage on the gathering of the Gentiles during the [Millennium]{.class_4725} (see Acts 10:14--17 and comments in that Commentary). When Paul preaches to those Gentiles, he doesn't preach a physical kingdom on this earth; he preaches a spiritual kingdom---the Kingdom of God (Acts 20:25).

Not having the Bible knowledge of a child in daily vacation Bible school, the Pope claims Peter's "keys" as the [spiritual]{.class_4725} [and temporal]{.class_4725} [authority]{.class_4725} for him and his church; that is, the Pope can not only tell you what to do to get to Heaven, he can tell your President, Chairman, Prime Minister, King, Queen, Congress, Parliament, etc., how to run their governments in line with the dictates of the Roman Catholic Church. That's how Europe got in the mess she was in in the Dark Ages, the Napoleonic Wars, and World War I and World War II. Napoleon, Mussolini, Hitler, and all those European dictators were either raised Roman Catholic or Greek Orthodox (the eastern branch of the Catholic Church), and several of them had concordats with the Pope and the church recognizing the Roman Catholic Church as the only legitimate religious body in their countries. That's the Roman Catholic Church. They haven't changed one bit in 1,500 years; they just have to be more subtle about it now than before because both America and Europe have become more secularized, making it more difficult to get away with their devilment openly.

Now, Christ has been lambasting the scribes, Pharisees, and lawyers for fourteen verses; and you can imagine how they must be feeling. They're mad enough to chew up nails and spit out the rust. They're [livid,]{.class_4725} boy. The Bible says they ["began to urge him VEHEMENTLY"]{.class_4723} (vs. 53): they're [violent]{.class_4725} in their provocation.

They try []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_371}["to provoke him to speak of many things"]{.class_4723} because ["in the multitude of words there wanteth not sin"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 10:19). They are trying to catch Him in His words ["that they might accuse him"]{.class_4723} (vs. 54). They're ["laying wait for him"]{.class_4723}: it's a [trap,]{.class_4725} an [ambush.]{.class_4725}

That's the way most congregations are. Most Christians don't go to church to hear the word of God and apply what they hear; they go to church with a chip on their shoulder, waiting for the pastor to knock it off. They figure that sooner or later the preacher will say something with which they don't agree so they can get mad and have an alibi to do what they want to do, regardless of what God said.

Moses faced this problem back in the Old Testament. Psalm 106:32--33 says, ["They angered him also at the waters of strife, so that it went ill with Moses for their sakes. Because they provoked his spirit, so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips."]{.class_4723}

The children of Israel got to griping and arguing with Moses there in Numbers 20:3--5 until it drove him to distraction. God told him to speak to the rock. Moses marched out to those people who were still murmuring, griping, and complaining; and he "loses it." He stood beside that rock just as mad as a hornet and yelled, ["Hear now, ye rebels; must we fetch you water out of this rock?"]{.class_4723} (Num. 20:10), and WHAM! He struck that rock and lost the chance to get into the Promised Land.

If you are a preacher, you know how Moses felt if you have any red blood in you. You get a bunch of people sitting listening to you for the sole purpose of finding a chink in your armor just so they can reject what you say. You stand there week after week trying to give them the truth, and finally your patience runs out. You figure, "All right, if they won't take it the way I've been giving it to them, I'll just let them have it with both barrels," and then you say something you probably shouldn't say.

Martin Luther was bad about that sort of thing. Those Catholics would ride him and ride him about works salvation, and he would finally break down and say things that anyone could use to hang him. He would say, "James is a right straw epistle, and some day I'm going to light my stove with James." Well, he didn't mean that; after all, he []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_372}translated the book and put it in his Bible. But he got tired of the Catholics ignoring Ephesians 2:8--9 and Titus 3:5 and throwing James 2:24 in his face.

Luther would say, "Every Christian ought to be a good, healthy sinner. Every Christian ought to have a good mug of beer once in a while and trifle with a young maiden just so the Devil doesn't get an advantage over him." Don't you know the Catholics used [that]{.class_4725} against him. But Luther was trying to drive home the point that salvation is by grace through faith, and your conduct cannot affect your salvation. But what a way to drive it home.

I have written enough (and spoken enough) that if one of my enemies wants to hang me, they won't have any trouble getting the rope out of my own works. I get to reading all these Bible correctors, and I get provoked, boy, I kid you not. I'd be a nice fellow if I just didn't read so much. But I get to reading these high-degreed jackrabbits attacking what God said and changing what God said, and I get upset. Occasionally I make what some would call an "overstatement" (though nowheres near as often as most people think). My enemies will take those things and magnify them so they can go right on attacking the Book and changing it to suit themselves. But they won't dare confront me face-to-face on what I say because I scare them.

I have held Bible conferences within ten miles of Tennessee Temple and Bob Jones and BBC and Hyles-Anderson where all the "big boys" are. The pastor advertised the meeting ahead of time and invited anyone in the community who wanted to come to attend an "open Bible forum" where they could ask any question of me they wanted: if they wanted to ask something about my personal life, they could. Do you think those professors and Ph.D.s and faculty members showed up? Not on your life, brother; they never showed their yellow-bellied snoots. Why? Because they knew I would kick them a country mile. But I guarantee you they talked about me in the classroom to their students and kept on taking away their students' faith in the Book. Those are the ones who don't like Ruckman's "crude language"; they're a bunch of lily-livered [cowards.]{.class_4725}

Now the Pharisees, scribes, and lawyers lay into Christ here in []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2278.xhtml_page_373}verses 53--54, and they don't let up until Luke 20:40. During all that time the Lord "kept His cool" and watched His tongue. When He finally "cuts loose" in Matthew 23, He says nothing wrong or unscriptural. When those Jewish leaders finally haul Him before Pilate, they have to [lie]{.class_4725} to bring an accusation against Him (see 23:1--2 and our comments on that passage).

Christian, there's nothing wrong with being angry or rebuking someone[ "sharply"]{.class_4723} (see Tit. 1:13) as long as you don't [sin]{.class_4725} while you're doing it (Eph. 4:26). The trick is to watch what you say so that no one can [rightly]{.class_4725} accuse you (they're going to accuse you of something anyway; just make sure they don't have a legitimate accusation). Jesus Christ did just that.

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml}

# Chapter 12 {.heading_78-0}

12:1 In the mean time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

2 For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known.

3 Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.

["Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy"]{.class_4723} (vs. 1). The verse is absolutely critical to understanding what you are about to read in the next chapter (13:20--21). ["Leaven,"]{.class_4723} in the Bible, has nothing to do with the Gospel. Paul warns you twice in his epistles about ["leaven"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 5:6; Gal. 5:9). He commands you to ["purge out\...the old leaven"]{.class_4723} because the Christian is supposed to be ["unleavened"]{.class_4723} like the Saviour who was crucified for him (1 Cor. 5:7). The Scriptures define ["leaven"]{.class_4723} as ["hypocrisy"]{.class_4723} (here), [false doctrine]{.class_4725} (Matt. 16:12), and ["malice and wickedness"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 5:8).

Now, in the companion passage in Matthew 16:12, ["the leaven of the Pharisees"]{.class_4723} is defined as ["the doctrine of the Pharisees."]{.class_4723} The Pharisees were "orthodox" in their acceptance of the Old Testament. They believed in spirits, angels, and the resurrection; whereas the Sadducees did not (Acts 23:8). As we just saw in 11:42, they were meticulous about such things as tithing. The problem with the Pharisees was that they [added tradition to the Scriptures.]{.class_4725}

["Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men"]{.class_4723} (Mark 7:7).

The purpose of this was to [profess]{.class_4725} belief in the Scriptures while having an excuse to [break]{.class_4725} them (Mark 7:13)---[hypocrisy.]{.class_4725}

The word [hypocrite]{.class_4725} is a very interesting word in Greek. It means []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_375}"an actor," like the people you see on TV and in the movies. It's someone who [pretends]{.class_4725} to be something he is [not.]{.class_4725} John Wayne didn't fight at the Alamo or in World War II or in Vietnam. "John Wayne" wasn't even his real name; it was [Marion Morrison]{.class_4725} (how's that for a manly name!). I have a list of 66 names in the back of the [Ruckman Reference Bible]{.class_4725} (App. 82) of TV and movie stars who have "aliases": names they assumed so people wouldn't know who they really were. Danny Thomas wasn't "Danny Thomas"; he was [Amos Alphonsus Muzyad Yaqoob.]{.class_4725} How would that look on a movie marquis? "Stevie Wonder" (a black singer) was really [Stevland Judkins,]{.class_4725} but who's going to buy a record from Stevland Judkins? No one's going to pay any attention to [Caryn Johnson,]{.class_4725} but they will to "Whoopi Goldberg."

Brethren, there are three groups of people who assume aliases: actors, gangsters, and Popes. "Birds of a feather flock together"; all three groups are just as crooked as a dog's hind leg. Pope Benedict XVI isn't "Benedict" at all; he's [John Ratzinger.]{.class_4725} John Paul II wasn't an Englishman; he was a Communist Pollock (Karol Wojtyla). But you see, names like Ratzinger, Wojtyla, and Pacelli (Pius XII) don't make for good press, so they change them to make them sound "holy" or to associate themselves with some "saint" of the past. They're [hypocrites.]{.class_4725}

["For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known"]{.class_4723} (vs. 2). What is covered up and hidden away on the inside (see 11:29 and our comments) is going to come out at the judgment.

["For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every SECRET THING, whether it be good, or whether it be evil"]{.class_4723} (Eccl. 12:14).

["In the day when God shall judge the SECRETS of men by Jesus Christ according to my gospel"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 2:16).

Do you see now why the UN and the NEA and the ACLU and the Congress and the Supreme Court don't want that Book around? They've thrown it out of the public schools and the courtrooms so that no one will [see]{.class_4725} it, [think]{.class_4725} about it, and [obey]{.class_4725} it. What do you suppose would happen if the verses I just gave you were posted in the hallways of every high school in the United States? Why, it would cause a panic. Either those kids would get scared and straighten up, []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_376}or they'd get mad and tear them down or vandalize them. Either way, [the government doesn't want your children getting the truth.]{.class_4725}

You never put your hand anywhere that the Lord didn't know you put it. You never went anywhere that the Lord wasn't aware you were there. I don't care how dark it was or how loud the music or how thick the smoke, there is no secret thing hidden from God. One of these days, young man, young woman, God will peel back the roof of that car you were in last Saturday night and let the whole universe see what you did there. That Bible says, ["The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 15:3).

One of these days, everything is going to come out. Do you want to know what really happened to Jimmy Hoffa? You'll find out. God will play it back in "3-D," "hi-fi," "high definition," and all that stuff. You'll find out who really shot Kennedy and why he did it. There's NOTHING that won't be revealed; the whole thing will come out.

["Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops"]{.class_4723} (vs. 3). Jesus said, ["every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 12:36).

Now let me ask you this: do you reckon you'll pass a judgment like that on your own? Most people think they are good enough to make it by the judgment, and if they do have some small vices, God will overlook them. But they aren't thinking the thing through clearly, and the reason they don't think it through is because they [know]{.class_4725} they won't make it. We are not talking about a human judge or jury here; we are talking about Someone who knows every secret thing you ever did, said, and even [thought.]{.class_4725} He said, ["I know the things that come into your mind, EVERY ONE OF THEM"]{.class_4723} (Ezek. 11:5).

When I go into those prisons and preach to the guys "in stir," I tell them, "Gentlemen, if I had to give account to God for everything I've thought in the last 24 hours, I'd go to Hell like a bullet." Amen! So would your preacher, so would your father, so would your mother, so would your grandparents, and [so would you.]{.class_4725} Don't kid []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_377}yourself in these matters. The best thing you can do is put all your skeletons in the closet, shut the door and lock it, and give the key to the Lord Jesus Christ. I [knew]{.class_4725} I wouldn't pass such a judgment; that's why I received Christ the fourteenth of March, 1949.

4 And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.

5 But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.

A man is what he [loves]{.class_4725} and what he [fears.]{.class_4725} The Bible says, ["The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 29:25). If you are trusting the Lord, there is no need to fear what any man can do to you (Psa. 56:4, 11, 118:6; Dan. 3:16--18; Heb. 13:6). Someone threatened to kill John R. Rice once for something he said in a meeting, and Rice told that fellow, "You can't threaten me with Heaven."

Now notice the contrast between the fear of man in verse 4 and the fear of God in verse 5. The standard explanation of the fear of the Lord by the Fundamentalists and Evangelicals is that the fear of God is a "reverential trust." Not only is that an unscriptural definition (see Heb. 12:28 and our comments on Heb. 11:7 and Prov. 1:7 in those Commentaries), but it flies completely in the face of the context. Jesus wasn't talking about any "reverential trust" in verse 4; why would He switch gears in verse 5? Christ is giving you a [warning]{.class_4725} in verse 5. A warning about what? A warning about a Being so powerful that not only can He drop you dead with a thought, He can pitch you into a Lake of Fire where you will never get out (Mark 9:44, 46, 48). You reckon that's Someone to be scared of?

At this point, I can hear the whines of: "But oh, Bro. Ruckman, you shouldn't [fear]{.class_4725} God; you should [love]{.class_4725} Him. Doesn't the Bible say, ['There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love']{.class_4723} (1 John 4:18)? Blah, blah, blah, blah, blah." Every Charismatic, Liberal, and Fundamentalist who quotes that verse to []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_378}prove a born-again child of God shouldn't fear God ignores the context of the passage. The verse right before it (1 John 4:17) is talking about having ["boldness in the day of judgment"]{.class_4723} because you are in Christ and have eternal life as a present possession. The ["fear"]{.class_4723} that is "cast out" by ["perfect love"]{.class_4723} is the fear of [going to Hell after you have trusted Jesus Christ as your Saviour.]{.class_4725} As a Christian, you may have a lot of things to fear, and rightfully so (see Heb. 12:28; Phil. 2:12; 2 Cor. 5:11), but losing your salvation is not one of them.

Brethren, that Bible says, ["the fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 111:10; Prov. 9:10). If you want wisdom, I'll tell you how to get it; it's a very simple thing. Just find out what to be afraid of and what not to be afraid of. That's all wisdom is; that's all there is to it. Wisdom is knowing when to be scared and when not to be scared. If you are afraid of something you shouldn't fear, you aren't wise; if you aren't afraid of something that you should fear, you aren't wise. If you fear man instead of God, you aren't wise, and if you don't fear God when you should, you are a fool.

These days the queers talk about straights having "homophobia." (I tell them I don't have homophobia; I have [homonausea:]{.class_4725} faggots make me [sick.]{.class_4725}) Well, if you did, that would be a healthy fear if you value your [family]{.class_4725} and your [life]{.class_4725} (see Gen. 19; Judg. 19). The Moslems talk about the people who oppose their murderous religion as having "Islamophobia" when their "holy book," the [Koran,]{.class_4725} commands them to spread [terror]{.class_4725} among anyone who is not a Mohammedan (Sura 17:59, 8:60, 33:26). The same people who "accept" the queers and "accept" the Moslems and "accept" Marxism and "accept" the Kinsey Report are [scared to death of the Bible.]{.class_4725} That's why they can't stand to have it read or displayed in public. They have [Bibliophobia.]{.class_4725} Those are fools who are afraid of the wrong thing.

You say, "Well how can you fear God and love Him at the same time?" If you had a good father, you know exactly how that works. You could love your daddy and look forward to seeing him each day when he got home from work; but if you had done something wrong where your momma had to tell you "Just wait till your father gets home," you didn't look forward to seeing him then. You were scared []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_379}of the belt coming out.

["Fear him, which AFTER he hath killed hath power to cast into hell"]{.class_4723} (vs. 5). Then your Hell is [not]{.class_4725} here on this earth; it comes after you die. One of the saddest things, in my estimation, is to go through trouble and tragedy on this earth and then die lost and wind up in Hell on top of that. I can hardly imagine it, but that's what happened to a lot of those Jews who died in the concentration camps during World War II. They went through "hell on earth" and then died and found themselves in a literal Hell. If you are lost, you had better fear the One who can tell you, ["Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 25:41). You had better get scared enough to do what He tells you to do to get out.

6 Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?

7 But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.

Now how's that for a "contradiction" in the Bible. Jesus just got through telling you to fear God because He can throw you into Hell, and now He says not to fear because God thinks more of you than He does a flock of birds. There's a verse like that in the Old Testament. The Lord comes down on Mt. Sinai in fire and smoke and thunder (Exod. 19:18--19, 20:18), and all those Israelites are standing there just shaking in their boots (Heb. 12:21). They're so scared that they come to Moses and say, "You talk to God for us, but don't let God speak to us directly, or we'll die" (Exod. 20:19). Do you know what Moses tells them? He says, "Don't be scared; God just came down to scare the fire out of you." Read it.

["And Moses said unto the people, FEAR NOT: for God is come to prove you, and that HIS FEAR MAY BE BEFORE YOUR FACES, that ye sin not"]{.class_4723} (Exod. 20:20).

God wants you scared, but He doesn't want you paralyzed with fear. His fear is to [help you.]{.class_4725} He wants the fear that will drive you to Him for mercy. You see, God is holy; He can't put up with your sin. []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_380}At the same time, He is love and doesn't want to send you to Hell. Nevertheless, He won't sin to keep you out of Hell. God's love doesn't guarantee you a free pass into Heaven. The death of Christ satisfies both attributes of God: it satisfies His holiness and justice by paying the penalty for your sins, and it satisfies His love and compassion by offering you salvation as a free gift. But you are never going to get to Heaven if you don't fear God enough to realize you are a sinner under the just condemnation of God for your sin and then take the only means out of that condemnation which He provided for you---the Lord Jesus Christ (John 3:18, 36).

8 Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:

9 But he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of God.

Now both of these verses are pre-Crucifixion verses, so you may not be able to apply them in a strict doctrinal sense, but you certainly can [practically.]{.class_4725} Technically speaking, you do not have to confess Christ publicly during an invitation, but it will help you if you do. Confessing Christ openly will settle things in your mind so that, later on, when the Devil works you over to get you to doubt your salvation (and he certainly will), you can point back to that time when you confessed Christ and say, "I know I did it [right there."]{.class_4725}

That was my case. Hugh Pyle led me to Christ in the record room of WEAR on the corner of Gregory and Palafox in downtown Pensacola on March 14, 1949. But it wasn't until the following Saturday, when I responded to an invitation at the Brent Baptist Church in a youth rally, and went forward and made a public profession of my faith, that I got assurance of my salvation.

Denying Christ in this dispensation (the Church Age) will not cause you to lose your salvation or even prove you were never saved to begin with. Simon Peter denied the Lord three times, and he didn't lose his salvation. But did you notice there in John 21 that Jesus made him openly confess his love three times to restore his [discipleship.]{.class_4725} This side of Calvary, confessing and denying Christ have to do with [fellowship, service,]{.class_4725} and [rewards;]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ2484.xhtml_page_381} not salvation. Look at 2 Timothy 2:12--13.

["If we suffer, we shall also REIGN WITH HIM ]{.class_4736}\[a Millennial reward for faithful service\][: if we deny him, he also will deny us]{.class_4736} \[["deny us"]{.class_4736} what? Salvation? No! Look at the next verse\][: If we believe not ]{.class_4736}\[what John Wesley and the old-time Methodists would call "apostatizing from the faith"\][, YET HE ABIDETH FAITHFUL: HE CANNOT DENY HIMSELF."]{.class_4736}

If you are saved, Christ is in you and you are in Him, and He isn't going to kick you out of His Body no matter what (Rom. 8:38--39; Eph. 4:30). But He will deny you a Millennial reign with Him. Denying Christ can result in [loss of rewards,]{.class_4725} but [not]{.class_4725} loss of salvation (2 John 8).

10 And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Ghost it shall not be forgiven.

This is the famous "unpardonable sin" that all the Charismatics and Pentecostals fear committing. The Fundamentalists aren't worried about that, but they will say it is rejecting Christ for the final time.
