When May 21 rolled around, his followers met together in little groups here and there eating pizza and [drinking beer.]{.class_4725} The only difference between Camping's followers and the skeptics and infidels holding "Rapture parties" was that the latter group did their drinking in [bars.]{.class_4725}

Now Christian, you are to live in such a way that when the Lord returns, He finds you busy doing what He called you to do. Someone asked Martin Luther what he would do if he knew the Lord was coming back tomorrow. Luther replied, "I'd go hoe my potato patch today." In other words, if you are living right, there's no need to change what you're doing in expectancy of the Lord's return.

Bible school students always have a bad problem in this regard. They come to school and hear a message on the Second Coming and figure they have no time to "waste" in school; they have to get out in the work and do something before the Lord comes back. Most of them who do that do something for a couple of years and then drop completely out of sight; you never hear from them again. Why is that? Because they stopped doing what the Lord called them to do. The Lord wanted them to train for the ministry, and they wouldn't do it. As a result, they made a big mess of things.

Has the Lord called you to Bible school? Then [finish!]{.class_4725} Don't worry about not getting anything done if He should come back while you are in school. The Lord isn't looking for "results"; He's looking for [faithfulness.]{.class_4725} He's looking to see if you're doing what He told you to be doing. You let the Lord worry about the results (1 Cor. 3:6--7).

Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "The best preparation for tomorrow is to do what you ought to do today." If you do today what you should do, you'll be in the center of the will of God tomorrow. The problem is today is a [mess.]{.class_4725} You glamorize tomorrow, thinking it will be better, but tomorrow is built on what you do [today.]{.class_4725}

Verse 14: ["But his citizens hated him."]{.class_4723} That's true of the [world]{.class_4725} (John 15:18), as well as the [Jews]{.class_4725} (John 15:24). The lost world hates your Saviour; and when push comes to shove, the mask of []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_556}politeness, courtesy, and tolerance comes off to reveal the malice beneath.

["We will not have this man to reign over us."]{.class_4723} Who said that? The man's ["citizens"]{.class_4723}---the ones over whom he had lawful rule. In the application of the parable, these would be the [Jews,]{.class_4725} Christ's own people. John wrote, ["He came unto his own, and his own received him not"]{.class_4723} (John 1:11).

David prophesied, ["The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed]{.class_4723} \[that's Christ; the word means "anointed one"\][, saying, Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 2:3--4). Peter applies that to the Jews turning Jesus over to Herod and Pilate to be crucified (Acts 4:25--28). Those Jews told Pontius Pilate, ["We have no king but Caesar"]{.class_4723} (John 19:15). They didn't want Jesus Christ ruling over them, so they got a line of demon-possessed mad men (Tiberius, Nero, Caligula, the Popes, Hitler, et al.).

Verse 15: ["And it came to pass, that when he was returned]{.class_4723} \[the Second Advent\][, having received the kingdom]{.class_4723} \[in the context, the Kingdom of God\][, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him\...that he might know how much every man had gained by trading."]{.class_4723} Now this might have some reference doctrinally to a judgment of the Tribulation witnesses (Rev. 7) that takes place on the ground at the Second Advent, but you can certainly apply it to the Judgment Seat of Christ. Unlike the Parable of the Talents, no servant in this story goes to Hell. That's the Christian's case at the Judgment Seat of Christ: his works burn up but [he does not]{.class_4725} (1 Cor. 3:15). All the wicked servant in this parable loses is [rewards,]{.class_4725} just as the Christian does at the Judgment Seat of Christ.

Now the Lord of these servants singles out three of the ten just as he does over in the Parable of the Talents, but here you have something not mentioned in Matthew 25. The faithful servants are given rule over cities in their Lord's new kingdom (vss. 17, 19). Over in Revelation 20:6, you are told that those who have ["part in the first resurrection\...shall be priests of God and of Christ, and shall reign with him a thousand years."]{.class_4723} That includes [you]{.class_4725} if you have been ["washed in the blood of the Lamb"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_557} (see Rev. 1:5--6).

Paul calls it an inheritance (1 Cor. 6:9; Gal. 5:21) that you get for suffering for Jesus Christ (2 Tim. 2:12). I used to have a friend in the ministry who said that a lot of Christians don't serve Jesus Christ enough to be appointed dog catcher in the Millennium.

Notice that the levels of trading match the levels of fruit-bearing in John 15: ["fruit"]{.class_4723} (John 15:2), ["more fruit"]{.class_4723} (John 15:2), ["much fruit"]{.class_4723} (John 15:5). Over in the Parable of the Sower, it was thirty, sixty, and hundredfold (Mark 4:20). The first servant turns his pound into ten pounds; the second servant turns his pound into five. What should have followed was the third servant turning his pound into two, but that's not what happened.

The third servant doesn't trade his master's pound at all. He wraps it up in a napkin and holds onto it until his Lord returns. His excuse for not doing what his Lord [commanded]{.class_4725} him to do (vs. 13) was: ["I feared thee"]{.class_4723} (vs. 21).

Well, there's nothing wrong with that. You should fear God (1 Pet. 2:17). But this wasn't the right kind of fear. Paul says, ["perfecting holiness in the fear of God"]{.class_4736} (2 Cor. 7:1). A proper fear of God should lead you to do [right,]{.class_4733} not wrong.

["I feared thee, because thou art an austere man."]{.class_4723} The word ["austere"]{.class_4723} means "severe, harsh, stern." This servant is accusing his master of being too hard and unreasonable with him. So the fear of this servant is a bitter, resentful kind of fear.

["Thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow."]{.class_4723} He's accusing the master of wrongdoing. He's saying, "We do all the work, and you get all the benefits." You see how bitter and resentful that accusation is?

Now, it's not true. The master obviously had enough respect and confidence in these servants to entrust them with his money. The master was obviously lavish in rewarding success, no matter how little it was: he turned over whole cities to his servants for making only five to ten pounds. If this fellow had only made two pounds, he would have gotten to rule two cities. How stern of a master could he be?

The Lord of this servant judges him by his own words (vs. 22). []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_558}That's what happened to David in 2 Samuel 12 and Ahab in 1 Kings 20. Jesus said, ["For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 12:37).

The master doesn't deny anything the servant said about him. Instead, he turns it right back around on the servant and says, "If that's the case, then why didn't you do this?" If the wicked servant really didn't want to be bothered with doing what his master told him to do, the least he could have done was stick the money in the bank and let it draw interest (vs. 23). (The Bible word is ["usury,"]{.class_4723} which is actually a better word. The bank is paying you to [use]{.class_4725} your money, and paying you a pittance at that. I don't know why they call it "interest." I'm not interested in it. They never pay you as much interest in your savings account as they charge you for taking out a loan.)

Now as we have said, this wicked servant doesn't go to Hell like the one in the Parable of the Talents (see Matt. 25:30). All he suffers is loss of rewards. It is the citizens who don't want the nobleman to reign over them who are slain (vs. 27). That is what Jesus Christ does at the Second Advent to those who reject the Gospel (2 Thess. 1:8--9).

For verses 24--26, see our comments on 8:18.

28 And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.

29 And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called the mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

30 Saying, Go ye into the village over against you; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring him hither.

31 And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.

32 And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.

33 And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?

34 And they said, The Lord hath need of him.

35 And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.

36 And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.

37 And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;

38 Saying, Blessed be the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.

39 And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.

40 And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.

This is Luke's account of the Triumphal Entry. Note the expression ["ascending up to Jerusalem"]{.class_4723} in verse 28. No matter from which direction you come in Palestine---even north to south---you always go [up]{.class_4725} to Jerusalem because Jerusalem is the high ground west of the Jordan.

Notice that Christ approaches Jerusalem from the east, from the Mount of Olives. That's where He left to return to Heaven (Acts 1:9--12), and that's where He's coming back (Zech. 14:3--4). So what you are reading here is a picture of Christ's Second Advent. That is why Christ sends [two]{.class_4725} disciples to fetch the colt. There were [two]{.class_4725} faithful spies who gave a good report in Numbers 13--14, there were [two]{.class_4725} angels that appeared to the disciples at the Ascension in Acts 1, and there are [two]{.class_4725} witnesses who show up in the Tribulation in Revelation 11.

Now before this great picture of Christ's return in power to rule as King over the whole earth from Jerusalem (Zech 14) gets underway, you have another type given: [the salvation of a sinner.]{.class_4725} You see, the ["colt"]{.class_4723} in verse 30 is an [ass's]{.class_4725} colt (Matt. 21:2--5). It is a [wild]{.class_4725} ass's colt, for it has not been "broken in" yet (["whereon yet never man sat"]{.class_4723}). Do you know what the Bible says about ["a wild ass's colt"]{.class_4723}?

Job 11:12, ["For vain man would be wise, THOUGH MAN []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_560}BE BORN LIKE A WILD ASS'S COLT."]{.class_4723}

Now if you want to understand this passage in regards to the unsaved man, don't run to the "original Greek"; it won't give you any revelation whatsoever. When I wanted to know about wild jackasses, I asked an old farmer in Wing, Alabama who had [experience]{.class_4725} with them. I won't relate the whole conversation here as I did in my Commentary on Matthew (see our remarks under Matt. 21:1--5), but here is the gist of what he told me.

1\. A wild ass is the [most stubborn]{.class_4725} critter in the world.

2\. Wild asses [run around]{.class_4725} in the woods [late at night.]{.class_4725}

3\. Some of their [mothers]{.class_4725} are wild.

4\. A wild ass can't be [tamed]{.class_4725} until he's been [ridden.]{.class_4725} (That old farmer told me, "He ain't been broke in till he's done been rid.")

5\. You can't whip a wild ass because he [doesn't understand a whipping until he's been broken in.]{.class_4725}

Now that's as good a commentary on the state of an unsaved man as you'll get at any Bible College or Seminary. Unsaved men are stubborn. They run around late at night getting into trouble like I did before I was saved. They're "wild" because their mothers were "wild" (Psa. 51:5). They have to be "ridden" by the Lord before they are "tamed," and until that happens, they don't understand any "whipping" the Lord gives them.

So when an unsaved man is born into this world, he is likened to the colt of a wild ass who has never been ridden. The disciples are commanded to ["loose him, and bring him hither."]{.class_4723}

Mark 11:2 says the colt was ["tied"]{.class_4723}; that is, it was bound so it couldn't go anywhere. The Bible says an unsaved fellow is ["holden with the cords of his sins"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 5:22); he is ["in the bond of iniquity"]{.class_4723} (Acts 8:23).

The Lord asks Job, ["Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?"]{.class_4723} (Job 39:5). The answer is [God did.]{.class_4725} So this is a picture of the Lord setting the sinner free from sin (see 4:18 and our remarks on ["preach deliverance to the captives"]{.class_4723}).

Verse 31: ["And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath NEED of him."]{.class_4723} There were only two things the Lord ever said He [needed]{.class_4725} []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_561}when He was down here on this earth, and both times they were connected with saving a sinner. He said He ["must NEEDS go through Samaria"]{.class_4723} (John 4:4) to save that Samaritan woman, and He needed this wild ass's colt, a picture of a sinner being freed from his sins by the Lord Jesus Christ.

Now think about that a moment: ["The Lord hath need of him."]{.class_4723} Why did the Lord [need]{.class_4725} a reprobate like me; I wasn't worth fifteen cents. [Neither were you.]{.class_4725} Yet the Lord told the Holy Spirit, "Go get that fellow down there living like the devil; I need him." My, my, my, what a thing to say.

Isn't that flattering that the Lord [needs]{.class_4725} you? Now, of course, we understand that one of the attributes of God is [self-sufficiency.]{.class_4725} He doesn't really "need" anything. He can get along perfectly well without you or me. But the Lord says He needs you because He has a job He wants you to do. I must admit, I don't understand it.

Why would a God who made the galaxies, the stars, and the planets need a [man]{.class_4725}? We're nothing but a bunch of ants crawling around here on an anthill. If you died today, nobody would miss you but a very few relatives and close friends. There are nearly seven billion people who would never know that you ever lived or died. The sun will rise and set without you, and the tide will continue to come in and go out without you having anything to say about it at all. Why would God "need" someone like that? But He says He does. The only explanation for it, brethren, is [grace.]{.class_4725} It sure has nothing to do with your [self-worth]{.class_4725} or your [self-importance.]{.class_4725}

That colt was ["tied by the door"]{.class_4723} (Mark 11:4 cf. John 10:9). It was ["in a place where two ways met"]{.class_4723} (Mark 11:4 cf. Matt. 7:13--14). Salvation is pictured all the way through there.

["And they brought him to Jesus"]{.class_4723} (vs. 35). There's salvation. That wild ass's colt is brought to Jesus, and Jesus rides him and tames him. The disciples bring this colt to Jesus just like the four men brought their palsied buddy to Jesus back in 5:18 (which see). Your job, Christian, is to go out and bring sinners to Jesus Christ. The old song goes: "Bring them in, bring them in. Bring them in from the fields of sin\....Bring the wandering ones to [Jesus]{.class_4725}."

That colt is brought to Jesus, and Jesus sits on it and rides it. That's what happens to you when you get saved. The Lord takes the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_562}driver's seat and has the reins; He's the one in control. You are just the beast of burden to take Him wherever He wants to go. "I'll go where you want me to go, dear Lord"---that's the business.

["And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way"]{.class_4723} (vs. 36). The symbolism of what is taking place is not lost on the Jews. Zechariah 9:9 is being fulfilled before their eyes (John 12:14--15; Matt. 21:4--5), and although they may not understand everything at the time (John 12:16), they certainly do recognize the offer of the Messianic kingdom when they see it (vs. 38). After all, there is the King right there. Can the kingdom be far behind?

Well, it was, but it didn't have to be. Jesus Christ still had to die according to the prophecies given in Isaiah 53 and Psalm 22, but those Jews didn't have to reject Him before or after the Resurrection. In the Old Testament, time and time again, the First and Second Advents are spoken of right together. If Israel had accepted her Messiah, the Second Advent would have followed immediately on the heels of the First Advent.

When the Jews reject Him in Acts 7, the Second Advent is put off 2,000 years, and the Church Age is revealed. Now the Church, the Body of Christ, was created at Calvary (Eph. 2:16), and there were members in it in Acts 2 (Acts 1:5 cf. 1 Cor. 12:13). But it wasn't revealed until the Apostle Paul (Rom. 16:25; Gal. 1:10--12); it was a mystery (Eph. 5:32) hidden from the disciples in Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John because nothing was fixed yet. Those Jews still had the ability to turn the tide of events if they had just accepted Christ.

The offer of the kingdom here is a legitimate one. The Jews were not mistaken as to Christ bringing in a literal, visible, physical kingdom, as all the Amillennial and Postmillennial scholars teach. The disciples were expecting it in Matthew 20:20--24, and Christ never said they were wrong for doing so. He simply told them that a baptism of suffering had to precede the kingdom. Even after the Resurrection, the disciples were still expecting the kingdom (Acts 1:6), and Christ didn't say they're wrong then either. All He told them was that it was not for them to know the times and seasons of when God would bring it in (Acts 1:7). Brethren, there are over 500 prophecies in that Bible of Jesus Christ reigning on David's throne in Jerusalem over the whole earth. If they don't come to pass, God is a [liar,]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_563} and you can throw the Bible out (Deut. 18:22).

["Blessed be the King that cometh in the name of the Lord"]{.class_4723} (vs. 38). That's not Mohammed because Mohammed doesn't come ["in the name of the Lord."]{.class_4723} You say, "But Ruckman, don't Moslems believe that Mohammed is Allah's prophet?" Yes, they do, but "Allah" isn't the name of Mohammed's god.

"Allah" is an [identification,]{.class_4725} not a [name.]{.class_4725} The word means "the god." "There is no god but 'the god,' and Mohammed is his prophet." You say, "What is the name of 'the god'?" [He never says.]{.class_4725}

Moslems claim to have 99 "names" for Allah, and [not one]{.class_4725} of them is an actual name. They're all [attributes:]{.class_4725}[ ]{.class_4737}Beneficent, Compassionate, etc. Now the God of the Bible does that too (["Holy"]{.class_4723}---Isa. 57:15; ["Jealous"]{.class_4723}---Exod. 34:14; ["The mighty God, The everlasting Father, the Prince of Peace"]{.class_4723}---Isa. 9:6; etc.), but He will actually give you His [name.]{.class_4725}

When Moses asked God what His name was, God told him:[ "I AM THAT I AM"]{.class_4723} (Exod. 3:14). That's [Jehovah]{.class_4725} (Exod. 6:3). When God the Son shows up in human flesh, He comes in His Father's name (John 5:43)---the name of [Jehovah.]{.class_4725} Je-sus: [Je]{.class_4725}-Jehovah; [sus]{.class_4725}-saves. The name [Jesus]{.class_4725} means "Jehovah saves." So as God in the flesh (1 Tim. 3:16), God has a [name]{.class_4725} in the New Testament, and that name is [Jesus]{.class_4725} (1 John 5:20). That's Jehovah God coming down here as a man to save you.

["Peace in heaven."]{.class_4723} Why do they say that? Because before the King shows up on earth with the kingdom, there's going to be a [war]{.class_4725} in Heaven (Rev. 12:7). As the Prince of Peace, Jesus Christ not only brings peace on earth by bruising the head of the serpent (Rom. 16:20); He brings peace to Heaven by kicking the Devil out once and for all.

["Glory in the highest."]{.class_4723} Why, there's Luke 2:14. The peace on earth announced by the angels had nothing to do [doctrinally]{.class_4725} with the First Advent. It was the Second Advent when the King sets up His kingdom.

Now the Pharisees object to the disciples publicly proclaiming Jesus as the promised King of the Old Testament. Again, Jesus doesn't say, "You're right. The kingdom's not literal. My disciples are just mistaken and overzealous. I'll take care of that right away." To []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_564}the contrary, Jesus says that if the disciples had kept their mouths shut about it, that ["the stones would immediately cry out"]{.class_4723} (vs. 40).

Now what about those ["stones"]{.class_4723}? John the Baptist had said, ["God is able OF THESE STONES to raise up children unto Abraham" ]{.class_4723}(3:8). That's [organic]{.class_4725} matter from [inorganic]{.class_4725} matter. That's what Darwin said: you came from [rocks.]{.class_4725} But Darwin had you coming from rocks by [random chance.]{.class_4725} The only One who can bring life from dead rocks is [God]{.class_4725} (Gen. 1--2). The Law of Biogenesis---a provable, demonstrable law of science---states that you cannot get life from lifelessness. But in Luke 3:8, you have the possibility of God giving life to stones and turning them into human beings. Now look at Joshua 24:27.

"And Joshua said unto all the people, Behold, THIS STONE shall be a WITNESS unto us; for it hath HEARD all the words of the LORD which he spake unto us: it shall be therefore a WITNESS unto you, lest ye deny your God."

Joshua ["took a great stone, and set it up there under an oak"]{.class_4723} that was by the Tabernacle (Josh. 24:26). He told the children of Israel that that stone had heard everything God said to them, and that if they turned away from God that it would be called to the stand as a witness someday against them. That stone had [recorded]{.class_4725} the words God spoke to the Israelites.

Do you know of what audio tapes and MP3 discs and computer chips are made? They're made of [silicon:]{.class_4733} that's [stone;]{.class_4733} that's [rock;]{.class_4733} that's [sand.]{.class_4733} Back in 1993, scientists developed equipment which could pick up sound waves from [stone]{.class_4733} 24 hours after they were recorded. Now if man can do that, don't you know that God has a "surveillance system" of His own that puts anything the CIA has come out with to shame.

If man is going to give account of every idle word that he speaks (Matt. 12:36), all God has to do is pull up those rocks and stones and boulders and retrieve everything recorded on them like you would retrieve data on your computer. God is able to bring those stones to [life]{.class_4725} and have them [talk,]{.class_4725} and when He does, they'll testify as to what they heard you say---every word. God has a computer system that puts Bill Gates and "the Beast" over there in Belgium out of business.

The good news is that if you're saved, God has hit the "erase" button on your sins.

["How much more shall the blood of Christ\...purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 9:14).

["When he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 1:3).

Whatever is under the blood is [wiped clean]{.class_4725} (1 John 1:7).

41 And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,

42 Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.

43 For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,

44 And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.

The passage should be read in conjunction with Christ's words in 13:34--35 (see our comments on that passage). Christ is weeping because He knows what's coming up: the Romans are going to come in and destroy the city (vss. 43--44). He says that if those Jews only knew what it would take to bring them peace, they might avoid what's going to take place, ["but now they are hid from thine eyes"]{.class_4723} (vs. 42). The leaders reject their King and set the stage for what's to come.

Verses 43--44 are a prophecy of the destruction of Jerusalem under the Roman General Titus in A.D. 70. Titus built a wall around the city so that no one could get in or out. He starved out the population, then came in and took 500 of them and crucified them along the roads leading to the city. His soldiers had heard stories about the gold of Solomon's Temple and thought it was hidden beneath the foundation. They literally took the Temple apart brick []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_566}by brick, and they even took the huge blocks that made up the foundation and rolled them over and dug underneath them trying to find the gold. Christ's words about not one stone being left on another were [literally fulfilled.]{.class_4725} Mohammed couldn't give you anything like that; as a prophet, he was a bust.

["Because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation"]{.class_4723} (vs. 44). That's the time when God would visit them in wrath and judgment. God hid the judgment from them and what to do to avoid it (vs. 42) because they rejected His Son.

Now Jerusalem is destroyed three times, and the first two times are a picture of the third time. Jerusalem was destroyed the first time in 586 B.C. by Nebuchadnezzar; it was destroyed the second time in A.D. 70 by Titus; and it will be destroyed a third time at the end of the Tribulation by the Antichrist (Zech. 14:2). Nebuchadnezzar is one of the greatest types of the Antichrist in the Bible. Here the destruction of Jerusalem under Titus is mentioned in the context of the Second Advent (vs. 42---["thy peace"]{.class_4723}) because it is a type of what will take place in the future. The typology there is so strong that Preterists like John Bray have all the prophecies about Christ's Second Coming fulfilled in A.D. 70 at the destruction of Jerusalem (get our study on "America's Greatest False Prophet").

[Zechariah 14:2 can't be Titus.]{.class_4725} During that final attack on Jerusalem, God Himself comes down to fight for the city (Zech. 12:8, 14:3), and when He's done, He rules the earth from a rebuilt Jerusalem, with a rebuilt temple (Zech. 14; Ezek. 40--48). That didn't happen in A.D. 70, it didn't happen at anytime between A.D. 100 and 2000, and it's not happening [now.]{.class_4725} When Jesus Christ comes back, He's not going to put up with the nonsense you've got going on now with Obama, Hillary, Achmedinejad, Pope Benedict XVI, the UN, the EU, and the Muslim Brotherhood.

45 And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;

46 Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.

47 And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_567}destroy him,

48 And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.

Verse 45 is the [second]{.class_4725} cleansing of the Temple, the first taking place at the [beginning]{.class_4725} of Christ's ministry in John 2:14--16. Notice in John 2:16 that Jesus calls the Temple ["my Father's house."]{.class_4723} Here in verse 46, He quotes Isaiah 56:7 and calls it ["MY house."]{.class_4723} That's Jesus claiming to be Deity: He's one with the Father (John 10:30). When He walks out of it for the last time before going to the cross, He says, ["your house is left unto you desolate"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 23:38); meaning that with His departure, [God]{.class_4725} has left the building (see our remarks under 13:35).

The chapter ends with Jesus in the Temple teaching each day (this would be Sunday, Monday, and Tuesday before His Crucifixion). The leaders of the people want to destroy Him for what He has done. The Triumphal Entry and this latest cleansing of the Temple were "the straws that broke the camel's back." The problem is, ["all the people were very attentive to hear him"]{.class_4723} (vs. 48). Mark says, ["the common people heard him gladly"]{.class_4723} (Mark 12:37).

Now why were ["the common people"]{.class_4723} so ["attentive"]{.class_4723} to hear Jesus? Because when Jesus taught, He didn't teach like the scribes and Pharisees; He taught with [authority]{.class_4725} (Mark 1:22)---as in the [AUTHORIZED Version.]{.class_4725} The scribes didn't speak with authority because [they had no authority.]{.class_4725}

I know how the "scribes" speak. I had to listen to them at Bob Jones for six years. "This version is better here." "Dr. Smellfungus says the original word in the Greek is such-and-such, but Dr. Nutinhead has a different opinion." "The oldest and best manuscripts say such-and-such, so a better reading would be\...." All just a perfect BLANK---[no authority.]{.class_4725} The authority of the scribes is the opinions and preferences of people who have opinions and preferences. It's never what [God said]{.class_4725} in a [Book]{.class_4725}---ANY book.

The thing that marks our graduates here at PBI is [not]{.class_4725} that they all turn out like "Ruckman." (That's what the apostates say who are envious of the fact that I have young men who will follow me in my []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3612.xhtml_page_568}teaching and preaching, and they can't get anyone to follow them across the street.) The graduates of our school aren't like me at all. The thing that marks our graduates is that when they preach or teach, they speak like they know what they are saying and are [sure of it.]{.class_4725} When you speak like that, you speak with [authority.]{.class_4725}

Of course, the reason they speak like that is because they have ["a more SURE word of prophecy"]{.class_4723} that is their final authority (2 Pet. 1:19). When a graduate leaves our school, he carries his final authority with him. He doesn't have to check back with the school or the president or the alumni association for anything.

The issue is not the [fundamentals;]{.class_4725} it's not [standards;]{.class_4725} it's not ["double separation";]{.class_4725} it's not even [soulwinning.]{.class_4725} The issue is [final authority,]{.class_4725} and that's what comes up immediately in the next chapter (20:2).

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml}

# Chapter 20 {.heading_78-0}

20:1 And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,

2 And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?

3 And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:

4 The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?

5 And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?

6 But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.

7 And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.

8 And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

Now Jesus is teaching in the Temple, and He's teaching with [authority]{.class_4725} (Matt. 7:29). The scribes and the priests have no authority, although they want the common people to listen to them instead of Jesus. So they confront Christ with the main issue: [final authority.]{.class_4725} ["By what AUTHORITY doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this AUTHORITY?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 2).

When you are in the ministry, the question you get most often from other preachers is: "Where did you go to school?" They ask you that so they can either affirm or dismiss your ministry. If you go to a school they accept, you get their stamp of approval, and if you go to a school they don't accept, you are written off. Who taught you? Whom do you accept as the [authority]{.class_4725} for what you believe?

Our young men get it all the time. "Where did you go to school?" "Pensacola." "Oh, that's a wonderful school!" They think he's talking []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_570}about Pensacola Christian. When they find out he's talking about the "other school" in Pensacola, their countenance drops, and they say, "Oh, Ruckman."

I got it when I went to school. On at least two occasions I had Southern Baptists tell me I would go further in the ministry if I stopped "carrying the torch" for Bob Jones Sr. I was offered the moon if I would just leave Bob Jones University and hook up to the Convention machinery. Eddie Martin offered me large stadiums of young people to which to preach, and an associational missionary offered me free tuition if I got my doctorate from Louisville Seminary instead of Bob Jones University. What did they want? They wanted me to come under their authority so they could take credit for my ministry. It wasn't till I turned them down that one Southern Baptist pastor admitted that the problem was that the SBC was [jealous]{.class_4725} of how God was using Bob Jones Sr. That was the problem with the priests and scribes here in Luke 20.

The issue is final authority: it always has been and it always will be. We had a couple of our graduates apply to Baptist Mid-Missions to go to the mission field (this was before Bible Baptist Mission Board was formed). These young men were clean-cut, once-married, soulwinning young men; there was nothing in their character to prevent them from going overseas under Baptist Mid-Missions. But when the candidate committee found out they went to PBI, they asked them one question: "Do you believe the [KJV]{.class_4725} has errors in it?" When the young men said no, the committee spent [two hours]{.class_4725} trying to get them to admit that there were errors in that Book. When they wouldn't do it, they were rejected as candidates for the mission field. The problem was the [authority]{.class_4725} of one Book ([AV ]{.class_4725}1611).

It doesn't matter about what you are talking; the issue is a matter of [authority.]{.class_4725} Before Genesis 1:2, the Devil said, "I'm going to be King of the mountain," and God told him, "No, you're not; you're going to Hell" (Isa. 14:12--15).

The issue in the home is who's in charge: the husband or the wife? You say, "Our marriage is fifty-fifty." Then you're headed for disaster. Fifty-fifty is a dead heat; you'll never get anything done that way. At some point it's going to have to be 51--49, and ladies, you'll have to be the 49. If you don't like it, tough apples. If a man can't run []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_571}his house, he'll wreck it.

The issue in society is who's running the show: is it the government or the Constitution? My position is that the Constitution was thrown out by Abraham Lincoln in 1861, and any pretence by any government official to follow it is merely for convenience. When it's not convenient, the Constitution will be dumped every time. If you don't believe it, just look at what happened out at Waco to those Branch Davidians or to Randy Weaver in Ruby Ridge, Idaho or to Greg Dixon up there in Indianapolis. If the government doesn't like what you're doing or the way you are doing it, it will come in with guns, SWAT teams, the National Guard, snipers, tanks, and [nerve gas,]{.class_4725} if necessary, either to shut you down or [kill you.]{.class_4725} How's [that]{.class_4725} for "government of the people, by the people, and for the people"?

In sports, it's who gets the gold medal, who wins the cup, who gets the pennant, who takes home the trophy; in other words, who's top dog? In the Middle East, it's the question of who gets Palestine: are Moslem Palestinians going to be in charge, or is it going to be the people to whom God gave the land---the Jews? In Europe (and now over here in America), the question is will the Moslems live under the laws of the countries in which they reside, or can they impose their Sharia ([Koranic]{.class_4725}) laws on you? You see, everything is a matter of [authority.]{.class_4725}

For the Christian, the final authority in all matters of faith and practice is the [Bible.]{.class_4725} It's not the Bible [and tradition]{.class_4725} or the Bible [and the church]{.class_4725} or the Bible [and science]{.class_4725} or the Bible [and popular opinion.]{.class_4725} It's the Bible---[period]{.class_4725} (2 Pet. 1:19--21). The question is [which Bible?]{.class_4725} There are over 233 of them in English and a couple of dozen in Greek and two or three of them in Hebrew. You can't go by all of them because they contradict in anywhere from 500--64,000 places.

Now you know me. My position is that God gave you a final authority in the universal language of the last days---English. You say, "But what about all the foreign languages?" I don't know; I don't speak or read those languages. I believe there are "reliable translations" in many of them, and by "reliable," I mean versions that come from the same line of Bibles as the [KJV:]{.class_4725} the Protestant text of the Reformation. I don't mean translations made by "good, []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_572}godly" men from a line of corrupt Greek manuscripts promoted by German rationalists and closet papists like Westcott, Hort, Griesbach, Lachmann, Tischendorf, et al.

But as good and as faithful and as reliable as versions like Valera in Spanish, Diodati in Italian, Olivetan in French, and others like them may be, God has invested an authority in that [King James]{.class_4725} Bible that He has not invested in any other translation, version, or set of manuscripts on earth; not even the originals. It was the [AV]{.class_4725} that was responsible for the greatest period of missions and revival the world has ever seen---the Philadelphia church period. More people have been saved through the preaching and teaching of the [King James Version]{.class_4725} than have been saved through the use of any other version, including the much vaunted "original autographs" of the apostles. It was the Philadelphia church that kept God's word (Rev. 3:8), not the church of Ephesus.

The [AV]{.class_4725} is the "gold standard" among English Bibles. Every corrupt translation on the market has to compare itself to it to get any sales. No new version claims to be updating the one before it; they all claim to be an improvement on one Bible from 1611. Someone must think a lot of that one version to have to keep going back to it in order to generate sales. That Book must have an authority no other Bible has when every branch of science, philosophy, and Biblical scholarship is trying to get rid of it.

The [Authorized Version]{.class_4725} is the only English Bible translated under the authority of a [King,]{.class_4725} and ["Where the word of a king is, there is power"]{.class_4723} (Eccl. 8:4). With absolute [time]{.class_4725} (Greenwich Mean Time), absolute [location]{.class_4725} (longitude), and absolute [temperature]{.class_4725} (British thermal unit) determined by [England,]{.class_4725} God waited for an [English]{.class_4725} King to come along who had a [Jewish]{.class_4725} name (James is the English word for [Jacob]{.class_4725}) to give the world the absolute standard for a Bible because ["the oracles of God"]{.class_4723} were committed to the [Jews]{.class_4725} (Rom. 3:2).

The Final Authority has shown up, and it is so authoritative that it will even tell the Antichrist what language to speak: that Beast speaks with the mouth of an [English lion]{.class_4725} (Rev. 13:2). It is King of the mountain and the Monarch of the books. It is well able to judge [any]{.class_4725} work by [any]{.class_4725} man [anywhere ]{.class_4725}at [any]{.class_4725} time; that includes [Vaticanus, Sinaiticus, Alexandrinus,]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_573} and the works of Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Nida, Bruce, Scrivener, Burgon, Westcott, Hort, Tregelles, Tischendorf, Lachmann, Griesbach, Delitzch, Trench, Thayer, Vincent, and the translation committees of the [RV, ASV, RSV, NASV, NIV, NKJV, Good News, CEV, ESV,]{.class_4725} etc.

["By what authority doest thou these things?"]{.class_4723} By an old black-backed, 66-caliber 1611 [Authorized Version.]{.class_4725} ["Who is he that gave thee this authority?"]{.class_4723} [The God who exalted His word above the name of His own Son]{.class_4725} (Psa. 138:2 cf. Phil. 2:9; Neh. 9:5).

Back to Luke 20. Now Jesus could have been just that explicit in His answer to the priests and scribes, and He had been already on more than one occasion (John 5, 7, 8, 10). So now Jesus decides to have a little fun with them. He is going to put [them]{.class_4725} "on the spot."

["I will also ask you one thing; and answer me: The baptism of John, was it from heaven or of men?"]{.class_4723} (vss. 3--4). Now you're going to find out who the real hypocrites and yellow-bellies are. Instead of giving a forthright answer, they get in a huddle to figure out what to say. "If we say it was from God, He'll say, 'Then why didn't you get baptized?' And if we say it was from man, all the people are going to stone us because they think John was a prophet." Hello, hypocrite! Hello, you low-down yellow-belly!

They won't say from Heaven because they'll look like hypocrites, and they won't say from men because they're scared of the people, so they say, "We don't know." The dirty, miserable liars did too know; they just were too scared to say what they honestly thought.

They claimed to be [agnostics.]{.class_4725} "Well, I don't know. There may be or there may not be. [But since I have no authority,]{.class_4725} I can't tell for sure." You dirty, lying rascal, you know perfectly well what the answer is.

Those cowardly hypocrites told Jesus, "We can't tell." So Jesus says, "I'm not going to tell you either; see you later." The Lord had no intention of answering a bunch of obfuscating hypocrites feigning ignorance in a desperate attempt to [hide from the truth.]{.class_4725} If He had given them a direct answer, they had no intention of believing what He said anyway, and Jesus knew it (see 22:67).

9 Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_574}certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.

10 And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.

11 And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

12 And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.

13 Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.

14 But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.

15 So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?

16 He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.

17 And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?

18 Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.

19 And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.

This is a parable taken from Isaiah 5:1--7, but it is also the account of Ahab taking Naboth's vineyard in 1 Kings 21, showing you that Naboth is a type of Christ in that chapter. Naboth was put on trial and accused by false witnesses of blasphemy just as Christ was []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_575}(1 Kings 21:13 cf. Mark 14:57--64). Of course, Jesus wasn't stoned as Naboth was, but on at least two occasions the Jews tried to stone Him (John 8:59, 10:31). In both the case of Naboth and Christ, the ones who killed them thought the vineyard now belonged to them, and they were both proven [wrong]{.class_4725} (Ahab by Elijah; the chief priests by the Resurrection).

With that in mind, let's get into the Parable of the Vineyard. Like the Parable of the Pounds, it covers the course of both Advents. The ["certain man"]{.class_4723} is ["the LORD of hosts,"]{.class_4723} and the ["vineyard"]{.class_4723} is ["the house of Israel and the men of Judah"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 5:7). The ["husbandmen"]{.class_4723} would be the ones in charge of the nation: Kings, Governors, priests, scribes, elders. The ["far country"]{.class_4723} here is Heaven just as it was back in 19:12 (see our comments on that verse). The ["long time"]{.class_4723} turns out prophetically to be [2,000 years]{.class_4725} (see our comments on Hos. 6:1--2 in that Commentary); in the context here, it would be the time from when Joshua took the land of Canaan until the First Advent.

Verses 10--12 describe the ministry of the Old Testament prophets (cf. Amos 3:7). They were sent to demand fruit of Israel only to be slapped around (1 Kings 22:24), threatened (1 Kings 19:1--2), thrown in pits and left to die (Jer. 38:6), executed (Jer. 26:23), stoned (2 Chron. 24:21), and generally harassed (Heb. 11:36--38).

The last option of ["the lord of the vineyard"]{.class_4723} is to send His beloved Son (3:22, 9:35). Notice how verse 13 blows John Calvin's absolute predestination out of the water. ["IT MAY BE they will reverence him when they see him."]{.class_4723} The Pharisees, scribes, and priests had the free will to reverence Jesus Christ, and the Lord acknowledges the possibility that they could have done that. None of them were under any eternal decree of election or reprobation; they had a [choice.]{.class_4725}

["But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours"]{.class_4723} (vs. 14). Why, you're reading about Joseph's brothers conspiring to kill him back in Genesis 37:17--20. Moses wrote that back in 1500 B.C.; how did he know what was going to take place in A.D. 33? Show me anything like that in the [Koran.]{.class_4725} Go on; I dare you. You won't find it because [it ain't there.]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_576} Mohammed could no more prophesy---out-rightly or in type---than he could jump the Grand Canyon on his camel.

["So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him"]{.class_4723} (vs. 15). Christ died ["without the gate"]{.class_4723} and ["without the camp"]{.class_4723} (Heb. 13:12--13). This time ["the vineyard"]{.class_4723} isn't the people of Israel and Judah; it's the [land itself]{.class_4725} (notice Psa. 80:8 where the people are the vine and the land is where they are planted), specifically the city of Jerusalem.

The end of verse 15 and all of verse 16 take you into the future to the Second Advent. This time it's not the enemies of Israel coming to destroy them as it was in 19:43--44; it's the Lord Himself. It's Jehovah incarnate (Zech. 12).

["And shall give the vineyard to others"]{.class_4723} (vs. 16). This time Jesus applies ["the vineyard"]{.class_4723} to the Kingdom of God.

["Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 21:43).

The verse follows on the heels of Christ's words that the vineyard will be ["let out\...unto other husbandmen"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 21:41). Now both the Roman Catholic Church and the "Islamic Nation" claim to be the ["nation"]{.class_4723} that takes Israel's place in the Kingdom of God in this age. But Jesus isn't talking about any group of sex-obsessed, murderous, blaspheming, lost sinners bent on taking over the world (the Roman Catholic Church and the Moslems are "peas in a pod" when it comes to those things). He is talking about a body of saved believers who have been transformed by the New Birth into ["a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, AN HOLY NATION, a peculiar people"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 1:23--2:9). No Moslem believes in Jesus Christ as the Son of God (Sura 3:58, 9:30, 17:39, 2:116, 72:3, 6:102, 37:152) who died for his sins (Sura 23:50 note 1723, 5:75 note 724, 3:54), and Catholics believe they get the New Birth by Roman Catholic water baptism, not by faith in Jesus Christ alone. So God didn't turn the Kingdom of God over to any Catholic or Mohammedan.

Now if the ["others"]{.class_4736} of verse 16 and the ["nation"]{.class_4736} of Matthew 21:43 are not the Church, the Body of Christ, then what you have is a Jewish body of believers in the Tribulation who experience a new birth that is [different]{.class_4733}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_577 .class-1} from that of the Church Age. This new birth comes by confessing that Jesus is the true Christ in opposition to Satan's "christ" in the temple at Jerusalem (see our comments on 1 John 5:1 in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles, ]{.class_4733}Vol. II). But that is getting very deep, and we don't want to drown the kiddies in the wading pool, so let's move on.

Verse 17 is a quote from Psalm 118:22. The only structure where the headstone is a cornerstone is a [pyramid.]{.class_4725} On a pyramid, the capstone is a corner of the pyramid, and it is shaped just like the pyramid. The Bible uses the imagery of a pyramid here because the universe is in the shape of a pyramid (see our comments on Eph. 3:18 in that Commentary; a pyramid is a structure with [four dimensions]{.class_4725} like you find it in that verse) with its head corner (God seated on His throne in Heaven) separated from the rest of the structure by a body of water that would make the combined oceans of the Atlantic, Pacific, Indian, Arctic, and Antarctic look like a drop in a bucket (see our remarks on the Great Deeps under Gen. 1:2 in that Commentary).

So on the back of your dollar bill, you have a picture of a pyramid with the capstone floating overhead, separate from the rest of the pyramid. And right smack dab in the middle of that headstone is an all-seeing eye just to match Zechariah 3:9 (see our comments on the verse in that Commentary).

The ["stone"]{.class_4723} of verse 18 is the Smiting Stone of Daniel 2:34 --35, 44--45; but [both]{.class_4725} Advents are given in the verse. In this age, Jesus Christ is a ["stumblingstone"]{.class_4723} for the self-righteous sinner to trip over and break his fool neck (Rom. 9:32--33; Isa. 8:14). At the Second Advent, that Stone falls from Heaven and crushes the kingdoms of this world and grinds them all to a fine dust that blows away ["like the chaff of the summer threshing floors"]{.class_4723} (Dan. 2:35).

Now the priests and scribes get Christ's message loud and clear: [they're the murderers in the parable.]{.class_4725} Having figured that out, how do they react? They ["sought to lay hands on him"]{.class_4723} to kill Him (vs. 19). Isn't it amazing how someone can know what the Scripture says about him and still fulfill it to a T? You parents, how often have you told your child, "How many times have I told you\...," and had []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_578}them do exactly the opposite of what you've told them? I had to do that with my boys.

A man can see his future written out where he can get it and still go to Hell despite being told. Pharaoh did. He got ten graphic warnings and then rode into the Red Sea and drowned. Judas did. He went into the Garden of Gethsemane to betray Christ, and he got knocked backwards by Christ's use of the Divine name (["I am,"]{.class_4723} John 18:6 cf. Exod. 3:14). He picked himself off the ground and stepped forward and betrayed Christ anyway.

Sinner, you can go to Hell, knowing that you're going, and still go despite that knowledge. Frankly, I don't understand that, because the first time I heard the Gospel at 27 years old, I got saved. You didn't have to tell me twice. But I have seen people who've heard the Gospel time and time again---and who acknowledge that they are headed for Hell if they reject it---turn down Christ until the day they die.

One of our graduates who pastored a church across the bay in Milton had an old man come out to a revival meeting one time. That old man had been witnessed to on several occasions, and each time he turned down the Gospel with the excuse "I'm a very stubborn man." Well, as it turned out, the old man had a stroke or heart attack or something at the revival meeting and died as a result of it. As that old man lay there on the floor of that church dying, the evangelist pleaded with him to receive Christ while he still could. With his dying breath, that old man gasped out, "I'm\...a very\...stubborn\...man," and off he went into Hell.

Sinner friend, you had better not turn down Jesus Christ too many times, or you'll get yourself into a rut out of which you can't get. These priests and scribes knew God was going to kill them for murdering His Son, and they still went ahead with their plans.

20 And they watched him, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.

21 And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_579}the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:

22 Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or no?

23 But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?

24 Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Caesar's.

25 And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which be Caesar's, and unto God the things which be God's.

26 And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.

Now the Pharisees and Sadducees want to kill Christ, but they're afraid of the people (vs. 19) because they all hang on His words (19:48). So their plan is to try to get Him to say something that will get Him in trouble with the Romans so they can turn Him over to Pontius Pilate and have the Romans do their dirty work for them. To that end, they enlist some spies to [pretend]{.class_4725} (["feign"]{.class_4723}---vs. 20) that they are good men who just want the truth. They figure they'll ask Jesus some tricky questions and get Him talking freely. They figure ["In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin"]{.class_4723} (Prov. 10:19), so when Jesus oversteps His bounds and misspeaks something, they'll be able to catch Him in His words and use what He said against Him. They were like an ancient version of CBS, NBC, ABC, CNN, and NPR.

Modern churches are filled with people like that. They come to church with a chip on their shoulder just waiting for the preacher to say something they don't like so they can spread it all over the country. They don't come to get anything from God; they come to get [ammunition.]{.class_4725}

Of course, Jesus Christ never sinned in His words, but He said plenty to upset the religious leaders of His day. Suppose Billy Graham were holding a meeting in Pensacola; and you came up to me and said, "Ruckman, Billy Graham's holding a crusade here, and the pastors of all the major churches are joining in. Would you be interested in participating too?" If I replied, "Let the blind lead the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_580}blind, and they'll both fall in a ditch," would that upset you? Well, that's what Jesus Christ said; why do you take it from Him and not from me? You say, "Well, He was God."

[That's the point.]{.class_4725} He was the Lord God manifested in the flesh. You had [better]{.class_4725} take it from Him. [That's]{.class_4725} the One they're trying to trip up.

Now these spies approach Jesus like Nicodemus did in John 3:2, and they try to "butter Him up." But unlike Nicodemus, they don't approach Christ as sincere inquirers. If Jesus were such a good Master and teacher of the truth, then why didn't they do what He said?

["Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or no?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 22). If Jesus says yes, He gets in trouble with the Jews because they weren't to have any King over them but another Jew (Deut. 17:15). When Israel was right with God, the promise was that they as a nation would lend to other nations and be paid back with interest (Deut. 28:12), and if they weren't right with God, they would be the ones borrowing and paying back (Deut. 28:44). Conversely, if Jesus said no, it was against the Mosaic Law to pay tribute to Caesar, the leaders could accuse Him to Pilate of sedition.

["But he perceived their craftiness"]{.class_4723} (vs. 23). You aren't going to put one over on the Lord. He knows what's in man (John 2:25).

["Shew me a penny"]{.class_4723} (vs. 24). As usual (see our comments on the pound under 19:13), the new "Bibles" want to change the word ["penny"]{.class_4723} to make it "more accurate." (When the [AV]{.class_4725} is clear, you change it to make it more accurate, and when it is accurate, you change it to make it clearer. Anything but leave the text as it stands.) But all the modern translations do is transliterate the Greek word [denarion]{.class_4725} (["denarius"]{.class_4725}), which tells the reader nothing.

While it is true that the penny has dramatically fallen in worth and buying power (e.g., when I was a boy, you could get a Hershey bar for five pennies; you couldn't get one now for 25 pennies), it still does not negate the basic definition of the word [penny.]{.class_4725} In the English economy (the [AV]{.class_4725} is an [English]{.class_4725} Bible), the penny was the basic silver coin for exchange just as the denarius was in ancient Rome. Hence, the proper translation in [English]{.class_4725} would be ["penny."]{.class_4723} []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_581}The fact that your government changed a silver penny to copper, and then took all the copper out, does not change the Bible; it just means the government has given itself the power to mint and print [counterfeit money.]{.class_4725}

In Christ's day, the penny was the standard pay for a day laborer (Matt. 20:2). In today's society of minimum wages, that would make it worth about \$64.00. Here, the penny was the basic tax paid to the Roman government.

["Whose image and superscription hath it?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 24). The Roman coins all bore the image and name of the current Emperor. In America, the ["superscription"]{.class_4723} on all coins at one time was the national motto: "In God We Trust," but so as not to offend a bunch of thin-skinned atheists, agnostics, humanists, and Moslems, it was moved to the outside edge where it wouldn't be noticed and where it would wear off quicker.

The movement in this country since 1962 (Engel vs. Vitale Supreme Court decision) has been to remove all mention of God, Jesus Christ, and the Bible from public life. You get rid of Bible reading and prayer from the public schools so that the kiddies only get [one]{.class_4725} point of view: the [evolutionist, humanist]{.class_4725} world view. Then you [mock]{.class_4725} the Bible and [question]{.class_4725} it on TV programs and newscasts. The next step is to make it [illegal]{.class_4725} to take the Bible's stance on women, blacks, children, and queers and make it a "hate crime." That was one of the first laws passed by Obama when he took office: the Hate Crimes Bill. If you run a business, you fire your employees for saying "Merry Christmas" because it might offend someone who doesn't believe in Jesus Christ. If you're the principal of a public school, you let the kiddies sing about Santa Claus getting stuck in the chimney and Rudolph the drunken-nose reindeer, but you don't let them sing "Silent Night," "Joy to the World," or "Away in a Manger" because the ACLU will sue you.

The current policy of the Obama administration, the State Department (Hillary Clinton), and the Democratic Party is that you have freedom of [worship,]{.class_4725} but not freedom of [religion.]{.class_4725} Freedom of religion is the freedom to believe anything you want and practice it anywhere you want regardless of whether it's in public or private. This country was established on [freedom of religion.]{.class_4725} When Patrick []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_582}Henry said "Give me liberty or give me death," he had just witnessed a Baptist beaten (who later died of the beating) by the British for [preaching on the street without a license. That's]{.class_4725} the liberty about which he was speaking.

What Obama and Hillary are pushing for is freedom of [worship:]{.class_4725} you can believe and practice anything you want as long as you keep it within the four walls of your church. That policy eventually leads to what went on in the Soviet Union: you can only preach and practice in the churches what the government says you can. You can't raise your children to believe on Jesus Christ and the Bible because they're the wards of the State, and making them accept your religion is [child abuse.]{.class_4725} That's where "the land of the free and the home of the brave" is headed right now.

Back to Luke 20: ["Render therefore unto Caesar the things which be Caesar's"]{.class_4723} (vs. 25). The penny belonged to Caesar; otherwise it wouldn't have had his picture and name on it. So if he wants his money back, give it to him. That was Christ's answer, and there was nothing in it that the Pharisees and Sadducees could lay hold on.

Brethren, mark it down and get it: the Bible nowhere encourages this modern tax-revolt movement. The argument is that we're not under Caesar; we're under the Constitution. The Constitution doesn't say anything about an income tax, so you shouldn't pay it. I have known at least three Christians in my time who have promoted that, and every one of them ended up in prison for not paying his taxes. Do you know what they did when they got out? They paid their taxes like a good little citizen so they wouldn't have to go back. All that talk about the Constitution didn't do them a bit of good; it just ruined their ministries and their lives.

I pay my taxes for the same reason I'd hand my wallet over to John Dillinger if he put a gun to my head and said, "Your money or your life." I don't pay them because I'm patriotic; I pay them because I don't want to suffer the consequences for not paying them.

When it comes to the New Testament, you are told to pay your taxes. Some IRS agents came to Peter one time and asked, ["Doth not your master pay tribute?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 17:26). Jesus' answer to Peter was to pay the taxes so you don't offend the government []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_583}officials (Matt. 17:27). Someone is looking at you, expecting you to do the right thing. You need to be a good testimony. Paul said, ["tribute to whom tribute is due"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 13:7). Don't sit around trying to come up with little cutesy arguments to get out of what the Apostle to the Gentiles and your Saviour told you was the [right]{.class_4725} thing to do.

["And they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace."]{.class_4723} What can they say? They can't keep their Law anyway. The very Temple in which they were worshipping was built by a heathen King. Herod wasn't a Jew; he was a pagan [Edomite.]{.class_4725}

If you want an interesting study in the Gospels, go through and mark everywhere that Jesus is asked a question. Whether the question is put to Him in sincerity or pretense, He always has an answer. No one is able to stump Him or catch Him off guard. Then go back and look at the instances where Jesus asks others a question, and you'll find that He will stump them every time. Many times they won't even answer Him.

That's a picture of what's going to happen at the judgment (see Rom. 3:3 and our comments on the verse in that Commentary). The sinner is going to try to put God "on the spot" with questions designed to justify himself, and God's going to answer every one of them. Then God's going to ask some questions, and all the sinner can do is stand there with his head hung in shame because he doesn't have an answer (Rom. 3:19). You aren't going to buffalo God, sinner friend. The Pharisees and Sadducees couldn't do it when He was down here on earth, and you sure aren't going to do it when you stand before Him as He is in all His power and glory.

27 Then came to him certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,

28 Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man's brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

29 There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.

30 And the second took her to wife, and he died []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_584}childless.

31 And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.

32 Last of all the woman died also.

33 Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.

34 And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:

35 But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:

36 Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.

37 Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

38 For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.

These are the Sadducees. They were the First-Century Modernists. They thought when you're dead, you're dead. They rejected what the Old Testament said about angels, spirits, and the resurrection of the dead (Acts 23:8). Jesus taught that the day was coming when ["all that are in the graves shall hear his]{.class_4723} \[i.e., the Son of man's\][ voice, and shall come forth"]{.class_4723}: some ["unto the resurrection of life"]{.class_4723} and some ["unto the resurrection of damnation"]{.class_4723} (John 5:27--29). So the Sadducees are going to give Him a hypothetical case to try to mess Him up in His doctrine.

The case with which they come up has to deal with the Old Testament law concerning a Jewish husband who dies leaving no children to carry on his family name; you will find it given in Deuteronomy 25:5--6. The nearest male next of kin was to go in to the widow and perform the duty of a husband and give her a child. Of course, the big "what if" is what if the woman isn't able to have a child by the husband's brother? In the case the Sadducees pose to Christ, the widow runs through [seven]{.class_4725} brothers without having a []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_585}child by any of them. The question is ludicrous, to say the least, because what are the chances of not having a baby when you go through seven men?

Nevertheless, the Sadducees press the issue and ask Jesus, ["Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 33): with whom does she get to shack up in Heaven? That would be a problem for Mohammed, because Moslems get to keep on shacking up with women after they die. If a Moslem gets to Paradise, he gets 72 virgins with whom to shack up (who remain virgins every time they have relations!), and if his wife (or wives! Moslems men are allowed four, plus any number of slave women as concubines) gets to Paradise, I guess she gets to stand by and watch. I never have figured out whom she gets. You talk about discrimination against women---the man gets 72 other women, and the wife gets stuck with the husband who beat her while she was on earth (Sura 4:34). In Islam (and Mormonism!), a woman can't even get into Paradise unless her husband lets her in ([Hadith,]{.class_4725} Mishkat al-Masabih, Book I, No. ii, 60).

Both Mohammed and Joseph Smith created a "Heaven" based on their own depraved lusts. Both men loved to fornicate, so they created an afterlife where they could fornicate forever. That's what Paradise is to Moslems and Mormons.

The question put to Christ by the Sadducees was ["in the resurrection"]{.class_4723} which of the seven brothers gets the woman, because in life, she was married to all seven. Of course, they didn't believe in a resurrection at all, but they figured that since Jesus did, this would throw a monkey wrench in His theological machinery.

People have all kinds of funny ideas about Heaven. Southerners think Heaven is going to be all the things you liked to do here on earth: fishing, coon hunting---that kind of stuff, you know. "Will the circle be unbroken, by and by, Lord, by and by?" That's the whole family being in Heaven, which I'm all for [if they're saved.]{.class_4725} But Southerners think everything's going to be the same up there as it was down here: grandma will be there in her rocking chair knitting. Would you ladies like to be eighty years old for all eternity? People can be awfully nutty sometimes.

Now watch Christ straighten out these nuts: ["The children of THIS WORLD marry"]{.class_4723} (vs. 34). ["THIS world"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_586} is set in opposition to ["THAT world"]{.class_4723} in verse 35; that is, [the world to come.]{.class_4725} To get to ["that world,"]{.class_4723} you have to ["be accounted worthy to obtain"]{.class_4723} it. That's why the Lord talks about ["the resurrection FROM the dead"]{.class_4723} and not the resurrection OF the dead. You say, "Is there a difference, Ruckman?" [You bet your eternal soul there's a difference.]{.class_4725}

There's a resurrection where ["death and hell"]{.class_4723} deliver up their dead to face Jesus Christ at the White Throne Judgment (Rev. 20:11--13; Acts 17:31). That's the resurrection OF the dead. Those are people ["dead in trespasses and sins"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 2:1) who die in their sins (John 8:21, 24) and go to Hell. They are resurrected in that spiritually dead state, judged, and sent on to ["the second death"]{.class_4723} in the Lake of Fire (Rev. 20:14).

The resurrection FROM the dead takes place [before]{.class_4725} that. When Martha told Jesus that she believed her brother would come up at the general resurrection of the Last Day, Jesus told her, ["I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die"]{.class_4723} (John 11:25--26). In other words, if you believe in the Lord Jesus Christ for salvation, you are coming up [before]{.class_4725} ["the last day."]{.class_4723} How do you know that? Because Jesus Christ came up before the Last Day (see our comments on Matt. 17:9 in that Commentary). That's called ["the first resurrection"]{.class_4723} in Revelation 20:5, and if you come up in[ "the first resurrection,"]{.class_4723} you have no part in ["the second death"]{.class_4723} (Rev. 20:6). That first resurrection consists of three parts:[ "Christ the firstfruits"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 15:20, 23) and the Old Testament saints He brought up with Him at His resurrection (Matt. 27:52--53), the [harvest]{.class_4725} of the New Testament saints who ["sleep in Jesus"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 15:23; 1 Thess. 4:14--16), and the [gleanings]{.class_4725} of the martyred Tribulation saints at a post-Tribulation rapture (Rev. 20:4--6, 14:13--16; Matt. 13:38--43). [That's]{.class_4725} the resurrection FROM the dead.

Back to verse 35: ["neither marry, nor are given in marriage"]{.class_4723} because they're [sexless.]{.class_4725} Oops! I'm sorry. That's what the [Scofield notes]{.class_4725} said; it's not what [Jesus Christ or the Bible]{.class_4725} said. Scofield taught that angels were sexless beings, so ["the sons of God"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_587} in Genesis 6 couldn't have been angels because sexless beings can't have relations with women.

After that you can write B-A-L-O-N-E-Y. Scofield didn't get many things wrong, but he screwed up that one really badly. When Jesus said ["the children of the resurrection"]{.class_4723} don't get married because ["they are equal unto the angels"]{.class_4723} (vs. 36), it has nothing to do with angels being "sexless." Everywhere an angel shows up in that Bible, he is [male]{.class_4725} (see our comments on Rev. 21:17 in that Commentary).

If ["the children of the resurrection"]{.class_4723} are ["equal unto the angels,"]{.class_4723} that would make them [male.]{.class_4725} That's why they are called ["SONS of God"]{.class_4723} (John 1:12). At the resurrection, those sons of God will be like THE Son of God, the Lord Jesus Christ (1 John 3:1--2). The resurrection body of the believer is ["fashioned"]{.class_4723} after the resurrection body of the Lord Jesus Christ (Phil. 3:21). Well, what was the resurrection body of Jesus? It was the glorified body of a 33½-year-old [male]{.class_4725}.

So up in Heaven, ladies, you are going to be a 33½-year-old male just like your Saviour. If that seems odd or peculiar or distasteful to you Christian ladies, just remember that you have a [man]{.class_4725} living inside of you (1 Tim. 2:5 cf. Col. 1:27), and that your destiny as a child of God is to be [conformed]{.class_4725} to that man's image (Rom. 8:29).

That being the case, the reason ["the children of the resurrection" "neither marry, nor are given in marriage"]{.class_4723} is because [they're not queers.]{.class_4725} The sons of God are all male, and [they're not sodomites.]{.class_4725} The Bible never recognizes "same-sex" marriages.

Now the problem the Sadducees had wasn't with the Mosaic Law about a man raising up children by his dead brother's widow; it was with the doctrine of the resurrection itself. So in verse 37, Jesus cuts to the heart of the matter: ["Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush."]{.class_4723} Though there are much clearer proof texts in the Old Testament for the resurrection (e.g., Job 19:26--27; Isa. 26:19; Ezek. 37:11--14), Jesus quotes Exodus 3:6. The Sadducees had quoted the Mosaic Law as the authority for their question (vs. 28), so the Lord quotes Moses right back at them.

["When he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_588} (vss. 37--38). The implication is that Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob weren't permanently dead. Their souls were still alive and awaiting a reunification with a resurrected body to live again.

Notice that it's Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; it's not Abraham, Ishmael, and Mohammed. Mohammed said Abraham was the first Moslem (Sura 2:132), and he sacrificed [Ishmael]{.class_4725} instead of Isaac (Sura 37:100). Moreover, Mohammed said Ishmael was a prophet and "was well pleasing to his Lord" (Sura 19:54). In other words, the [Koran's]{.class_4725} Abraham and Ishmael were just as fake as its "Jesus."

The Abraham of the Bible never called God "Allah" a day in his life; he called Him ["El Shaddai"]{.class_4725}---["God Almighty"]{.class_4723} (Exod. 6:3). He offered up Isaac as a type of the death, burial, and resurrection of the Lord Jesus Christ (Gen. 22 cf. Heb. 11:17--19). He offered up [Isaac,]{.class_4725} because by Genesis 22, God had told Abraham to ["cast out"]{.class_4723} Ishmael because [Isaac]{.class_4725} was the chosen seed (Gen. 21:10--12; Gal. 4:28--30).

Ishmael was no more a "prophet" than Mohammed was. If you want a prophet, read what [Jacob]{.class_4725} prophesied in Genesis 49; Jacob could prophesy more in one chapter of one book of the Bible than Ishmael or Mohammed could in 114 Suras of the [Koran.]{.class_4725} There is only one person in the entire Bible with whom God is ["well pleased"]{.class_4723}; and it's not Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Ishmael, Mohammed, or any other sinner. It's His [Son,]{.class_4725} the Lord Jesus Christ (see 3:22 and our comments on the verse). Mohammed didn't even believe God had a Son (Sura 3:58, 9:30, 17:39, 2:116, 72:3, 6:102, 37:152).

["For all live unto him"]{.class_4723}---another statement by the Lord Jesus on the bodily resurrection of the believer. That is exactly what Paul said about the resurrected Christ (Rom. 6:10). The one sure proof that you won't stay down if you are saved is that your Saviour didn't (Rom. 8:11; John 14:19).

39 Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.

40 And after that they durst not ask him any question at all.

41 And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_589}David's son?

42 And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,

43 Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.

44 David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?

Both the Conservatives and the Liberals have now tried their hand at tripping up Christ in His words, and both have failed. In response to Christ's answer to the Sadducees concerning the resurrection, one of the scribes mumbles, ["Master, thou hast well said"]{.class_4723} (vs. 39), and they're done. They have nothing more to say.

The word ["durst"]{.class_4723} in verse 40 is archaic English for [dare.]{.class_4725} The idea is they dare not ask Jesus any more questions to catch Him in His words if they don't want to be publicly humiliated any more by His answers.

But Jesus isn't done with them yet. It is now His turn to ask the question and see just how smart they are. And the question that Jesus is going to get them on deals with His [Deity.]{.class_4725}

["How say they that Christ is David's son?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 41). Well, because the promise to David was that he would always have a son to sit on his throne forever (2 Sam. 7:16; Jer. 23:5--6, 33:17--26).
