But the Messiah was to be much more than just David's physical descendant. Look at the Scripture Christ quotes in verses 42--43: Psalm 110:1.

"The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool."

The psalm is entitled ["A Psalm of David,"]{.class_4723} so this is David writing about God's coming anointed King, the Messiah. That's [Christ]{.class_4725} in Greek.

["The LORD"]{.class_4723}---who's that? Any Jehovah's Witness will tell you that's the [King James']{.class_4725} word for [Jehovah,]{.class_4725} and he would be [right.]{.class_4725} Jehovah ["said unto my Lord."]{.class_4723} There's a [second]{.class_4725} Lord. It's obviously a reference to God the Father speaking to God the Son, for it is Jesus Himself who ends up at the Father's right hand (Mark 16:19; Heb. 1:1--3).

Notice that David calls that second Lord ["MY Lord."]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_590} Well, if that second Lord is the Messiah, and the Messiah is David's son, what is David doing calling his son ["Lord"]{.class_4723}? Would David call his own son ["Lord"]{.class_4723}? Are you kidding? David is a Shemite, an [Oriental.]{.class_4725} Orientals don't call their children "Lord."

Orientals demand complete obedience from their children, which is one reason that the smallest jail and prison population in America is Oriental. Orientals are taught to reverence their ancestors. Of course, in places like Japan, that reverence is carried to the extreme and gets into idolatry. But you can see from that why no Oriental would even think of calling his children or any of his descendants "Lord."

["David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?"]{.class_4723} (vs. 44). Those Pharisees, Sadducees, and scribes have no answer; they are as quiet as a turkey farm on Thanksgiving afternoon. The horrible truth that they won't accept is that [Christ can't be David's literal son.]{.class_4725} He has a divine nature that He doesn't get from David. He's the Son of man: he has a human nature that He gets from David through Mary. He's the Son of God: He has a divine nature that comes from God because God is His Father and He Himself is a member of the Godhead.

The Jews understood perfectly what it meant for a man to claim to be the Son of God, even if the stupid Jehovah's Witnesses don't. When Jesus said[ "I and my Father are one"]{.class_4723} (John 10:30), He certainly did [not]{.class_4725} mean "one in purpose" like a JW will tell you. Those Jews ["took up stones\...to stone him"]{.class_4723} (John 10:31) because Jesus was making Himself [God]{.class_4725} (John 10:33). Those Jewish leaders didn't want God showing up in the flesh to be their King anymore than the Pope wants Jesus Christ to come back and kick him off the papal throne in Rome.

45 Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,

46 Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;

47 Which devour widows' houses, and for a shew make []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3776.xhtml_page_591}long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.

Right after silencing His enemies, Jesus now turns to the common people and warns them about their religious leaders. Here it is just the scribes who are singled out, but in Matthew 23, Christ includes the Pharisees along with them.

The thing to note here and in Matthew 23 is that the Pharisees and scribes did not die out in the First Century. Do you know of any group of religious leaders who like to walk around ["in long robes"]{.class_4723} (vs. 46) and to be called "Father" (Matt. 23:9)? I'll give you a hint: they charge widows for high Masses to get their dead husbands out of Purgatory (vs. 47).

Catholic Priests bear all the marks Christ ascribes to the Pharisees and scribes. If Christians had heeded their Lord's warnings, there would be no Roman Catholic Church today.

["And for a shew make long prayers"]{.class_4723} (vs. 47). So Mohammedans pray in large groups, many times outdoors, to be sure that they are [seen]{.class_4725} praying so everyone will think they are holy. As I have said many times, there is nothing original in Mohammed's system. Moslems are monotheists; so were the Pharisees. Moslems don't eat pork; neither did the Pharisees. Moslems don't believe Jesus is the Son of God; neither did the Pharisees. And just like the Pharisees, religious hypocrites like Catholic Priests and Moslem Imams ["shall receive greater damnation"]{.class_4723} than those they make ["twofold more"]{.class_4723} the children of Hell than themselves (Matt. 23:15).

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml}

# Chapter 21 {.heading_78-0}

21:1 And he looked up, and saw the rich men casting their gifts into the treasury.

2 And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites.

3 And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:

4 For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.

Jesus has been teaching and preaching at the Temple, and in this passage, He takes a break to observe how the people gave as they entered the Temple (Mark 12:41). This was a system set up by Jehoiada the high priest back in the Old Testament (2 Kings 12:9; 2 Chron. 24:8). A chest with a hole in the top was set on the right side of the entrance into the outer court of the Temple where the people came to sacrifice. As they entered, they would put their monetary gifts in the chest. The original intent of that offering was the maintenance of the Temple.

As Jesus looks on this scene, Mark says, ["many that were rich cast in much"]{.class_4723} (Mark 12:41). But right in the midst of all these rich men casting in their large gifts, up walks a poor widow woman who casts in ["two mites"]{.class_4723} (vs. 2). A mite (Gk.--[lepton]{.class_4725}) was a copper coin that had the least value in the Palestinian economy. ["Two mites"]{.class_4723} made up ["a farthing"]{.class_4723} (Mark 12:42), which was worth half a penny (see our comments on 20:24). This widow woman didn't have enough money to make up a full day's wage.

But Jesus wasn't concerned with the amount the woman gave; He was concerned with the sacrifice she made to give her two mites. The rich gave ["of their abundance"]{.class_4723} (vs. 4). It would be like Bill Gates giving a million dollars to charity; a million dollars wouldn't be any kind of sacrifice at all to a [billionaire]{.class_4725} like Gates. But you take a widow living on a fixed income every month, for her, \$500 would be a real sacrifice because she's depending on that money to pay her bills. []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_593}That's the situation here.

Jesus said that widow gave ["of her penury"]{.class_4723} (i.e., extreme poverty) ["all the living that she had."]{.class_4723} Percentage-wise, she gave [100%]{.class_4725} of her income, while those rich men may have given only 10--20%. Now thank God for "big givers" like LeTourneau and Wanamaker (see our remarks under 18:24). A lot of these large Christian works owe their growth to some rich Christians who were generous in their giving. But when it comes to [rewards in Heaven, ]{.class_4725}you may see a poor widow or elderly saint get more at the Judgment Seat of Christ because it was a real sacrifice for her to give what she did. Brethren, you are going to see a lot of surprises at the Judgment Seat of Christ.

5 And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts, he said,

6 As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

7 And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what sign will there be when these things shall come to pass?

8 And he said, Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.

9 But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not by and by.

10 Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:

11 And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.

["And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts"]{.class_4723} (vs. 5). The gifts of verse 1 were no longer being used just for maintaining the Temple; they were now being used to make it more ornate. Those chief priests and scribes []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_594}don't realize that in less than forty years the whole thing will be razed to the ground. Then all the ["goodly stones,"]{.class_4723} ["gifts,"]{.class_4723} and adorning would be one big, hugemongous [waste.]{.class_4725}

I can take you to outfits in the town where I minister where there is, literally, a multi-million dollar facility. Now you know what's going to happen to the sports center, the Olympic-size swimming pool, the bowling alley, the Belgium chimes, the marble hallways, the skating rink, the planetarium, the twenty million dollar buildings, etc.? They're all going to [burn]{.class_4725} (2 Pet. 3:10--12).

Do you know why we don't waste our time on building big buildings and monuments at the Pensacola Bible Institute and the Bible Baptist Church? [Because it's all going up in smoke.]{.class_4725} Your house? It will burn. St. Peter's basilica? It will burn. Robert Schuller's "Crystal Cathedral"? It will melt. Joel Osteen's stadium? It will go up in flames. I mean, burn, baby, burn.

Do you want to invest in something that will last? Then invest in building [men.]{.class_4725} You can't stop the buildings from burning, but you can stop a man from burning. If you get a man saved, he won't burn in Hell. If you train him to reach others with the Gospel, he will stop a lot of other people from burning in Hell. ["He that doeth the will of God abideth for ever"]{.class_4723} (1 John 2:17).

We have already commented on the material in verse 6 under 19:44 (which see).

Now what you have throughout the rest of the chapter is what is known as the Olivet Discourse, which you can also find in Matthew 24 and Mark 13. The theme is the Second Coming of Christ. The ["sign"]{.class_4723} of verse 7 turns out to be ["the sign of THY COMING"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 24:3).

The Second Coming is the main theme of the entire Bible; there are more than ten times as many verses on that subject as there are on church membership, "sacraments," or the plan of salvation. If your preacher doesn't put the emphasis on the return of the Lord Jesus Christ, he is lop-sided and unbalanced.

The Second Coming is bound up with every fundamental of the faith. Both Jesus and Paul connect the Blood Atonement for sin with Christ's return (John 14; 1 Cor. 15). The Virgin Birth is connected with the Resurrection (Rom. 1:3--4), and the Resurrection is []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_595}connected with the Second Coming (1 Cor. 15:20--23). The authority of the Bible is connected to the Second Coming (2 Pet. 1:19), as is the Deity of Christ (Tit. 2:13).

There is no meaning to the Lord's Supper without the Second Coming (1 Cor. 11:26). Your redemption is incomplete without the Second Coming (Rom. 8:18--23). None of the covenants God made with Israel are valid without the Second Coming (Rom. 11:25--27). The Christian has no Millennial inheritance without the Second Coming (see our remarks on 19:12). There will be no everlasting peace or righteousness without the Second Coming (Isa. 9:6--7). The curse on the earth can't be lifted without the Second Coming (Rom. 8:19--23).

The Second Coming will accomplish the [salvation of nature]{.class_4725} (Isa. 35:1--2). [Wild animals will be tamed]{.class_4725} because of it (Isa. 11:6--9). It [solves the "Jewish problem"]{.class_4725} (Mic. 4:3--4). It [abolishes all false religions and fake "sacred scriptures" ]{.class_4725}(Isa. 2:2--3), including [the obliteration of the Roman Catholic Church]{.class_4725} (Rev. 17--18). It completely fulfills[ all the prophecies about Christ]{.class_4725} which were made 400--1,500 years before He was born.

["And they asked him, saying"]{.class_4723} (vs. 7). ["They"]{.class_4723} turns out to be only [four]{.class_4725} of the disciples: ["Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately"]{.class_4723} (Mark 13:3). It is Christ's "inner circle" (with the addition of Peter's brother, Andrew) which receives Christ's teaching on His Second Coming.

Now, Jesus gives six signs here of His coming: false Christs (vs. 8), proliferation of wars (vss. 9--10), ["great earthquakes"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), ["famines"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), ["pestilences"]{.class_4723} (vs. 11), and signs in the heavens (vs. 11). To those you can add the following:

1\. The budding of ["the fig tree"]{.class_4723} (Jer. 8:11--13 cf. Mark 13:28--29; Luke 21:29--32), which is the rebirth of the nation of Israel (Isa. 66:8).

2\. The return of the Jews to the land of Palestine (Ezek. 20:34; Isa. 11:10--12).

3\. The Jews buying land in Israel (Jer. 32:44).

4\. The order of the Jews' return to the land (Isa. 43:5--6).

5\. The appearance of a pure language (Hebrew---Zeph. 8:8--9).

6\. The restoration of the shekel as the standard Israeli currency []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_596}(Exod. 30:13).

7\. The cities of ancient Israel being rebuilt (Ezek. 36:10, 22, 25, 38).

8\. The productivity of the land of Israel (Isa. 27:6).

9\. Perfect irrigation in Israel (Isa. 58:11--12).

10\. Trees are now growing in forests in Israel (Isa. 41:19--20).

11\. Vultures are now nesting in Israel after fifteen centuries (see our remarks under 17:37).

12\. The rainfall in Israel has increased from 2.1 inches annually (1931--1960) to 21.1 inches annually (1960--1980) (Amos 9:11--15).

13\. Israel is a democracy ruled by ["governors"]{.class_4723} (Zech. 9:7, 12:5--6).

14\. The whole world is now caught in the "web" of the Internet (Eccl. 9:12).

15\. Everything is prepared for the rebuilding of the temple (2 Thess. 2:4; Rev. 11:1--2).

16\. Fake peace treaties with the "Palestinians" and Moslems (Dan. 11:27).

17\. Unbelief and apostasy within the Body of Christ (1 Tim. 4:1--3; 2 Tim. 3:1--9, 13, 4:3--4; 2 Pet. 2:1--3).

18\. The installation of an international Police State (Rev. 13:7; Dan. 7:19--21).

19\. Satanic Bible versions (Jer. 23:1--40; 2 Pet. 3:16; 2 Cor. 2:17).

20\. The gathering of the United Nations to be destroyed (Zeph. 3:8; Matt. 13:40).

Now let's go back and pick up what the Lord says about His Second Coming. Jesus said, ["Take heed that ye be not deceived"]{.class_4723} (vs. 8). The New Testament Christian is warned to ["Be not deceived"]{.class_4723} (Gal. 6:7; 1 Cor. 6:9, 15:33), ["Let no man deceive you"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 5:6; 2 Thess. 2:3; 1 John 3:7), and ["Let no man deceive himself"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 3:18). Paul tells the Christian that ["in the last days"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:1), ["evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:13) and that this will be accomplished ["by good words and fair speeches"]{.class_4723} (Rom. 16:18); as in Joel Osteen, Rick Warren, and Robert Schuller---nothing negative, all positive.

We have already commented on false Christs (vs. 8) in our []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_597}remarks on 17:22--23 (which see).

["Wars and commotions"]{.class_4723} (vs. 9) have always been a part of human history. According to the [Encyclopedia of Military History]{.class_4725} (1986), there have been 4,345 battles fought since 3500 B.C.; that's one every nine to ten months since Genesis 14. But of the bloodiest battles ever fought in history, the top twenty took place in World War I and World War II. In fact, more people died in wars in the Twentieth Century than died in all the wars fought from Genesis 14:2 to 1900, causing Time-Life books to call it "The Violent Century." When the Christian Colleges and Seminaries dumped the [Authorized Version]{.class_4725} for the [American Standard Version]{.class_4725} in 1901, what followed was World War I, World War II, the Korean War, the Vietnam War, the Gulf War, and Bush's "War on Terror."

According to Professor Rudolph Rummell of the University of Hawaii, from 1900--1987, 38.5 million died in war, and another 170 million died in genocides by their own governments (Germany, Russia, Cambodia, Rwanda, etc.). As the body count goes up, so does the cost of "bringing in the kingdom." In the Civil War, it took \$2.00 to kill a man; in World War I, it took \$50.00; in World War II, it was \$200.00; in Korea, it was \$3,000.00; in Vietnam, it was \$6.000.00.

In 1945, the United Nations was founded as mankind's "last, best hope for peace." According to President Truman, it was to usher in "the greatest era of peace" the world had ever seen. What followed was [177 armed conflicts]{.class_4725} (up to 2008) around the globe. Of those, twelve were [major wars;]{.class_4725} 21 were [secondary wars;]{.class_4725} 23 were [revolutions;]{.class_4725} sixteen were [wars of succession]{.class_4725} (inner revolutions to gain authority); 36 were [civil wars]{.class_4725} to change regimes; and 24 were "military interventions." None of those figures include Obama's war in Afghanistan, his overthrow of Khadafi in Libya, or the "Arab Spring" in Tunisia, Egypt, and Syria. And you are about to go to war with Iran before Acheminajab develops a nuclear bomb to wipe Israel off the map.

Now [that's]{.class_4725} what Christ meant when He said ["wars and commotions."]{.class_4723} The "biggest and best" are yet to come: Revelation 6, 19, 20. In Revelation 6:4, there is a war that kills off [two-and-a-half billion people]{.class_4725} (Rev. 9:18).

["Great earthquakes\...in divers ]{.class_4723}\[i.e., [diverse]{.class_4725}\][ places"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_598} (vs. 11). Seismologists say an earthquake is considered "major" if it measures 7.0--7.9 on the Richter scale and "great" if it measures 8.0--9.9. According to the statistics put out by the National Earthquake Information Center of the U.S. Geological Survey, between 1987 and January 2010, there were 22 "great" earthquakes and 324 "major" earthquakes. In 2004, 228,802 people died from the 9.1 earthquake that caused the tsunami in the Indian Ocean basin. In the Tribulation, there is an earthquake so great that it sinks every island in the sea, levels all the mountains, and destroys every major city on earth (Rev. 16:18--21).

["Famines."]{.class_4723} You already see it happening in east Africa and India. Al Gore and the "global warming" boys want to reduce CO[2]{.class_4744} (carbon dioxide) levels globally, but plants use CO[2]{.class_4744} to produce [food.]{.class_4725} The less CO[2]{.class_4744} in the atmosphere, the less food that is produced. In the Tribulation, it takes a full day's wage just to buy enough wheat or barley to make a loaf of bread (Rev. 6:6).

["Pestilences"]{.class_4723}---that's EBOLA, influenza, GRID (called AIDS by the news media). Worldwide (especially in Africa), AIDS (originally called GRID---GAY Related Immunity Deficiency) has now killed[ five times]{.class_4725} the number that died from the Black Plague in the Dark Ages or the flu epidemic of 1917--18.

["And fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven."]{.class_4723} That's Revelation 6:12--13, 8:10--12, 9:1, 15:1; Joel 2:30--31; and Isaiah 13:10. You can see it illustrated on page 151 of [Ruckman's Apocalypse.]{.class_4725}

12 But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name's sake.

13 And it shall turn to you for a testimony.

14 Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answer:

15 For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.

16 And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.

17 And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake.

18 But there shall not an hair of your head perish.

19 In your patience possess ye your souls.

Verses 12--13 were fulfilled by the apostles and disciples in the book of Acts. Verses like 16--17 go clear through the Church Age. The Apostle Paul said, ["Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:12).

Jesus is telling the disciples to expect persecution, but not to sit around worrying about what they will say when it comes. If you are faithful putting out the Gospel while you have a chance, the Holy Spirit will give you the right words to say when the axe falls.

["And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake"]{.class_4723} (vs. 17). Jesus told the apostles in John 15:18, ["If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you."]{.class_4723} John wrote, ["Marvel not, my brethren, if the world hate you"]{.class_4723} (1 John 3:13). If everyone loves you and no one criticizes you in regards to your faith in Christ and the Bible, [you aren't speaking up for Jesus Christ like you ought.]{.class_4725}

Now if you want a contradiction in the Bible, then compare the end of verse 16 to verse 18: ["Some of you shall they cause to be put to death\....But there shall not an hair of your head perish."]{.class_4723} How can you be ["put to death"]{.class_4723} and yet not have ["an hair of your head perish"]{.class_4723}?

Well, look at verse 19:[ "In your patience possess ye your SOULS."]{.class_4723} If you are saved, not one hair on your head will perish, no matter whether you die or not, because you are coming up in a glorified body some day.

20 And when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.

21 Then let them which are in Judaea flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the countries enter []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_600}thereinto.

22 For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

23 But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.

24 And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.

The passage has a double application. Verse 24 is obviously a reference to the destruction of Jerusalem by Titus in A.D. 70, because ["the times of the Gentiles"]{.class_4723} follow. But verses 21--23 are connected with the Tribulation (see Matt. 24:16--21). Note ["the days of vengeance"]{.class_4723} in verse 22 (see Isa. 34:8, 61:2, 63:4; Jer. 46:10, 51:6; 2 Thess. 1:7--9). Of course, the reason for the double application is that Jerusalem is going to be ["compassed with armies"]{.class_4723} (vs. 20) by the Antichrist at the end of the Tribulation (see our remarks under 19:43--44).

Notice the peculiar "Jewishness" of the passage: ["Jerusalem"]{.class_4723} (vs. 20), ["Judaea"]{.class_4723} (vs. 21). ["The land"]{.class_4723} of verse 23 is Palestine, and ["this people"]{.class_4723} are the Jews. The passage has no application to "Christians." It deals with ["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_4723} (Jer. 30:7). A holocaust is coming up that will make Hitler's Holocaust pale in comparison. The upcoming "policy" of the UN, the USA, the EU, NATO, and the Muslim Brotherhood is the complete genocide of the Jewish people (Psa. 83:2--8; Lam. 5:2--14; Zech. 14:2; Rev. 12:12--17).

["Until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled"]{.class_4723} (vs. 24). The end of ["the times of the Gentiles"]{.class_4723} began in 1917 with the Balfour Declaration. That's when God used a Gentile King to send His people back to the land He gave them (like He did in Ezra 1:1--4; see our comments on that passage in that Commentary). The finale of the Gentiles takes place at the Second Advent (Rom. 11:25--26). Right now, you are in a transition period. Up until the Rapture, you are still in the Church Age, so salvation is by grace through faith as a free gift. But [nationally,]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_601} God is setting aside the Gentile nations and is concerned with [Israel.]{.class_4725} Right now, God is making Jerusalem ["a cup of trembling"]{.class_4723} and ["a burdensome stone" "to ALL PEOPLE"]{.class_4723} (Zech. 12:2--3); that includes Bush, Obama, Clinton, and the [United States of America.]{.class_4725}

25 And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;

26 Men's hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.

27 And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

28 And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.

We have already given you the cross-references on the ["signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars"]{.class_4723} (vs. 25) under our remarks on the last part of verse 11 (which see).

The ["distress of nations"]{.class_4723} is the ["wars and commotions"]{.class_4723} between the nations that we covered back up in verses 10--11. You can have all the "peace processes" and "peace summits" you want, and you won't stop war between the nations until Jesus Christ comes back and puts them all down at the Second Advent.

["With perplexity."]{.class_4723} He's talking about the Tribulation. Isaiah wrote, ["For it is a DAY OF TROUBLE]{.class_4723} \[Jer. 30:7\][, and of TREADING DOWN]{.class_4723} \[Second Advent, Isa. 63:1--6\][, and of PERPLEXITY]{.class_4723} \[there's Luke 21:25\][ by the Lord GOD of hosts in the valley of vision]{.class_4723} \[Second Advent, Joel 3:12--14\][, breaking down the walls, and of crying to the mountains"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 22:5).

["The sea and the waves roaring."]{.class_4723} It can be literal (see our comments on Psa. 46:2--3 in that Commentary) or figurative (Isa. 5:29--30). The tsunamis in Indonesia and Japan are "only a foretaste of damnation divine" in this regard.

The common expression [heart failure]{.class_4725} is taken from verse 26 (["men's hearts failing them for fear"]{.class_4723}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_602}). The number one killer in the United States is [heart disease:]{.class_4725} coronaries, heart failure, etc.

["For the powers of heaven shall be shaken."]{.class_4723} This has nothing to do with the signs in the heavens in verses 11 and 25. These are the ["principalities"]{.class_4723} and ["powers"]{.class_4723} that are connected with ["the rulers of the darkness of this world"]{.class_4723} and ["spiritual wickedness in high places"]{.class_4723} (Eph. 6:12). Those ["high places"]{.class_4723} [aren't]{.class_4725} high offices of government like Origen taught. Look at Isaiah 24:21.

["And it shall come to pass in THAT DAY]{.class_4723} \[the Day of the Lord---Second Advent\][, that the LORD shall punish the host of the HIGH ones that are on HIGH, and the kings of the EARTH upon the EARTH."]{.class_4723}

So those ["high places"]{.class_4723} in Ephesians 6:12 [aren't on the earth.]{.class_4725} They are up there in the first and second heavens where their Prince is (Eph. 2:2).

Verse 27 is definitely the Second Advent, so Christ tells the disciples that when they see the things about which He told them in verses 8--26 ["begin"]{.class_4723} to take place, to ["look up\...for your redemption draweth nigh"]{.class_4723} (vs. 28). Now these aren't signs for the Rapture; they're signs for the Advent. The signs are for that Jew, not the Christian. But let's just face it, folks, have these signs BEGUN ["to come to pass"]{.class_4723}" Yes! Then ["LOOK UP\...for your redemption draweth nigh"]{.class_4723} (cf. Rom. 8:23).

There's no need to get overly dispensational here. Take heart! If the Advent is so close that anyone can see the signs for it, then [take heart]{.class_4725} because the Rapture is not that far away. The Rapture of the Church takes place [before]{.class_4725} the Advent, so any sign for the Second Advent is also a sign for the Rapture. And even if the signs are not meant for you, Christian, that doesn't mean you can't read them. Bless God, even at 91 years old, I'm not looking for the undertaker; I'm looking for the UPPERTAKER! As far as I'm concerned, the sooner the better, and He's already late enough for me now.

29 And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;

30 When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_603}own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.

31 So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.

32 Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.

33 Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

["And he spake to them a parable"]{.class_4723} (vs. 29). Jesus is about to give the disciples prophetic truth in the form of a parable. The symbolism is ["the fig tree, and all the trees."]{.class_4723} What are ["the trees"]{.class_4723}? In the Old Testament, they are [Kings and kingdoms]{.class_4725} (Judg. 9:7--15; Dan. 4:20--22; Ezek. 15:6, 17:1--24, 19:10--14, 31:1--18). ["The fig tree"]{.class_4723} was the tree in the Garden of Eden that represented [self-righteousness]{.class_4725} (Gen. 3:7). Jesus cursed the fig tree because it had leaves with no fruit (Matt. 21:19; Mark 11:13--14). So the fig tree is a picture of self-righteousness, religious Israel (Rom. 10:1--3) without any fruit (Jer. 24:2--8), which was cursed by God and ceased to exist as a nation in A.D. 70.

Jesus says when all the nations get together to restore Israel as a nation (["When they now shoot forth,"]{.class_4723} vs. 30 cf. Matt. 24:32), you'll know that ["summer is now nigh at hand"]{.class_4723} (vs. 30); that is, the Lord is about to return to set up His kingdom here on earth (vs. 31). That took place on May 14, 1948 when the United Nations established Israel as a State again in its own land. Jesus said the generation that saw that would still be around when everything from verse 8 to verse 31 takes place (vs. 32).

The question is how long is a ["generation"]{.class_4723}? In Genesis 15:13, it is [100 years;]{.class_4725} in Psalm 90:10, it is [70 years;]{.class_4725} in Matthew 1:17, it works out to about [42 years.]{.class_4725} As of this writing (2012), it is quite obvious that the generation that saw the rebirth of Israel cannot be 42 years, for that would have put the Second Advent at 1990. Seventy years is out because to fit a seven-year Tribulation in before 2018, you would have to have had the Rapture by 2011, and that's come and gone. So what you are left with is the 100-year generation, which would put the Advent no later than 2048.

And then a great verse that every Christian should memorize: []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_604}["Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my WORDS shall not pass away"]{.class_4723} (vs. 33). Jesus didn't say the fundamentals, the principles, the message, or even "the Greek text"; He said ["my WORDS."]{.class_4723} It's those ["WORDS"]{.class_4723} that are ["pure"]{.class_4723} and preserved (Psa. 12:6--7). The promise of preservation was not to a "text" or even a "Bible"; it was to those WORDS. They must be around now where you can get them, or God broke His promise.

The profession in vogue now among many cowardly, lying, hypocritical Fundamentalists and Baptists is: "We believe the [King James]{.class_4746} Bible is the inspired Word of God (or the inspired text), but we don't believe in 'double inspiration' like that heretic Ruckman." Aww, quit your lying, you hypocrite. I never professed to believe in "double inspiration" a day in my life. I have never taught the [AV]{.class_4746} was ["given by inspiration"]{.class_4747} in the same way the originals were: by God breathing His words out of a man so that they were copied down at the time they were spoken (see Jer. 36).

What I have always taught is that the [King James Version]{.class_4725} is the [Scriptures in English]{.class_4725} and that ["ALL scripture is given by inspiration of God"]{.class_4723} (2 Tim. 3:16). The Book I hold in my hand is the WORDS of God ["given by inspiration"]{.class_4723} and preserved without proven error in the universal language of the last days so that I have the WORDS God wants me to have in my own language.

After fifty to sixty years of me kicking these apostates in the seat of the britches on the [King James]{.class_4725} issue, they have finally had to come out and make a profession of belief in that Book, but they have to do it in such a way that they aren't associated with "Ruckman." "I believe the Bible, but I'm not a 'Ruckmanite.'" Yeah, you're a silly old woman; that's what you are.

I have seen one of these birds state in print that if you believed [any]{.class_4727} part of the [King James]{.class_4727} was not inspired that you were [robbing God,]{.class_4727} and then at the very last moment, he had to protect his standing with the apostate brethren by saying: "but I don't believe in the heresy of 'double inspiration' like Ruckman." Aww, your father's moustache. The issue has [never]{.class_4727} been "Ruckman." It has always been [that Book.]{.class_4727} Is the [AV]{.class_4727} the Scriptures or not? Because if it is, then ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration of God."]{.class_4723} Double, triple, or quadruple inspiration has nothing to do with it. If that Book is the Holy Scriptures in English, then it []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_605}["is given by inspiration."]{.class_4723}

34 And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.

35 For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.

36 Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.

37 And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.

38 And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.

The chapter ends with a warning to the disciples in light of everything the Lord just told them about His return. They are first to watch out how they live in the world in preparation for their Lord's return.

Verse 34 hearkens back to the unfaithful servant of 12:45 (which see). The word ["surfeiting"]{.class_4723} is another way of saying [gluttony]{.class_4725} or [overeating.]{.class_4725} The disciple who's not ready for the Lord's return is preoccupied with filling his own gut and taking care of himself.

Notice the ["cares of this life"]{.class_4723} in verse 34; that's one of the things that causes the new believer to become unfruitful toward God (see our comments on 8:14). Modern society is set up to eat up your time with insignificant little things that mean nothing in light of eternity. Everyone is subject to that mess; I am no exception.

When I had a back operation several years ago, the greatest disappointment to me about it was having to think about [myself.]{.class_4725} I have no interest in myself; I have lived long enough with myself to know all my tricks. I thank God for whatever He has given me that I can give to others, but as far as the man himself, [I'm nothing.]{.class_4725} Having to take the pills and make sure you take this one with a meal and that one after a meal and the other with no water, and walk on your toes []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_606}fifty feet when you get out of bed in the morning, and all that stuff. To me, that's all a waste of time, but you have to do it if you are going to stay in any degree of health.

["And so that day come upon you unawares."]{.class_4723} In other words, don't get so caught up in this world that you get "caught with your pants down" when the Lord comes back. Don't let the Lord catch you unprepared; be ready for Him to return RIGHT NOW.

["For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth"]{.class_4723} (vs. 35). Now the immediate context is the Second Advent, but it is certainly true of the Rapture as well. The world won't be any more ready for Christ to catch out His Bride than it will for His return in power. It's a snare, it's a trap to them (Isa. 24:17--18).

The Bible says: ["For man also knoweth not his time: as the fishes that are taken in an evil net, and as the birds that are caught in the snare; so are the sons of men snared in an evil time, when it falleth suddenly upon them"]{.class_4723} (Eccl. 9:12).

The Apostle Paul told the Thessalonians that the return of Jesus Christ comes on the unsaved world ["as a thief in the night"]{.class_4723} (1 Thess. 5:2). They are so caught up with ["Peace and safety"]{.class_4723} that ["sudden destruction cometh upon them\...and they shall not escape"]{.class_4723} (1 Thess. 5:3).

Now while the ["Watch"]{.class_4723} and ["pray"]{.class_4723} part of verse 36 can be applied to the Christian looking for his Saviour to return at the Rapture (2 Thess. 5:6, 17), the rest of the verse certainly [cannot.]{.class_4725} No Christian has to ["pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things\...and to stand before the Son of man." ]{.class_4723}The Rapture of the Christian is [automatic]{.class_4725} because of his [standing in Christ]{.class_4725} (1 Thess. 4:16--17). The Christian is not striving to be worthy to escape the Tribulation because ["God hath not appointed us to wrath"]{.class_4723} because of our faith in Christ's death on the cross (1 Thess. 5:9--10). The thing a Christian is striving to be worthy to receive is a Millennial inheritance in the Kingdom of God when it shows up on earth (2 Thess. 1:5). Verse 36 is addressed to Old Testament saints under the Mosaic Law. In the Tribulation, the Mosaic Law is back in effect as part of salvation (Rev. 12:17, 14:12).

Verse 37: ["And in the day time he was teaching in the []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ3925.xhtml_page_607}temple, and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives."]{.class_4723} Jesus leaves the city at night and goes to the Garden of Gethsemane on the Mount of Olives and sleeps outdoors (cf. 22:39). There is no record of Jesus ever spending the night in Jerusalem. He would either go to Bethany, to the home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus (Matt. 21:17 cf. John 12:1), or He would go to the Garden of Gethsemane. He doesn't stay in Jerusalem until He enters as King at the Second Advent, and the city is called ["Jehovah-shammah":]{.class_4725} ["The LORD is there"]{.class_4723} (Ezek. 48:35).

Verse 38 is self-explanatory. Note the old usage of ["for"]{.class_4723} in the verse: ["for to hear him."]{.class_4723} The old Negro spiritual goes: "Swing low, sweet chariot, coming FOR TO CARRY ME HOME." It means "for the purpose of," "in order to." The old, archaic, Elizabethan English isn't as hard as it looks, unless you've fried your brain on TV, rock music, drugs, liquor, and the American public school system. You might have to work a little harder to get it if that's the case.

[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml}

# Chapter 22 {.heading_78-0}

22:1 Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.

2 And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.

3 Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.

4 And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.

5 And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.

6 And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.

Note that ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723} in verse 1 is defined as ["the DAY of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723} in verse 7. This means that ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723} in verse 1 is [not]{.class_4725} the seven-day feast that starts the day after Passover (Lev. 23:5--6). The Passover is called ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723} here because the Passover lamb was to be eaten with unleavened bread (Exod. 12:8, 18; Num. 9:11; Deut. 16:2--3).

The reason this is so important is because all the critics of the [AV]{.class_4725} say that the word ["Easter"]{.class_4723} in Acts 12:4 is a [mistake;]{.class_4725} the proper translation should be ["Passover"]{.class_4725} (once again the [NKJV]{.class_4725} reads with the [NASV]{.class_4725} and [NIV]{.class_4725}). But Acts 12:4 couldn't possibly be a reference to the Passover, for ["Then were the DAYS]{.class_4723} \[plural\][ of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723} (Acts 12:3). ["The DAY]{.class_4723} \[singular\][ of unleavened bread"]{.class_4723}---the Passover (Luke 22:1, 7)---was over and done with (Lev. 23:5--6). ["Easter"]{.class_4723} in Acts 12:4 is obviously a pagan feast---celebrated by a pagan, Roman Idumean (Edomite) who worshipped ["Ashtaroth"]{.class_4723} (Judg. 2:13, 10:6; 1 Sam. 7:3; 1 Kings 11:5; 2 Kings 23:13)---that coincided with the seven-day Feast of Unleavened Bread (Acts 12:3)---NOT Passover.

For verse 2, see our comments on 19:47--48 and 20:19. Verse 3 is one of [two]{.class_4725} times Satan enters into Judas Iscariot. He enters into []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_609}him here to move him to covenant with the chief priests to betray Christ, and He enters into him again in John 13:27 at the Last Supper to carry out the betrayal.

With the number of devils Satan has at his command, he normally doesn't have to fool with most people individually, but there are several people in the Scriptures in whom he takes a personal interest. He deals with Eve personally to get to her husband (2 Cor. 11:3). He works on Job personally (Job 1--2). He ["provoked David to number Israel"]{.class_4723} (1 Chron. 21:1). He resists ["Joshua the high priest"]{.class_4723} in Zechariah 3:1. He tempts Christ there in Luke 4. Here in 22:31, he tries to sift Simon Peter like wheat. In Acts 5:3, he fills the heart of Ananias ["to lie to the Holy Ghost."]{.class_4723}

That last reference is important, because it shows you that a Christian can be filled with [Satan]{.class_4725} just like he can be filled with the Holy Spirit. The Charismatics quote 1 John 4:4 (["greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world"]{.class_4723}) as if that exempts a Christian from Satanic control. In 1 John 4:4, the Apostle John is merely stating a fact. The believer has overcome the world [positionally]{.class_4725} because he has the Holy Spirit living in Him; [practically]{.class_4725} is something else. A Christian can love the world (1 John 2:15) and become the enemy of God (James 4:4). He can yield the members of his body to unrighteousness (Rom. 6:13) and become a servant of sin (Rom. 6:16). A Christian can turn ["aside after Satan"]{.class_4723} (1 Tim. 5:15) and be delivered ["unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 5:5). A Christian can give control over his body and mind to Satan despite the indwelling presence of the Holy Spirit.

Now that is not the case with Judas. Judas was not a believer who lost his salvation, nor was he a believer who yielded himself to Satan. Jesus said Judas was ["a devil"]{.class_4723} (John 6:70). Do you reckon he was a [saved]{.class_4725} devil?

This is what the Bible calls ["the mystery of iniquity"]{.class_4723} (2 Thess. 2:7): it is [the Devil manifest in the flesh.]{.class_4725} Christ calls Judas ["the son of perdition" ]{.class_4723}(John 17:12) because before his incarnation, this devil was the King of the Bottomless Pit (Rev. 9:11). His name is ["Abaddon"]{.class_4723} in Hebrew and ["Apollyon"]{.class_4723} in Greek. It means "Destroyer" and is translated ["perdition"]{.class_4723} in John 17:12 and 2 Thessalonians 2:3. So when Judas dies, he doesn't go to Hell; he goes ["to his own place"]{.class_4723} (Acts 1:25): the Bottomless Pit, [perdition]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_610} (Rev. 17:8, 11). The only difference between the Son of Perdition at his first coming (John 17:12) and the Son of Perdition at his second coming (2 Thess. 2:3) is that the second time Judas shows up (the Beast ["was, and is not"]{.class_4723} when John wrote Rev. 17:8 and would someday ["ascend out of the bottomless pit"]{.class_4723}), he will not just be the incarnation of ["a devil"]{.class_4723} (John 6:70), but [Satan himself]{.class_4725} (2 Thess. 2:8--9).

Back to Luke 22: ["and covenanted to give him money"]{.class_4723} (vs. 5). The deal Judas strikes with the chief priests is to betray Christ ["for thirty pieces of silver"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 26:15). This wouldn't be the Roman penny (see our remarks under 20:24); this would be the Temple shekel (see Exod. 30:13). Judas betrayed the Lord for the price of a [slave]{.class_4725} (Exod. 21:32) and the value of a [woman]{.class_4725} for a vow (Lev. 27:4). In so doing, he fulfilled Zechariah 11:13, written 400 years before He was born.

In that passage, the price of Christ's betrayal was called ["a goodly price."]{.class_4723} That's [God]{.class_4725} talking. The Lord says, "['Thirty pieces of silver']{.class_4723} is a pretty good price for me." That's the Lord being sarcastic again (see our remarks under 18:25). That ["goodly price"]{.class_4723} at one time was worth about seventeen dollars in American money. Of course, I'm sure that with inflation it would be worth more now, but isn't that something over which to lose your soul? Suppose it was worth \$1,700.00. Would you want to go to Hell over that amount? But that's what Judas did, and he did it with Zechariah 11:13 sitting right there where he could read his fate. Sinner friend, if you wind up in Hell, you will get there with a Bible telling you exactly what would happen to you if you rejected Christ, and you either refused to [read it]{.class_4725} or [believe it.]{.class_4725} You will have [no excuse,]{.class_4725} especially in America where you can get ahold of a Bible anytime you want.

Verse 6 is self-explanatory. The priests want to arrest and try Christ clandestinely because ["they feared the people"]{.class_4723} (vs. 2), and of course, that's what happens.

7 Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed.

8 And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_611}the passover, that we may eat.

9 And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?

10 And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.

11 And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?

12 And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.

13 And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.

We have already discussed verse 7 under verse 1 (which see). This would be [Tuesday evening.]{.class_4725} The preparation for the Passover started on Tuesday at 6:00 p.m. and ran to Wednesday at 6:00 p.m.

Notice in verses 10--12 how commonplace prophecy is to the Lord Jesus Christ. Jesus thinks nothing of prophesying over a little matter like finding a place to eat the Passover supper. Any saint in the Bible is more of a prophet than Mohammed (Rev. 19:10). You will search in vain in the [Koran]{.class_4725} or the [Hadith]{.class_4725} for Mohammed doing anything like what Jesus Christ did here. If Mohammed is Allah's prophet, then fire the bum, because he never fulfilled his office a day in his life. Mohammed couldn't even do what Samuel did in 1 Samuel 10:1--9, and Samuel wasn't even counted as a "Major" or "Minor" Prophet (Isaiah--Daniel; Hosea--Malachi). Mohammed couldn't even prophesy like [Saul]{.class_4725} (1 Sam. 10:10--11), and Saul was a demon-possessed madman (1 Sam. 19:24).

14 And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.

15 And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:

16 For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.

17 And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_612}this, and divide it among yourselves:

18 For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.

19 And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.

20 Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.

["With desire I have desired"]{.class_4723} (vs. 15). This repetition of thought is a common construction in Hebrew grammar (see our remarks on the subject under Hab. 1:9 in that Commentary). The ["desire"]{.class_4723} here is a [strong]{.class_4725} desire. If you desire something strongly enough, the expression is you have a [passion]{.class_4725} for it. So the final week of Christ's life is called "Passion Week." Notice that the ["desire"]{.class_4723} in verse 15 is connected with Christ's suffering (["before I suffer"]{.class_4723}), so when we speak of the "passion of Christ" (see Acts 1:3), we are referring to His [suffering.]{.class_4725}

["For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God"]{.class_4723} (vs. 16). What is the ["it"]{.class_4723}? It can either be the Passover or His suffering, but either way it involves the same thing. Look at 1 Corinthians 5:7.

"For even Christ our passover is sacrificed for us."

When Christ suffers and dies on the cross, He's fulfilling His role as the Passover Lamb (John 1:29). So Jesus is saying that He won't eat anything else until that has been accomplished. The next time Jesus eats with the disciples, it is [after]{.class_4725} the Resurrection (24:30, 41--43). That is one of the ["infallible proofs"]{.class_4723} of His [bodily]{.class_4725} resurrection (Acts 1:3).

Now although the Kingdom of God has nothing to do with what you eat or drink (Rom. 14:17), what happens at Calvary is the formation of a spiritual Body into which both Jewish and Gentile believers are placed at the New Birth (Eph. 2:5, 16). When Jesus Christ comes up from the dead, he is no longer merely a man ["after the flesh"]{.class_4723} (2 Cor. 5:16); He is a spiritual being in a glorified body. That body could make a trip up from the heart of the earth to the third heaven and return to earth in less than twelve hours. It could []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_613}walk through shut doors with no problem. No man could do that [physically;]{.class_4725} the glorified body of the Lord Jesus Christ was a [supernatural, spiritual]{.class_4725} body.

Yet at the same time that body had ["flesh and bones"]{.class_4747} (Luke 24:39) that could be [touched]{.class_4746} (Matt. 28:9; John 20:27) and could eat (Luke 24:41--43). So when the King is there in His supernatural body, both the physical Kingdom of Heaven and the spiritual Kingdom of God are present.

Verses 17--20 are what the Roman Catholics call "the Mass," which is a misnomer. The word [mass]{.class_4725} is a Latin word meaning "dismissal." According to Noah Webster (1828), "the word signifies primarily [leisure, cessation from labor]{.class_4725}\...hence a [feast]{.class_4725} or [holiday."]{.class_4725} So Catholics talk about "celebrating the Mass."

Why would you "celebrate" the bloody suffering and death of a man?

Suppose your son was executed for a crime he didn't commit. Would you take the instrument of his demise and display it prominently in your house and have a party once a week to celebrate his passing? Then why would you "celebrate" the death of your Saviour like that?

When the Apostle Paul talks about glorying ["in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ"]{.class_4723} (Gal. 6:14), he's not glorying over the whip, the wood, the nails, the crown of thorns, and the spear; he is glorying over what Jesus accomplished on the cross---[the finished atonement.]{.class_4725} No Catholic believes the atonement is completed; that's why he sacrifices Christ ["afresh"]{.class_4723} every Sunday (Heb. 6:6).

In the New Testament, the believers "celebrate" the [Resurrection]{.class_4725} of the Lord Jesus Christ by gathering for worship and fellowship on the first day of the week (see our comments on 1 Cor. 16:2 in that Commentary). In the Pauline Epistles, the Lord's Supper is a [memorial]{.class_4725}, not a "celebration" (1 Cor. 11:24--25). It is a [demonstration]{.class_4725} of His death (1 Cor. 11:26), not a "celebration."

The Mass is the [heart]{.class_4725} of the Catholic Church. If you eliminate the Mass, you eliminate the whole body of Catholic dogma and practice because the center of the religion is the [Mass.]{.class_4725} In Roman Catholicism, the Mass is a [sacrifice]{.class_4725} of which a communicant must partake in order to be saved.

Do you know what the Apostle to the Romans said about such heretical blasphemy? He said that [Jesus Christ]{.class_4725} made the [one]{.class_4725} effectual sacrifice for sins (Heb. 10:12), and any other sacrifice made by a Gentile priest was a ["sacrifice to devils"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 10:20).

The Lord's Supper is not the Mass, and the Mass is not the Lord's Supper. One is ["the cup of the Lord" ]{.class_4723}and[ "the Lord's table,"]{.class_4723} and the other is ["the cup of devils"]{.class_4723} and ["the table of devils"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 10:21). ["The cup of devils"]{.class_4723} is fermented hootch; ["the cup of the Lord"]{.class_4723} is ["the fruit of the vine"]{.class_4723} (Luke 22:18). That's ["new wine"]{.class_4723} (Isa. 65:8) that is called ["the pure blood of the grape"]{.class_4723} (Deut. 32:14). It is obtained by squeezing the juice from a cluster of grapes directly into a cup (Gen. 40:11). It is to be distinguished from the wine of the Roman Catholic Mass, which is said to be ["the poison of dragons, and the cruel venom of asps"]{.class_4723} (Deut. 32:33) because it is intoxicating liquor (Prov. 23:31--32).

The cup and table of devils are supposed to be the [literal]{.class_4725} body and blood of Jesus Christ. A Baalite priest (see Judg. 17--18) transforms bread and wine by black magic into flesh and blood so he can practice [cannibalism]{.class_4725} and [vampirism.]{.class_4725} Sure, man, that's what it is; there's no need to be squeamish about it. If that bread is the literal body of Jesus Christ, then you're a [cannibal]{.class_4725} if you eat it; and if that fermented liquor is Christ's literal blood, that's [vampirism]{.class_4725} if you drink it.

Moreover, if you drink Christ's literal blood in the Mass, you are breaking the commandment of God under [three]{.class_4725} testaments: [before]{.class_4725} the Law (Gen. 9:4), [under]{.class_4725} the Law (Lev. 17:10), and [after]{.class_4725} the Law (Acts 15:20, 29). David said, ["their DRINK OFFERINGS OF BLOOD will I not offer"]{.class_4723} (Psa. 16:4).

In the Lord's Supper, the elements are [figurative]{.class_4725} and [symbolic;]{.class_4725} they're not literal. Look at verse 17 here in Luke 22: ["And he took the cup\...and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves."]{.class_4723} Do you reckon each of the disciples broke a piece off that cup as it was passed around? You say, "Don't be silly, Ruckman; He's talking about what's in the cup." Exactly! ["The cup"]{.class_4723} is a metaphorical term for the [contents]{.class_4725} of the cup.

When Jesus says ["This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you"]{.class_4723} (vs. 20), He can't possibly be speaking literally, for [no blood has been shed yet.]{.class_4725} []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_615}["The fruit of the vine"]{.class_4723} [represents]{.class_4725} the blood He is going to shed for them on Calvary; He didn't cut His wrist and bleed into the cup and make them drink it.

When Jesus says ["This is my body which is given for you"]{.class_4723} (vs. 19), He's not saying the bread is His literal body because His literal body is holding the bread. In this age, Christ's [literal]{.class_4725} body is up in [Heaven]{.class_4725} at ["the right hand of God"]{.class_4723} the Father (Mark 16:19). His [spiritual]{.class_4725} body is made up of [every saved person]{.class_4725} who has received Him as Saviour (2 Cor. 12:12--27), so Paul says this:

["The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the COMMUNION of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the COMMUNION of the body of Christ? For WE being many are ONE BREAD, and ONE BODY: for we are all partakers of that one bread"]{.class_4723} (1 Cor. 10:16--17).

So according to the apostle who actually wrote to the Romans, the bread and new wine of the Lord's Supper [aren't]{.class_4725} Christ's body and blood at all. They're the [communion of the saints]{.class_4725} who have trusted in the sacrifice of Christ's body on the cross and His shed blood for salvation. The Lord Jesus Christ Himself said that if you [could]{.class_4725} eat His literal flesh and drink His literal blood, it would do you no good at all (John 6:63). In John 6, He told you [three times]{.class_4725} that eating His flesh and drinking His blood was a metaphorical statement for [believing on Him]{.class_4725} (John 6:35, 40, 47). It's those ["WORDS of eternal life"]{.class_4723} that save you, not eating Christ's flesh and blood (John 6:63, 68).

There is nothing more ironic than a Roman Catholic telling you that the bread and wine in the Mass are Christ's literal flesh and blood and that you have to eat Christ's literal flesh and blood to be saved, and then telling you that no man can know for sure he is saved till after he dies. Well, why don't you? After all, Jesus said, ["Whoso eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood, HATH ETERNAL LIFE"]{.class_4723} (John 6:54). You say you're eating Christ's literal flesh and drinking Christ's literal blood in the Mass? Then do you have eternal life or not?

Mother Teresa couldn't tell you where she was going when she died. You never heard John Paul II give a testimony where he was going when he died. Why couldn't they just point to John 6:54 and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_616}say, "There---I ate Christ's body and drank His blood in the Mass, and Jesus said I have eternal life if I do that"?

That's not all. What is this business of taking the Mass every week? If you get eternal life by eating and drinking Christ's flesh and blood, why would you need to do it more than once? Did you get eternal life at the Mass and then lose it during the week, so you have to eat Christ again the next Sunday? Jesus said, ["I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall NEVER hunger, and he that believeth on me shall NEVER thirst"]{.class_4723} (John 6:35).

When Jesus explained what He meant by eating His flesh and drinking His blood, not only did He say it meant believing on Him (see above), He likened it to how He lived by His Father (John 6:57). Do you reckon that Jesus ate God the Father's flesh and blood? Are you some sort of psychotic nut? ["God is a Spirit"]{.class_4723} (John 4:24); He doesn't have flesh and blood. Jesus was in His Father just like you are in Jesus Christ (John 6:56, 17:23). That happens when you trust Christ as your Saviour, and the Holy Spirit puts you in Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 12:13). Eating and drinking anything [literally]{.class_4725} has nothing to do with it at all.

But the main thing wrong with the Roman Catholic Mass is that no Pope knows what it is for. The Lord's Supper is to ["shew the Lord's death TILL HE COME"]{.class_4736} (1 Cor. 11:26). You never heard any Pope talk about the Second Coming of Christ a day in your life. If a Pope got twenty million dollars for every sermon he preached on the Second Coming of Christ, he wouldn't have enough money to buy a bus ticket from Pensacola to Mobile.

I brought this up to the Catholic lawyer Karl Keating when I debated him years ago out in California. He said, "Every Catholic is taught about the Second Coming, and we always talk about it in the Mass." Unfortunately for him, down on the front row were about twenty saved Mexicans who had come out of the Roman Catholic Church. When he said that, they all shook their heads [no.]{.class_4725} I thought to myself, "Your sheep are bleating on you, Saul" (see 1 Sam. 15:14). Those Mexicans went to Mass week after week, year after year, and never heard the Priest let out a peep about the Second Coming during the Mass. That's not abnormal in a Catholic church; it's [typical.]{.class_4725}

21 But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.

22 And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!

23 And they began to enquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.

Now look at verse 21. There's Judas right there at the table taking the "Mass." If he's eating Christ's flesh and drinking His blood, then how come he's not saved (John 6:54)? Judas partakes of the elements and dies [lost.]{.class_4725}

For verse 22, see our comments on 17:2.

["And they began to enquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing"]{.class_4723} (vs. 23). Now ten of the disciples ask[ "Lord, is it I?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 26:22). John doesn't say that, and Judas doesn't say that. John asks Jesus, ["Lord, who is it?"]{.class_4723} (John 13:25). John has a clear conscience in the matter and knows he's not the traitor. Judas, on the other hand, does not address Jesus as ["Lord"]{.class_4723}; he says, ["MASTER, is it I?"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 26:25). Judas was the only disciple to consistently call Jesus ["Master"]{.class_4723} instead of ["Lord"]{.class_4723} (see Matt. 26:25, 49; Mark 14:45). As a devil (John 6:70), Judas could not make the confession of 1 Corinthians 12:3 (NOT Phil. 2:10--11) because ["no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, BUT BY THE HOLY GHOST."]{.class_4723}

Now, the answer Jesus gives to the disciples' inquiry in Matthew 26:23 is not given to the disciples as a group. It is given privately to [John]{.class_4725} (John 13:26). In an interesting side note, Jim Lince, who is now home with the Lord, was teaching at his Bible Institute once on Judas being the Antichrist (John 17:12 cf. 2 Thess. 2:3). One of those North Carolina ridge-runners in his class raised his hand and stuttered, "P-p-preacher, you know Judas is the Antichrist because Jesus gave him ['the sop.']{.class_4723}" Bro. Lince gave the kid a blank look and said, "What do you mean?" "Well," said that hillbilly, "Jesus gave Judas ['the sop.']{.class_4723} S-O-P: Son Of Perdition." Evidently, the Holy Ghost can show things to a farm boy from North Carolina in a [King James]{.class_4725} Bible that He won't show to a professor in a Christian College or []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_618}Seminary fiddling with "the original Greek."

Jesus dips the sop and gives it to Judas (John 13:26). Judas asks Jesus, ["Master, is it I?"]{.class_4723} Jesus tells him, ["Thou hast said"]{.class_4723} (Matt. 26:25)---"You said it." As soon as Judas accepts the sop, Satan enters Judas for the second time (see our comments on vs. 3). At that point, Jesus tells Judas, ["That thou doest, do quickly"]{.class_4723} (John 13:27), and Judas leaves to betray the Lord (John 13:30).

24 And there was also a strife among them, which of them should be accounted the greatest.

25 And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.

26 But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.

27 For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.

The passage here should be read in conjunction with John 13:14--16. The disciples are having an argument (again!---see Mark 9:34) on who would be the greatest in the kingdom. Jesus gives them an "object lesson" to show them that to be great in the kingdom, they had to be [servants.]{.class_4725} He rises from supper and does what none of them would do before the supper began: He washes everyone's dirty feet. He was the ["greater\...that sitteth at meat,"]{.class_4723} but He became the one ["that serveth"]{.class_4723} (vs. 27). The Bible says Christ ["made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant"]{.class_4723} (Phil. 2:7).

When He comes back, He'll serve those who serve Him now (see our comments on 12:37). That's the example set by your Lord for you to follow, and it goes with both Advents.

["The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors]{.class_4723} \[bene---good; factors---workers; so a "benefactor" here is a [do-gooder]{.class_4725}\][. But ye shall not be so"]{.class_4723} (vss. 25--26). That shows you that the Roman Catholic Church is not a [Christian]{.class_4725}[]{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_619} church. The "first Pope" (Simon Peter according to all the Popes from Leo I onward) instructed elders NOT to be ["lords over God's heritage"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 5:3). The Pope (any of them) sits on a [throne]{.class_4725} and wears a [triple crown]{.class_4725} as the [head of a Gentile State.]{.class_4725} That's exactly what Jesus told the disciples NOT to do.

In the New Testament local church, each congregation is taught by elders (1 Pet. 5:1). They are to lead and teach the congregation spiritually as a shepherd would feed his flock (1 Pet. 5:2), so they are called [pastors]{.class_4725} (Eph. 4:11). The presiding elder of a congregation is called a bishop (1 Tim. 3:1) because he oversees the ministry of the local church (Acts 20:28). As an overseer, he is not a taskmaster but a [servant.]{.class_4725} He feeds and protects God's flock and supports the weak (Acts 20:28--35). When it comes to Bible-believing Christianity, the Bible knows nothing of State churches or Church-States.

28 Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.

29 And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;

30 That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

Now, Jesus is at the table with all the apostles except Judas (see our remarks under vs. 23), so when He says ["Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations"]{.class_4723} (vs. 28), that's to whom He's referring. Judas had just left to betray Him.

When Jesus gave the discourse on the Bread of Life in John 6, a bunch of disciples left Him because they didn't understand about what He was talking when He said they had to eat His flesh and drink His blood to get eternal life (John 6:66). When that happened, Jesus turned to the twelve and asked, ["Will ye also go away?"]{.class_4723} (John 6:67). As usual, Peter spoke up for everybody and said, ["Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life"]{.class_4723} (John 6:68). In other words, "We have nowhere else to go, Lord. Nobody else has ever been able to tell us how to get eternal life, so if it's all []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_620}the same to you, we'll just stick around."

And they did. They stuck by Jesus when they thought the Jews were going to kill Him (John 11:16). Of course, that's going to change in just a couple of hours: they're all going to forsake Him in the Garden of Gethsemane when the soldiers come to take Him to the high priest (Matt. 26:31). But the Lord doesn't hold that against them (Matt. 26:32), and here He promises them a reward in His kingdom for their loyalty.

The kingdom here is the physical Kingdom of Heaven (cf. Rev. 11:15), as is clear from the latter half of verse 30 (cf. Matt. 19:28), but in the context, the kingdom has been the Kingdom of God. As we have said on several occasions now, when Christ comes back at the Second Advent, He comes as the King of [both]{.class_4725} kingdoms. That is how one can ["eat and drink at my table in my kingdom"]{.class_4723} (vs. 30) in a spiritual kingdom (see our remarks under vs. 18 and 14:15).

31 And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat:

32 But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.

33 And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.

34 And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.

This is Jesus warning Peter that he is going to deny the Lord later on that night. He starts out saying, ["Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat"]{.class_4723} (vs. 31). Modern Americans aren't familiar with the process of sifting. Back when I was growing up, a housewife had a flour sifter. It looked like a tin can with a hand crank on the outside. Inside at the bottom was a concave fine wire mesh. Connected to the hand crank on the inside was a piece of wire made to fit the mesh at the bottom as it was turned. You would put a little flour inside that thing and turn the hand crank. That piece of wire would force the flour through the wire mesh into a bowl you put underneath it, and anything that wasn't a fine []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_621}consistency like that flour would be left in the sifter. That's how you got boll weevils (a small bug) out of your flour.

Jesus is saying that Satan is going to put Peter through the sifter to get rid of Peter's love for Christ. Of course that works both ways. Peter is in a situation like Job where God and the Devil are working on him at the same time. While Satan is trying to remove Peter's love and loyalty to Christ, the Lord is working on Peter to get rid of anything that stands in the way of those things.

When the Lord allows Satan to put you to the test, the goal is to remove the impurities from your life. Job said, ["When he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold"]{.class_4723} (Job 23:10). That's the Lord melting you down in the refiner's pot so the dross will come to the top and be removed. What's left is pure gold.

Having gone through the fire of Satan's testing, Peter tells those to whom he writes: ["Beloved, think it not strange concerning the fiery trial which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened unto you: But rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ's sufferings; that, when his glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy"]{.class_4723} (1 Pet. 4:12--13). You are going to be tried; there's no doubt about that. But you can rejoice that you are going through the trial for Christ's sake, and you can rejoice that if you make it through successfully, the Lord is going to reward you at the Judgment Seat of Christ (2 Thess. 4:7--8; James 1:12).

["But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not"]{.class_4723} (vs. 32). Now isn't that something? That's your Saviour and Lord praying that you will make it through the trial successfully. That's Jesus Christ up in Heaven interceding to God the Father for you (Heb. 7:25; Rom. 8:26--27). In His great High Priestly prayer, Jesus said, ["I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine\...Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word"]{.class_4723} (John 17:9, 20).

Now thank God for the prayers of God's people. I attribute what health I've still got to the grace of God and the prayers of Bible-believing Christians; that's how much I think of intercessory prayer. I probably have a couple of thousand people praying for me, and []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_622}thank God they do. I could use the prayers of 10,000 more, and that would suit me just fine. But I've got someone praying for me who has never had a prayer turned down. If you can't get your prayer answered, [He can.]{.class_4725} That's the One praying for [you]{.class_4725} if you are saved.

["And when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren. "When thou art converted"]{.class_4723}?! Wasn't he converted back in Matthew 16:16--17 when he confessed Jesus was the Christ? Yes, he was, but in Luke 22, [before Calvary,]{.class_4725} the disciples are still in an Old Testament setting.

In the Old Testament, a believer could commit a sin that made him lose the Holy Spirit (Psa. 51:11). That's not your case as a New Testament Christian (Eph. 4:30). As an Old Testament saint under the Law at this point, Peter was [not born again,]{.class_4725} he was [not in the Body of Christ,]{.class_4725} he was [not spiritually circumcised,]{.class_4725} and he was [not an adopted child of God.]{.class_4725} "Conversion" in the Old Testament is not the same as conversion in the New Testament; conversion in the New Testament is [permanent ]{.class_4725}(Rom. 8:38--39; Phil. 1:6). In the New Testament, upon your conversion to Christ, your destination is [fixed]{.class_4725} (Rom. 8:29).

Now Peter does not lose his salvation. Jesus prays that his faith would not fail, and the Lord states in His prayer in John 17 that He had not lost any of the disciples except Judas (John 17:12). But Peter does lose something, and it is [his discipleship.]{.class_4725} Look at Mark 16:7---["But go your way, tell his disciples AND PETER."]{.class_4723} Peter had denied his Lord and lost his discipleship and had to have it restored in John 21.

Peter denied Christ three times, so in John 21, he has to confess Christ three times. All the scholars, expositors, and commentators who become fixated on Christ's use of [agapao]{.class_4725} and [phileo]{.class_4725} in that chapter [miss the point]{.class_4725} of the passage completely. And each time Peter confesses Christ, the Lord tells him, ["Feed my lambs"]{.class_4723} (John 21:15); ["Feed my sheep"]{.class_4723} (John 21:16--17). That's how Peter was to ["strengthen thy brethren"]{.class_4723} (vs. 32). That's what Peter commanded the elders to whom he wrote (1 Pet. 5:1--2).

Peter's problem wasn't a lack of love for Christ. He never lost his faith, not even as he was denying the Lord. The problem was that he got mad at Jesus. He wanted to fight, and the Lord wouldn't let him []{#aanhLZRRSx2riOtByQm3wQ4052.xhtml_page_623}fight (John 18:11). Christian, one of the most dangerous situations into which you will ever get is when you become mad at God because you thought He should have handled a matter a certain way, and He didn't do what you thought He should have done. You get upset with the Lord and figure you'll just show Him exactly how you feel. You have to watch your attitude in a situation like that.

35 And he said unto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.

36 Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.

37 For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.

38 And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.

If you want to understand why Peter is so frustrated with Jesus in John 18:11, then look at verse 36: ["and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one."]{.class_4723} The Lord told them to buy a sword if they didn't own one, and they say, ["Lord, behold, here are two swords"]{.class_4723} (vs. 38). Peter owned one of those swords (John 18:10). He figured he was doing what the Lord told him to do because when he showed Jesus his sword back in the upper room, Jesus told him, ["It is enough"]{.class_4723} (vs. 38).

Peter is ready to fight to the death for Jesus, and Jesus tells him, ["Put up thy sword into the sheath"]{.class_4723} (John 18:11). "I could call for twelve legions of angels to protect me, Peter (Matt. 26:53); I don't need your help. Put the sword away." Peter stands there aghast at what the Lord just told him. He can't fight, and the soldiers are now coming to take him, so he runs. He runs away, and he hates himself for it. He hates himself for running, and he hates the Lord for putting him in that position. That's why he cusses there at the fire. The Devil took Peter's love for Christ and twisted it into bitterness; that's how subtle he is (see our remarks under 4:5--6).

Now what Jesus is doing is revising the instructions He gave the disciples back in 9:2. The reason is that He is going to the cross (vs. 37) and, eventually, back to the Father (John 14:28). After He is gone, the apostles will be going to more than just ["the lost sheep of the house of Israel."]{.class_4723} They will be going outside of the land of Israel to the Gentiles; the Gentiles weren't going to provide for Jewish apostles like Jews looking for their Messiah. They would need money, clothing, and a weapon to protect themselves along the way against robbers and bandits. The sword was for self-defense, not for overthrowing ["the powers that be."]{.class_4723}

Verse 37 is a quote from Isaiah 53:12. The ["end"]{.class_4723} of verse 37 is a reference to the fulfillment of the Old Testament prophecies on the First Coming of Christ.

39 And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.

40 And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.

41 And he was withdrawn from them about a stone's cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,

42 Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.

43 And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.

44 And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.

45 And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,

46 And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.

The word ["wont"]{.class_4723} in verse 39 is an archaic word meaning "accustomed."
